title: Classic Cottage Pie
steps: Peel and chop the potatoes into equal-sized chunks, then boil them in salted water for 15-20 minutes until very tender.
While potatoes boil, finely chop the onion and carrots.
Heat oil in a large oven-safe saucepan or casserole dish over medium heat.
Add the beef mince and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat.
Stir in the chopped onion and carrots, cooking for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Sprinkle plain flour over the mince and vegetables, stir well and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes and beef stock cube dissolved in 200ml hot water. Bring to a simmer.
Stir in the frozen peas. Season with salt and pepper. Cook gently for 10-15 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken.
Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Drain the cooked potatoes thoroughly. Mash them with the milk, butter/margarine, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Spoon the mashed potato evenly over the mince filling, roughing up the top with a fork for a crispy finish.
If using, sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the mashed potato.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.

title: Sausage and Mash with Onion Gravy
steps: Peel and chop the potatoes into chunks. Boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until very tender.
While potatoes cook, preheat your grill to medium-high or oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Cook the sausages under the grill or in the oven for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through.
For the gravy, thinly slice the onions. Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions to the pan and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply softened and caramelised.
Sprinkle the plain flour over the softened onions and stir well, cooking for 1 minute.
Dissolve the beef stock cube in 400ml hot water. Gradually pour the stock into the pan with the onions, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the gravy to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens to your desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper.
Once potatoes are cooked, drain thoroughly. Mash with milk, butter/margarine, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy.
Serve the hot sausages on a bed of mashed potato, generously drizzled with the rich onion gravy.

title: Toad in the Hole
steps: Preheat your oven to 220°C (200°C fan/Gas Mark 7).
In a jug, whisk together the plain flour, eggs, milk, and a pinch of salt until you have a smooth batter.
Place a large oven-safe dish (approx. 25x30cm) or a muffin tin (for individual portions) into the preheated oven with the vegetable oil.
Heat the oil in the dish for 5-10 minutes until it is smoking hot. This is crucial for a good rise.
Carefully remove the hot dish from the oven. Add the sausages to the hot oil.
Pour the prepared batter evenly over and around the sausages in the hot dish.
Immediately return the dish to the oven and cook for 20-25 minutes without opening the oven door, until the batter is golden brown and well risen.
Serve immediately with vegetables and gravy (gravy recipe from sausage and mash can be used).

title: Hearty Beef Stew with Dumplings
steps: Cut the beef into 2-3cm chunks. Season well with salt and pepper. Toss in 1 tablespoon of flour.
Heat oil in a large oven-safe casserole dish or heavy-bottomed pan over high heat. Brown the beef in batches, then set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Chop onion, carrots, and celery. Add to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Return the beef to the pan. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of flour and mixed herbs, cooking for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and beef stock cubes dissolved in 600ml hot water. Bring to a simmer.
Cover the dish and cook in a preheated oven at 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3) for 1.5-2 hours, or until the beef is very tender.
While the stew cooks, prepare the dumpling mix: combine 100g plain flour, 50g vegetable suet, and a pinch of salt in a bowl.
Gradually add 3-4 tablespoons of cold water, mixing until a soft, pliable dough forms. Roll into 8 small balls.
After the stew has cooked for 1.5 hours, add the chopped potatoes to the stew. Place the dumpling balls on top of the stew.
Recover the dish and return to the oven for another 25-30 minutes, or until the dumplings are puffed and cooked through.
Serve hot, ensuring beef is fork-tender and vegetables are soft.

title: Creamy Fish Pie
steps: Peel and chop potatoes into chunks. Boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until very tender.
While potatoes boil, gently poach the fish: place fish in a shallow pan, add 300ml of the milk and the bay leaf.
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 5-7 minutes until the fish is opaque and cooked through.
Carefully remove the fish and bay leaf. Flake the fish into large pieces, checking for any bones.
Melt 20g of butter/margarine in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux.
Gradually whisk in the reserved milk from poaching the fish (discarding bay leaf) and the remaining 200ml fresh milk.
Bring the sauce to a simmer, stirring constantly, until it thickens. Season with salt and pepper.
Stir the flaked fish and frozen peas into the white sauce. Pour the mixture into an oven-safe pie dish.
Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
Drain the cooked potatoes thoroughly. Mash with the remaining 20g butter/margarine, a splash of milk (if needed), salt, and pepper until smooth.
Spoon the mashed potato over the fish mixture, spreading it evenly. Roughen the top with a fork.
If using, sprinkle grated cheddar cheese over the mash.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, until golden brown and bubbling around the edges.

title: Creamy Chicken and Leek Pie
steps: Dice chicken breasts into 1-2cm pieces. Trim, wash, and thinly slice the leeks.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the chicken and cook until lightly browned and cooked through, then remove and set aside.
Add the sliced leeks to the same pan, adding a little more oil if needed. Cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Melt butter/margarine with the leeks. Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute.
Dissolve the chicken stock cube in 200ml hot water. Gradually whisk the stock and milk into the pan with the leeks, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until thickened. Return the cooked chicken to the pan.
Season the filling with salt and pepper. Pour the chicken and leek mixture into an oven-safe pie dish.
Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Unroll the puff pastry and place it over the pie dish, trimming any excess and crimping the edges to seal.
If using, beat the egg and brush over the pastry top for a golden glaze. Make a small slit in the centre of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed, and the filling is bubbling.

title: Cheesy Vegetable Crumble
steps: Chop broccoli and cauliflower into small florets, and carrots into small chunks. Boil or steam all vegetables (except peas) until tender-crisp, about 5-7 minutes. Add frozen peas for the last 2 minutes.
Drain vegetables thoroughly and place them in an oven-safe dish.
Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
For the cheese sauce: Melt 30g of butter/margarine in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in 30g plain flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the milk and the dissolved vegetable stock cube (in 100ml hot water). Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Remove from heat and stir in half of the grated cheddar cheese until melted. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour the cheese sauce over the cooked vegetables in the dish, ensuring they are well coated.
For the crumble topping: In a bowl, rub together the remaining 30g butter/margarine and 50g plain flour until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Stir in the remaining 50g grated cheddar cheese and a pinch of salt and pepper into the crumble mix.
Evenly sprinkle the crumble topping over the saucy vegetables.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the crumble topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.

title: Traditional Rice Pudding
steps: Preheat your oven to 150°C (130°C fan/Gas Mark 2).
Grease an oven-safe baking dish (approx. 1.5 litre capacity) with the butter.
Pour the pudding rice into the greased dish. Sprinkle over the caster sugar.
Pour the milk over the rice and sugar. Add the vanilla extract, if using, and stir gently to combine.
Grate a tiny pinch of nutmeg over the top of the pudding.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for 1.5 to 2 hours.
Stir the pudding halfway through the cooking time (after about 45 minutes) to prevent the rice from sticking to the bottom.
Continue baking until the rice is tender, the milk has been mostly absorbed, and a creamy skin has formed on top.
Serve warm, perhaps with a dollop of jam or a sprinkle of cinnamon if desired.

title: Lancashire Hotpot
steps: Cut the lamb/beef into 2-3cm pieces. Season with salt and pepper. Toss in 1 tablespoon of flour.
Heat oil in a large, oven-safe casserole dish or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat. Brown the meat in batches, then set aside.
Thinly slice the onions and carrots. Add them to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Return the meat to the pan. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of flour and cook for 1 minute.
Dissolve the beef stock cubes in 500ml hot water. Pour into the pan. Add soy sauce and bay leaf. Bring to a simmer.
Cover the casserole dish and cook in a preheated oven at 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3) for 1 hour.
While the stew cooks, peel the potatoes and slice them thinly (about 3mm thick).
After 1 hour, remove the casserole from the oven. Arrange the potato slices neatly over the top of the stew, overlapping slightly.
Brush the top layer of potatoes with a little oil or melted butter and season with salt and pepper.
Return the uncovered dish to the oven and cook for another 1 to 1.5 hours, or until the lamb/beef is very tender and the potato topping is golden and crisp.

title: Classic Apple Crumble
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Peel, core, and slice the cooking apples into roughly 1.5cm chunks.
Place the apple slices in an oven-safe pie dish. Sprinkle with the granulated sugar, a generous pinch of cinnamon, and a splash of lemon juice.
Toss the apples gently to coat, then spread them evenly in the dish.
For the crumble topping: In a large bowl, combine the plain flour and the cold, cubed butter/margarine.
Using your fingertips, rub the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs.
Stir in the porridge oats (if using) and the caster sugar until well combined.
Evenly sprinkle the crumble mixture over the prepared apples, ensuring they are completely covered.
Bake in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the crumble topping is golden brown and the apple filling is soft and bubbling.
Serve warm, optionally with custard, ice cream, or cream.

title: Pasta e Fagioli (Hearty Bean & Pasta Soup)
steps: Finely chop the onion, carrot, and celery. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and stir well. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and add 1 litre of water.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then add the drained and rinsed cannellini beans.
Add the small pasta to the pot and cook according to package directions, usually 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
The soup should thicken as the pasta cooks and releases starch. If it becomes too thick, add a little more hot water.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley if desired.
Serve hot with crusty bread for a complete meal.

title: Cacio e Pepe (Cheese & Pepper Pasta)
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, crush the black peppercorns coarsely using a mortar and pestle or a rolling pin.
Heat a large skillet or frying pan over medium heat. Add the crushed peppercorns and toast for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add a splash of olive oil to the skillet with the toasted pepper. Ladle about 100-150ml of the pasta cooking water into the skillet and bring to a gentle simmer.
Drain the pasta, reserving at least 200ml of the starchy cooking water. Add the drained pasta directly to the skillet with the pepper mixture.
Remove the skillet from the heat. Add half of the grated Pecorino Romano cheese to the pasta, tossing vigorously to combine.
Gradually add more pasta water, a tablespoon at a time, and continue tossing until a creamy emulsion forms and the cheese melts into a smooth sauce.
Add the remaining Pecorino Romano and toss again until the sauce is glossy and coats the pasta beautifully.
If the sauce is too thick, add a tiny bit more pasta water. If too thin, add a little more cheese.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra black pepper and a sprinkle of Pecorino Romano if desired.

title: Aglio e Olio e Peperoncino (Garlic, Oil & Chilli Pasta)
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, thinly slice the garlic cloves. If using fresh chilli, deseed and finely slice it. If using dried flakes, measure them out.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the sliced garlic and chilli (flakes or fresh).
Gently cook the garlic until it is fragrant and lightly golden, being careful not to burn it (burnt garlic becomes bitter). This should take 3-5 minutes.
When the pasta is nearly ready, scoop out about 150ml of the starchy pasta cooking water.
Drain the spaghetti and immediately add it to the skillet with the garlic and chilli oil.
Toss the pasta well to coat it in the oil. Add about half of the reserved pasta water and continue tossing vigorously.
The pasta water will emulsify with the oil to create a light, glossy sauce. Add more pasta water if needed to achieve the desired consistency.
Stir in the fresh chopped parsley. Season with a pinch of salt if necessary (the pasta water is already salted).
Serve immediately, ensuring each serving gets plenty of garlic and chilli from the pan.

title: Risotto ai Funghi (Mushroom Risotto)
steps: Bring 1 litre of water to a simmer in a separate pot and dissolve the vegetable stock cube in it. Keep the stock hot over low heat.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Slice the mushrooms.
In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil and half of the butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the Arborio rice to the pot and stir constantly for 2-3 minutes until the edges of the grains become translucent. This is called 'toasting' the rice.
Pour in a ladleful of hot stock, stirring continuously until it is almost completely absorbed by the rice.
Continue adding the hot stock, one ladleful at a time, stirring constantly and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding the next. This process should take about 20-25 minutes.
The rice should be creamy but still have a slight bite (al dente). If you run out of stock and the rice isn't cooked, use some hot water.
Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the remaining butter and grated Parmesan cheese. Beat vigorously for a minute to make the risotto incredibly creamy (mantecare).
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Let it rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Serve immediately, garnished with fresh chopped parsley and extra Parmesan if desired.

title: Pasta alla Norma (Sicilian Aubergine Pasta)
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Slice the aubergine into 1cm thick rounds or cubes. Toss with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread the aubergine in a single layer on a baking tray. Roast for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and lightly browned.
While the aubergine roasts, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and let it gently cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has slightly thickened.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
Add the roasted aubergine to the tomato sauce and gently stir to combine. Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Drain the pasta, reserving about 100ml of the pasta cooking water. Add the drained pasta directly to the sauce.
Toss everything together, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce seems too thick. Tear in most of the fresh basil leaves.
Serve immediately, garnished with remaining fresh basil. Traditionally, grated ricotta salata is added, but it's optional for a vegan version.

title: Pesto Pasta with Green Beans & Potatoes (Genoese Style)
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut them into small, bite-sized cubes (about 1.5 cm). Trim the ends off the green beans.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the potato cubes and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the green beans to the pot with the potatoes and cook for another 3 minutes.
Add the pasta to the same pot with the potatoes and green beans. Cook everything together according to the pasta package instructions until the pasta is al dente and the vegetables are tender.
Before draining, reserve about 100ml of the starchy pasta cooking water.
Drain the pasta, potatoes, and green beans thoroughly.
Return the drained ingredients to the empty pot (off the heat). Add the pesto and about half of the reserved pasta water.
Toss everything together until the pasta and vegetables are evenly coated in the pesto sauce. Add more pasta water if needed to achieve a smooth, loose sauce consistency.
Serve immediately. No need for extra cheese if using a traditional pesto, but a sprinkle of Parmesan is always welcome.

title: Spaghetti al Tonno (Tuna and Tomato Pasta)
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or frying pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and dried oregano, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and let it cook for 10-12 minutes to thicken slightly.
Drain the tuna (you can reserve a little of the oil for extra flavour if desired) and flake it into the tomato sauce. Stir gently to combine.
Season the sauce with salt and black pepper to taste. Keep the sauce warm over low heat.
Drain the spaghetti, reserving about 60ml of the pasta cooking water.
Add the drained spaghetti directly to the tuna and tomato sauce. Toss well to combine, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick.
Stir in fresh chopped parsley if using. Serve immediately for a quick and satisfying meal.

title: Risotto al Pomodoro (Simple Tomato Risotto)
steps: Bring 1 litre of water to a simmer in a separate pot and dissolve the vegetable stock cube in it. Keep the stock hot over low heat.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
Add the Arborio rice to the pot and stir constantly for 2-3 minutes until the edges of the grains become translucent.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and tomato passata. Stir well to combine with the rice.
Begin adding the hot vegetable stock, one ladleful at a time, stirring constantly and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding the next.
Continue this process for about 20-25 minutes until the rice is creamy but still has a slight bite (al dente).
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. If using, tear in the fresh basil leaves and stir through.
Remove from heat and let the risotto rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Serve hot. For a richer flavour, a knob of vegan butter can be stirred in before resting.

title: Pasta with Chickpeas and Spinach (Pasta con Ceci e Spinaci)
steps: Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic.
Stir in the tinned chopped tomatoes and the vegetable stock cube. Add 500ml of water and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the drained chickpeas to the pot and let them simmer in the tomato sauce for about 10 minutes to allow the flavours to meld.
Add the small pasta to the pot. Stir well and cook according to package directions, usually 8-10 minutes, until the pasta is al dente.
In the last 2-3 minutes of cooking, add the fresh spinach to the pot. Stir gently until the spinach wilts down into the sauce.
Check the consistency; if it's too thick, add a little more hot water. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, as the pasta will continue to absorb liquid if left to sit too long.

title: Creamy Chicken & Asparagus Tagliatelle
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the tagliatelle according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Trim the woody ends off the asparagus and chop the spears into 2-3 cm pieces.

Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

Add the chopped onion to the same skillet and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add the minced garlic and asparagus, cooking for another 3 minutes until the asparagus is bright green and slightly tender-crisp.
Crumble in the chicken stock cube and add 100ml of hot water to the skillet, stirring to dissolve the stock. Stir in the single cream.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and let it cook for 2-3 minutes to slightly thicken. Return the cooked chicken to the skillet.
Drain the tagliatelle, reserving about 60ml of the pasta cooking water. Add the drained pasta directly to the skillet with the chicken and asparagus sauce.
Toss everything together until the pasta is well coated. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of reserved pasta water until it reaches the desired consistency.
Stir in the fresh chopped parsley. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Serve immediately, topped with grated Parmesan cheese if desired.

title: Creamy Roasted Red Pepper Pasta
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Halve and deseed the red bell peppers. Place them cut-side down on a baking tray.
Chop the onion into large chunks and smash the garlic cloves (no need to peel for roasting). Add them to the baking tray with the peppers.
Drizzle everything with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast for 25-30 minutes until the peppers are softened and slightly charred.
Once roasted, remove the garlic skins. Transfer the roasted peppers, onion, and garlic to a blender. Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, single cream, and a crumbled vegetable stock cube.
Blend until completely smooth, adding a splash of hot water if needed to achieve a creamy consistency.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the fusilli according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, gently heat the blended red pepper sauce in a large saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Drain the pasta, reserving about 100ml of the starchy pasta cooking water. Add the drained pasta directly to the red pepper sauce.
Toss the pasta thoroughly with the sauce, adding a little reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick to coat evenly.
Stir in fresh basil leaves if using. Serve hot, garnished with a drizzle of olive oil or a sprinkle of Parmesan if desired.

title: Creamy Dal Makhani
steps: Soak the black urad dal overnight in plenty of cold water, then drain thoroughly.

In a slow cooker, combine the soaked dal, rinsed kidney beans, 1 litre of water, and half a teaspoon of salt. Cook on high for 4-5 hours or low for 8 hours until very tender (or pressure cook for 30 minutes).
Finely chop the onion, garlic, and ginger. Set aside for the tempering process.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chopped garlic and ginger to the pan, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, cumin powder, Kashmiri chilli powder, and remaining salt. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the oil separates.
Carefully transfer the cooked dal and kidney beans into the pan with the tomato mixture.
Stir well to combine all ingredients, mashing some of the dal against the side of the pan to thicken the curry slightly.
Add the butter and garam masala, stirring until the butter has melted completely into the dal.
Finally, stir in the double cream and heat through gently for 2-3 minutes, ensuring it does not boil. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired and serve hot.

title: Spicy Chicken Chettinad
steps: Cut the chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces and season with half a teaspoon of salt and a pinch of turmeric powder.

In a dry pan, lightly toast the dried red chillies, black peppercorns, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cardamom pods, and cinnamon stick over medium heat until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes.
Transfer the toasted spices to a blender and grind them into a fine powder. Set aside.
Chop one of the onions, all the garlic, and ginger. Blend these with half of the chopped tomatoes and a splash of water to form a smooth paste.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or karahi over medium-high heat. Add the remaining chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the blended onion-garlic-ginger-tomato paste to the pot. Cook, stirring frequently, until the raw smell disappears and the paste thickens, roughly 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the ground spice mixture and the remaining turmeric powder. Cook for another 2 minutes, ensuring not to burn the spices.
Add the seasoned chicken pieces to the pot and sear them on all sides until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and 100ml of water. Add the curry leaves and the remaining chopped tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and cooked through, stirring occasionally. Serve hot with rice or flatbread.

title: Bengali Macher Jhol (Fish Curry)
steps: Cut fish fillets into large pieces and marinate with half a teaspoon of turmeric powder and half a teaspoon of salt. Peel and cut potatoes into medium chunks.
Heat mustard oil (or vegetable oil) in a large pan or karahi over medium-high heat until it just begins to smoke (if using mustard oil, reduce heat once it stops smoking).
Gently pan-fry the marinated fish pieces for 2-3 minutes per side until lightly golden brown. Remove and set aside.
In the same oil, add the potato chunks and fry until they are partially cooked and lightly browned. Remove and set aside with the fish.
Add mustard seeds to the hot oil. Once they splutter, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the ginger paste, garlic paste, and slit green chillies. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the raw aroma disappears.
Add the remaining turmeric powder, chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and a pinch of salt. Cook the spices for 1 minute, adding a splash of water if they stick.
Stir in the chopped tomato and cook until it breaks down and the oil begins to separate from the masala, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in 500ml of hot water and bring the gravy to a boil. Add the fried potato chunks back into the simmering curry.
Reduce heat, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes until the potatoes are almost tender. Gently add the fried fish pieces to the gravy.
Cook uncovered for another 5-7 minutes until the fish is cooked through and the gravy has thickened slightly. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.

title: Goan Prawn Curry
steps: In a dry pan, lightly toast the dried red chillies, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds for 2 minutes until fragrant. Let cool.
Blend the toasted spices with the garlic, ginger, and half of the chopped onion, adding a splash of water if needed, to create a smooth curry paste.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add the remaining chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the blended curry paste and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring continuously, until the raw smell disappears and the oil starts to separate.
Add the turmeric powder and curry leaves, cooking for another minute.
Pour in the coconut milk and 100ml of water. Stir well and bring the curry to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the tamarind paste and salt to taste. Let the curry simmer for 5 minutes to allow the flavours to meld.
Add the defrosted prawns to the simmering curry. Cook for only 3-5 minutes, or until the prawns turn pink and are just cooked through, taking care not to overcook them.
Serve the Goan prawn curry immediately with steamed rice or crusty bread.

title: Hearty Chana Masala
steps: Finely chop the onion, mince the garlic, grate the ginger, and slit the green chillies lengthwise. Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and let them splutter for a few seconds.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it turns golden brown and softens, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and slit green chillies. Cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add the chopped tomatoes, coriander powder, turmeric powder, chilli powder, and salt. Mix well.
Cook the tomato mixture for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the oil starts to separate from the masala, indicating it's well cooked.
Stir in the drained chickpeas. Add 100ml of water and bring the curry to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat, cover the pan, and let it cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the chickpeas to absorb the flavours.
Stir in the garam masala and amchur powder (dried mango powder) for a tangy kick. Cook for another 2 minutes, stirring gently.
Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve the hearty chana masala hot with rice, naan, or roti.

title: Keralan Vegetable Stew (Avial Style)
steps: Peel and cut all the vegetables (carrots, green beans, potatoes, courgette) into roughly 2-inch long, thin strips.
In a large pot, combine the cut vegetables with 200ml of water, half a teaspoon of turmeric powder, and half a teaspoon of salt. Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover the pot, and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender but still hold their shape.
While the vegetables are cooking, prepare the coconut paste: In a blender, combine the dessicated coconut, green chillies, and cumin seeds with a splash of water.
Blend until a coarse paste is formed. Do not make it completely smooth; a little texture is desired.
Once the vegetables are cooked, stir in the prepared coconut paste. Cook for another 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld, ensuring it doesn't boil rapidly.
Gently fold in the plain vegan yoghurt. Heat through for just 1-2 minutes; do not boil the curry after adding the yoghurt to prevent curdling.
For the tempering: In a small frying pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the curry leaves and let them splutter for a few seconds.
Pour the hot tempered curry leaves over the vegetable stew. Stir gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve the Keralan vegetable stew warm with rice or as a side dish.

title: Classic Palak Paneer
steps: Cut the paneer into 1-inch cubes. Blanch the fresh spinach in boiling water for 1-2 minutes, then immediately drain and plunge into ice water to retain its vibrant green colour. Squeeze out excess water.
Blend the blanched spinach with a small piece of ginger and one green chilli until it forms a smooth purée. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Lightly fry the paneer cubes until golden brown on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add a little more oil if needed. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and remaining slit green chilli. Cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomato and cook until it softens and breaks down, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cumin powder, coriander powder, and turmeric powder. Cook the spices for 1 minute, adding a splash of water if they start to stick.
Pour in the spinach purée and mix thoroughly with the spice mixture. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavours to develop.
Add the fried paneer cubes to the spinach gravy. Stir gently to combine. Add salt to taste.
Stir in the butter and garam masala, mixing until the butter melts. Finish by drizzling in the double cream and heating through for 1-2 minutes without boiling. Serve hot with rice or naan.

title: Hearty Keema Aloo (Lamb Mince & Potato Curry)
steps: Peel and dice the potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Finely chop the onions, mince the garlic, grate the ginger, and slit the green chillies.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or karahi over medium-high heat. Add the lamb mince and brown it thoroughly, breaking it up with a spoon, about 8-10 minutes. Drain any excess fat if necessary.
Remove the browned mince from the pot and set aside. Add the chopped onions to the same pot and sauté until translucent and lightly golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and slit green chillies. Cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, chilli powder, and salt. Mix well.
Cook the tomato-spice mixture for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the oil separates from the masala.
Return the browned lamb mince to the pot. Add the diced potatoes and mix everything together thoroughly.
Pour in 250ml of hot water, bring the curry to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Stir in the frozen peas and garam masala. Cook for another 5 minutes, uncovered, until the peas are heated through and the curry has thickened to your desired consistency.
Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve the keema aloo hot with rice, naan, or crusty bread.

title: Simple Tadka Dal
steps: Rinse the toor dal thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Place the dal in a medium pot with 700ml of water, half a teaspoon of turmeric powder, and half a teaspoon of salt.

Bring the dal to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the dal is completely soft and cooked through. Mash it lightly with the back of a spoon if needed.
While the dal cooks, prepare the tadka. Finely chop the onion, tomato, mince the garlic, grate the ginger, and slit the green chillies.
Heat the vegetable oil in a separate small frying pan over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and let them splutter for a few seconds.
Add the asafoetida (hing) and immediately add the chopped onion. Sauté until the onion turns golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and slit green chillies. Cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomato and remaining chilli powder. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and blend into a thick masala.
Pour the hot tadka mixture directly into the cooked dal. Stir well to combine all the ingredients thoroughly.
Bring the dal back to a gentle simmer for 2-3 minutes to allow the flavours from the tadka to infuse.
Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve the simple tadka dal hot with rice, chapati, or roti.

title: Rich Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
steps: Cut chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl, marinate the chicken with plain yoghurt, lemon juice, half a teaspoon of salt, half a teaspoon of ginger-garlic paste (from the total), half a teaspoon of turmeric, and half a teaspoon of Kashmiri chilli powder. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally longer in the fridge.
Finely chop the onion. Mince the remaining garlic and grate the remaining ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and sear them in batches until lightly browned on all sides. Remove the chicken and set aside.
In the same pan, add a tablespoon of butter. Sauté the chopped onion until soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the remaining minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

Stir in the chopped tomatoes, remaining Kashmiri chilli powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down into a thick, pulpy sauce and the oil separates.

Add 150ml of hot water to the tomato mixture and bring to a simmer. Blend the sauce using an immersion blender until smooth, or carefully transfer to a regular blender and blend, then return to the pan.
Stir in the seared chicken pieces, garam masala, and crushed kasuri methi. Season with salt to taste. Simmer for 15-20 minutes, covered, until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Stir in the remaining butter and double cream. Heat through gently for 2-3 minutes, without boiling, until the curry is rich and creamy.
Garnish with fresh coriander if desired and serve the rich butter chicken hot with rice or naan bread.

title: Spicy Lamb Rogan Josh
steps: Cut the lamb into 1.5-inch cubes. Finely chop the onions. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the whole spices (cinnamon stick, cardamom pods, cloves, bay leaves) and fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until deeply golden brown, about 10-12 minutes, stirring frequently to ensure even cooking.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 2-3 minutes until their raw aroma disappears.
Add the diced lamb to the pot and sear it on all sides until well browned, about 8-10 minutes. This step is crucial for flavour development.

Reduce the heat to medium. Stir in the Kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and turmeric powder. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring continuously, adding a splash of water if the spices stick.
Add the chopped tomatoes and 150ml of water. Bring the mixture to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Season with salt. Cover the pot and reduce the heat to low. Simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the lamb is very tender and easily falls apart, stirring occasionally.
Once the lamb is tender, stir in the garam masala. Cook uncovered for another 10-15 minutes to allow the gravy to thicken and the oil to separate, creating a rich texture.
Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve the spicy lamb Rogan Josh hot with basmati rice or naan bread.

title: South Indian Coconut & Chickpea Curry
steps: Finely chop the onion and tomato. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger. Slit the green chillies lengthwise. Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or karahi over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds and cumin seeds, letting them splutter for a few seconds.
Add the curry leaves and slit green chillies, frying briefly until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and sauté until it turns translucent and lightly golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 2-3 minutes until their raw aroma disappears.
Add the chopped tomato, coriander powder, and turmeric powder. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and blend into a thick masala, and the oil begins to separate.
Pour in the full fat coconut milk. Stir well to combine all ingredients and bring the curry to a gentle simmer.

Add the drained chickpeas to the simmering curry. Season with salt to taste. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavours to deepen and the chickpeas to absorb the gravy.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the curry becomes too thick, add a splash of water to reach your desired consistency.
Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve the South Indian coconut and chickpea curry hot with steamed rice or a fresh chapati.

title: Garlic Chicken & Broccoli Stir-fry
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips or bite-sized pieces.
In a bowl, combine 10g soy sauce, 5g cornflour, and 10g water with the chicken. Mix well and set aside to marinate for 10 minutes.
Chop the broccoli into florets and peel the stalk, then slice it into thin pieces.
Mince the garlic cloves and grate the ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 20g soy sauce, rice vinegar, 5g cornflour, sugar, and 40g water to make the sauce.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated chicken and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the pan. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the broccoli florets and stalk pieces to the pan. Stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Give the prepared sauce a quick stir and pour it over the chicken and vegetables.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats everything evenly.
Serve hot with freshly cooked white rice.

title: Vegetable Egg Fried Rice
steps: If using freshly cooked rice, spread it on a tray to cool completely before using.
Finely dice the small onion and mince the garlic cloves.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt and pepper, and whisk well.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble them, breaking into small pieces as they cook. Once cooked, remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the wok. Add the diced onion and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and frozen mixed vegetables. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with your spatula. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, ensuring the rice heats through and gets slightly toasted.
Return the scrambled egg to the pan. Pour in the soy sauce and a dash of sesame oil (if using).
Stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes, mixing all ingredients thoroughly until everything is well combined and hot.
Chop the spring onion and garnish each serving before enjoying.

title: Beef in Black Bean Sauce with Peppers
steps: Pat the beef strips dry with kitchen paper. In a bowl, toss beef with 5g soy sauce, 5g cornflour, and 10g water. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Rinse the fermented black beans under cold water and roughly chop them. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger.
Chop the green pepper into 1-inch squares and dice the onion into similar sized pieces.
In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 15g soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, 5g cornflour, and 60g water to create the sauce.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add the marinated beef and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned on all sides. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the pan. Add the chopped black beans, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced onion and green pepper to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Return the cooked beef to the pan. Give the prepared black bean sauce a quick stir and pour it over the beef and vegetables.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats all the ingredients.
Serve hot with freshly cooked white rice.

title: Tofu & Mixed Vegetable Stir-fry
steps: Press the tofu: wrap it in kitchen paper or a clean tea towel, place a heavy object on top, and press for 15-20 minutes. Then cut into 1-inch cubes.
Chop broccoli into small florets. Peel and slice carrots thinly or into small matchsticks. Slice mushrooms.
Mince the garlic cloves and grate the ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, cornflour, and water to make the stir-fry sauce.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu cubes and fry for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the pan. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add carrots and broccoli to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until slightly tender.
Add the sliced mushrooms and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes until they soften.
Return the crispy tofu to the pan. Give the prepared sauce a quick stir and pour it over the vegetables and tofu.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring gently, until the sauce thickens and coats all ingredients.
Serve immediately with freshly cooked white rice.

title: Szechuan Chilli Chicken (Nut-Free)
steps: Cut chicken breast into 1-inch cubes. Marinate with 5g cornflour, 5g soy sauce, and 10g water. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Cut dried chillies into 1-inch pieces, discarding most seeds for less heat. Mince garlic and ginger. Slice spring onions, separating white and green parts.
In a small bowl, whisk together remaining 25g soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, remaining 5g cornflour, and 50g water to make the sauce.
Heat 15g vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add Szechuan peppercorns and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant. Remove peppercorns if you prefer to not eat them.
Add marinated chicken to the wok and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add remaining 15g vegetable oil to the wok. Add dried chillies, minced garlic, grated ginger, and white parts of spring onion. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return cooked chicken to the pan. Pour in the prepared Szechuan sauce. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken.
Stir in the green parts of the spring onion. Serve hot with freshly cooked white rice.

title: Prawn Fried Rice
steps: If using freshly cooked rice, spread it on a tray to cool completely before using.
Pat the defrosted prawns dry with kitchen paper. Mince the garlic cloves. Chop the spring onion.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt and pepper, and whisk well.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the prawns and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until they turn pink and opaque. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the wok. Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble them, breaking into small pieces. Remove and set aside.
Add minced garlic and frozen peas to the wok. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until peas are tender.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until the rice is heated through and slightly toasted.
Return the cooked prawns and scrambled egg to the pan. Add the soy sauce and a dash of sesame oil (if using).
Stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes, mixing all ingredients thoroughly until well combined and hot.
Stir in the chopped spring onion just before serving hot.

title: Sweet and Sour Chicken
steps: Cut chicken breast into 1-inch cubes. In a bowl, toss chicken with 5g cornflour and 10g water. Season with a pinch of salt.
Dice red and green bell peppers and onion into 1-inch pieces. Drain pineapple chunks, reserving 20g of the juice.
In a separate bowl, whisk together tomato ketchup, rice vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, remaining 10g cornflour, 60g water, and the reserved pineapple juice to make the sweet and sour sauce.
Heat 20g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and fry for 5-7 minutes until golden brown and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Add the remaining 20g vegetable oil to the pan. Add the diced peppers and onion. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they are tender-crisp.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Add the pineapple chunks.
Give the sweet and sour sauce a quick stir and pour it over the chicken and vegetables.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and beautifully coats all the ingredients.
Serve hot with freshly cooked white rice.

title: Garlic Pak Choi Stir-fry
steps: Wash the Pak Choi thoroughly. Separate the leaves and chop the thicker white stems into 1-inch pieces. Leave the green leaves whole or halve them.
Mince the garlic cloves and grate the ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat until shimmering.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until very fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add the white stem pieces of the Pak Choi to the pan. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the green leaves of the Pak Choi to the pan. Continue to stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until the leaves wilt.
Pour in the soy sauce and 30g water. Toss everything together and cook for another minute until the liquid has mostly evaporated and the Pak Choi is tender-crisp.
Serve immediately as a healthy side dish or light meal.

title: Home-style Chicken Chow Mein
steps: Boil water in a kettle. Place egg noodles in a bowl and pour hot water over them. Let sit for 3-4 minutes until tender, then drain and rinse with cold water to prevent sticking. Toss with a tiny bit of vegetable oil.
Slice chicken breast into thin strips. Marinate with 5g soy sauce, cornflour, and 10g water. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Shred cabbage thinly and julienne carrots into thin matchsticks. Mince garlic and grate ginger. Chop spring onions.
In a small bowl, whisk together remaining 25g soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and 20g water to make the chow mein sauce.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat.
Add marinated chicken and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining 10g vegetable oil to the wok. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add shredded cabbage and julienned carrots. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the drained noodles and cooked chicken to the wok. Pour in the prepared chow mein sauce.
Toss and stir-fry everything together for 2-3 minutes, ensuring the noodles and ingredients are well coated and heated through.
Stir in the chopped spring onions just before serving hot.

title: Tomato Egg Stir-fry with Rice
steps: Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt, and whisk well. Slice the tomatoes into wedges or chunks. Mince the garlic and chop the spring onion, separating white and green parts.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sugar, and 50g water to create the sauce for the tomatoes.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Pour in the beaten eggs and quickly scramble them until just set and still slightly moist. Do not overcook. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the pan. Add the minced garlic and white parts of the spring onion. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced tomatoes to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, gently breaking them down with your spatula until they start to soften and release their juices.
Pour in the prepared sauce over the tomatoes. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce slightly thickens.
Return the scrambled eggs to the pan with the tomatoes. Gently fold the eggs into the sauce, taking care not to break them up too much.
Stir in the green parts of the spring onion. Cook for another minute until everything is heated through.
Serve hot over freshly cooked white rice.

title: Classic Chicken Fried Rice
steps: If using freshly cooked rice, spread it on a tray to cool completely before using.
Dice the chicken breast into small 1/2 inch cubes. Mince the garlic cloves. Chop the spring onion.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt and pepper, and whisk well.
Heat 10g vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced chicken and stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 10g vegetable oil to the wok. Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble them, breaking into small pieces. Remove and set aside.
Add minced garlic and frozen mixed vegetables to the wok. Stir-fry for 3 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with your spatula. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, ensuring the rice heats through and gets slightly toasted.
Return the cooked chicken and scrambled egg to the pan. Add the soy sauce and a dash of sesame oil (if using).
Stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes, mixing all ingredients thoroughly until everything is well combined and hot.
Stir in the chopped spring onion just before serving hot.

title: Speedy Chicken Fajitas
steps: Slice chicken breast into thin strips.
Slice onion and bell peppers into thin strips.
Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add chicken strips to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Add sliced onions and peppers to the pan with the chicken.
Sprinkle the fajita seasoning mix over the chicken and vegetables.
Stir well to coat everything evenly and cook for another 5-8 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Warm the tortillas according to pack instructions (microwave or dry pan).
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the fajita mixture just before serving.
Serve hot fajita mixture in warm tortillas for a delicious meal.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Bean Burritos
steps: Rinse and drain black beans and kidney beans thoroughly.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic cloves.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic, cumin powder, and chilli powder, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, black beans, kidney beans, and vegetable stock cube dissolved in 100ml hot water.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens nicely.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to package instructions until fluffy.
Warm tortillas in the microwave or a dry pan to make them pliable.
To assemble, spread a spoonful of rice on a tortilla, then top with a generous serving of the bean mixture and a sprinkle of grated cheese.
Fold in the sides and roll up tightly to form a burrito. Serve immediately for a satisfying meal.

title: Classic Beef Chilli Con Carne
steps: Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic cloves.
Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat.
Add beef mince and brown for 5-7 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat.
Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, chilli powder, cumin, and paprika. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, rinsed and drained kidney beans, and the beef stock cube dissolved in 200ml hot water.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for at least 30 minutes (or longer for more flavour), stirring occasionally.
While chilli is simmering, cook the rice according to package instructions until light and fluffy.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more spice if desired.
Serve hot chilli con carne over cooked rice for a hearty meal.

title: Zesty Fish Tacos with Slaw
steps: Pat fish fillets dry and cut into strips. Sprinkle generously with smoked paprika and garlic powder.
Finely shred the white cabbage and grate the carrot for the refreshing slaw.
In a small bowl, mix shredded cabbage and grated carrot with the juice of half a lime. Set aside to marinate.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat.
Cook fish strips for 3-4 minutes per side until flaky and cooked through. (Alternatively, air-fry for 8-10 mins at 180C).
Warm corn tortillas in a dry pan or microwave until pliable and warm.
To assemble, place a portion of cooked fish in each warm tortilla.
Top the fish with a generous spoonful of the zesty slaw.
Garnish with chopped fresh coriander and a squeeze of the remaining lime juice.
Serve immediately for delicious and vibrant fish tacos.

title: Cheesy Loaded Nachos
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (Fan 160°C) for even cooking.
Spread tortilla chips evenly in a single layer on a large oven-safe dish or baking tray.
Rinse and drain the black beans thoroughly. Dice the red onion finely.
Scatter half of the grated cheese over the tortilla chips.
Spoon over the black beans, chopped tomatoes, and diced red onion.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheese evenly over the toppings, ensuring good coverage.
Arrange sliced jalapeños over the cheese for a spicy kick.
Bake in the preheated oven for 8-12 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the chips are slightly golden.
Remove from oven and let stand for a minute before serving.
Serve hot with generous dollops of sour cream and fresh chopped coriander scattered over the top.

title: Sweet Potato & Black Bean Enchiladas
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C (Fan 170°C) and prepare an ovenproof dish.
Peel sweet potatoes and cut into small cubes. Boil or steam until tender, about 10-15 minutes. Drain well.
Finely chop onion and mince garlic. Heat olive oil in a pan, add onion and cook until softened. Add garlic, chilli powder, and cumin, cook for 1 minute.
Stir in cooked sweet potatoes and rinsed black beans. Mash lightly with a fork to combine, creating the flavourful filling.

For the sauce: In a separate pan, heat a little oil, stir in plain flour to make a roux. Gradually whisk in vegetable stock and tinned chopped tomatoes until smooth and thickened. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Spread a thin layer of sauce in the bottom of an ovenproof dish.
Fill each tortilla with the sweet potato and bean mixture, roll up tightly, and place seam-side down in the dish.
Pour remaining sauce generously over the rolled tortillas, ensuring they are well covered.
Sprinkle generously with grated cheddar cheese for a golden crust.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden and bubbly. Serve hot from the oven.

title: Quick Chicken & Cheese Quesadillas
steps: Shred or finely dice the cooked chicken. Finely dice the bell pepper and red onion.
Heat a small amount of olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat.
Add diced bell pepper and red onion and cook for 3-5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the shredded chicken, cumin powder, and chilli powder. Cook for another 2 minutes to heat through and combine flavours.
Wipe the pan clean or use a separate dry pan. Place one tortilla in the pan over medium heat.
Sprinkle half of the grated cheese over the tortilla, leaving a small border.
Spoon half of the chicken and vegetable mixture onto one half of the cheese-covered tortilla.
Fold the other half of the tortilla over the filling, pressing down gently.
Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and the cheese is melted and gooey.
Remove from pan, cut into wedges, and repeat with remaining ingredients. Serve immediately for a quick snack or meal.

title: Speedy Prawn Tacos with Mango Salsa
steps: Prepare the mango salsa: Dice the mango, finely chop the red onion, and chop fresh coriander. Combine these in a bowl.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the mango salsa and mix gently. Set aside to allow flavours to meld.
Pat prawns dry with kitchen paper. Mince the garlic clove.
Heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and chilli flakes, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add prawns to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and cooked through, curling slightly.
Squeeze the remaining half lime juice over the cooked prawns for extra zest.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry pan or microwave until pliable and soft.
To assemble, place a portion of cooked prawns in each warm tortilla.
Top generously with the fresh and vibrant mango salsa.
Serve immediately for a refreshing and quick meal.

title: Mexican Rice Bowls with Chicken & Veg
steps: Cook rice according to package instructions until fluffy and ready.
Dice chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Finely chop the onion and dice the bell pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add chopped onion and diced bell pepper to the same pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in chilli powder and cumin powder, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return cooked chicken to the pan. Add rinsed black beans, sweetcorn, and tinned chopped tomatoes.
Stir well and simmer for 5-7 minutes to allow flavours to meld and sauce to thicken slightly.
To assemble, divide cooked rice among serving bowls.
Spoon the chicken and vegetable mixture generously over the rice.
Garnish with fresh chopped coriander and a squeeze of fresh lime juice for brightness.

title: Crunchy Lentil & Veggie Tostadas
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (Fan 160°C). Lightly brush corn tortillas with olive oil and bake for 8-10 minutes until crisp and golden. Set aside to cool and crisp further.
Finely chop onion, dice bell pepper, and mince garlic cloves.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper, cook until softened (5-7 minutes).
Add minced garlic, chilli powder, and cumin powder. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in rinsed and drained lentils, tinned chopped tomatoes, and vegetable stock cube dissolved in 100ml hot water.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened. Lightly mash some lentils for texture.
Prepare toppings: Shred lettuce finely, dice avocado (if using) into small cubes.
To assemble, spread a generous spoonful of the warm lentil mixture onto each crispy tostada shell.
Top with shredded lettuce and diced avocado for freshness.
Serve immediately while tostadas are still crunchy and warm.

title: Hearty Greek Lentil Soup (Fakes)
steps: Rinse the brown lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Set aside.
Finely chop the onion and carrot. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and carrot to the pot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and dried oregano, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add the rinsed lentils, tinned chopped tomatoes, vegetable stock, and bay leaf to the pot.
Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30-40 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in the red wine vinegar and season generously with salt and black pepper to taste.
Remove the bay leaf before serving. Serve hot, optionally with a drizzle of extra olive oil.

title: Spanish Chicken and Pepper Rice
steps: Cut the chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces and season with salt and pepper.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Dice the red bell pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large, shallow pan or paella dish over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken pieces and cook for 5-7 minutes until lightly browned on all sides. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the pan, cooking for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the rice to the pan and stir for 2 minutes until it becomes translucent and lightly toasted.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes, chicken stock, and saffron threads. Bring to a simmer.
Return the chicken to the pan, ensuring it's submerged in the liquid. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
Stir in the frozen peas, cover again, and cook for another 5-10 minutes, or until the rice is tender and most liquid has been absorbed.
Remove from heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes before serving.

title: Homemade Hummus with Warm Pita and Salad
steps: Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas thoroughly. Set aside a few whole chickpeas for garnish if desired.
Peel and finely mince the garlic cloves. Squeeze the juice from the lemon.
In a blender or food processor, combine the drained chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, 2 tbsp of olive oil, cold water, and a pinch of salt.
Blend until completely smooth, scraping down the sides as needed. If the hummus is too thick, add a little more water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Transfer the hummus to a serving bowl.
Warm the pita bread in a dry pan on the hob for about 1 minute per side until soft and lightly puffed.
Dice the cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion. Roughly chop the fresh parsley.
Serve the hummus drizzled with the remaining olive oil and garnished with chopped parsley, alongside the warm pita bread and a simple salad of diced vegetables.

title: Moroccan Lentil and Chickpea Tagine
steps: Rinse the brown lentils thoroughly under cold water. Dice the onion, carrots, and courgette. Mince the garlic and grate the fresh ginger.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot or tagine over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and smoked paprika. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the rinsed lentils, diced carrots, tinned chopped tomatoes, and vegetable stock to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes.
Stir in the diced courgette, drained chickpeas, and dried apricots. Continue to simmer, covered, for another 15-20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and lentils are cooked through.
Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. If the tagine is too thick, add a little more stock or water.
Garnish with fresh coriander before serving. Best served hot, optionally with couscous or crusty bread.

title: Greek Chicken Souvlaki with Tzatziki
steps: Cut the chicken breast into 2-3cm cubes. Mince two garlic cloves.

In a bowl, combine the chicken cubes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, the juice of half a lemon, dried oregano, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. Mix well and let marinate for at least 30 minutes (or longer in the fridge).
While the chicken marinates, prepare the tzatziki: Grate the cucumber and squeeze out as much excess water as possible using a clean tea towel.

In a separate bowl, combine the grated cucumber with the Greek yogurt, finely chopped fresh dill, the remaining minced garlic clove, a drizzle of olive oil, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper. Mix well and refrigerate.
Preheat your grill to a medium-high heat. Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Grill the chicken skewers for 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through and nicely charred.
Slice the lettuce, tomatoes, and red onion for serving.
Serve the hot chicken souvlaki skewers with a generous dollop of tzatziki and a fresh side salad.

title: Halloumi and Vegetable Skewers
steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for at least 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Dice the halloumi cheese into 2cm cubes. Cut the bell peppers, courgette, and red onion into similar-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, combine the halloumi and chopped vegetables with olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Toss gently to ensure everything is evenly coated.
Thread the halloumi and vegetables alternately onto the soaked skewers.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. You can also cook these in an air fryer at 180°C or roast in the oven at 200°C (fan 180°C).
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 10-15 minutes, turning frequently, until the halloumi is golden brown and the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the cooked skewers just before serving. Serve hot as a main course or side dish.

title: Spiced Couscous with Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Chop the bell peppers, carrots, red onion, and courgette into bite-sized pieces.
Toss the chopped vegetables with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper on a large baking tray.
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly caramelised, stirring halfway through.
While the vegetables are roasting, prepare the couscous: Place the dry couscous in a heatproof bowl.

Boil the vegetable stock (or water) in a kettle. Pour the hot stock over the couscous, ensuring it is just covered. Add a drizzle of olive oil, cover the bowl with a plate or cling film, and let it sit for 5 minutes.
After 5 minutes, fluff the couscous with a fork.
Once the roasted vegetables are ready, combine them with the fluffed couscous and drained chickpeas in a large bowl.
Chop the fresh parsley and stir it into the couscous mixture. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve warm.

title: Spanish Garlic Prawns (Gambas al Ajillo)
steps: Ensure prawns are fully defrosted and patted dry with kitchen roll. Peel and thinly slice the garlic cloves.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan or small earthenware dish over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the sliced garlic and chilli flakes to the hot oil. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and just beginning to turn golden (do not let it burn).
Add the defrosted prawns to the pan. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring continuously, until the prawns are heated through and slightly pinker.
Finely chop the fresh parsley. Squeeze the juice from the lemon.
Remove the pan from the heat. Stir in half of the chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice. Season with a pinch of salt to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with the remaining fresh parsley. Best enjoyed with plenty of crusty bread for dipping in the delicious garlic and chilli infused oil.

title: Homemade Falafel Wraps with Tahini Sauce
steps: Soak the dried chickpeas in cold water overnight (at least 12 hours), then drain thoroughly and pat dry. Do not cook them.
Roughly chop the onion, garlic, fresh parsley, and fresh coriander.
In a food processor, combine the soaked chickpeas, chopped onion, garlic, parsley, coriander, flour, baking powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and salt.
Pulse until the mixture is finely ground but still has some texture, similar to coarse sand. Do not over-process into a smooth paste. If the mixture is too wet, add a little more flour.
Form the falafel mixture into small patties or balls, about 2-3cm in diameter.
Heat about 2 inches of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan or pot over medium-high heat until it reaches 170-180°C (a small piece of falafel should sizzle and float).
Carefully fry the falafel in batches for 3-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on kitchen paper.
While falafel fries, prepare tahini sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together tahini, lemon juice, and cold water until smooth. Add more water if needed to reach desired consistency. Season with salt.
Warm the wholemeal wraps. Slice the lettuce, tomato, and cucumber.
Assemble the wraps by spreading tahini sauce, adding falafel, and topping with salad vegetables. Roll up and serve immediately.

title: Mediterranean Baked Fish with Tomatoes and Olives

ingredients.name: White Fish Fillets (e.g., Pollock, Cod, Basa)

steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
Pat the fish fillets dry with kitchen paper. Season both sides with salt and black pepper.
Finely mince the garlic cloves. Roughly chop the pitted black olives.
In an ovenproof dish large enough to hold the fish in a single layer, pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes.
Stir in the minced garlic, chopped black olives, dried oregano, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil into the tomatoes. Season the sauce lightly with salt and pepper.
Nestle the fish fillets into the tomato mixture, ensuring they are partially submerged.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of your fillets.
While the fish bakes, finely chop the fresh parsley and cut the lemon into wedges.
Once cooked, drizzle the remaining tablespoon of olive oil over the fish and squeeze fresh lemon juice over it. Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve hot, straight from the baking dish. This dish is excellent with crusty bread or a simple green salad.

title: Quick Chicken Red Thai Curry
steps: Slice chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and finely dice the onion.
Chop the red pepper into strips and trim the green beans.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or wok over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the red Thai curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken pieces to the pot and stir-fry until sealed and lightly browned on all sides.
Pour in the coconut milk and chicken stock, stirring well to combine.
Bring the curry to a gentle simmer, then add the red pepper and green beans.
Cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Meanwhile, cook the jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Serve the curry hot with the cooked jasmine rice, garnished with fresh coriander.

title: Green Tofu & Vegetable Curry
steps: Press the tofu to remove excess water, then cut into 2cm cubes.
Peel and slice carrots into thin rounds. Prepare broccoli florets and drain bamboo shoots.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or wok over medium-high heat. Fry tofu cubes until golden brown and crispy, then remove and set aside.
Add green Thai curry paste to the same pot and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable stock, stirring well to combine. Bring to a simmer.
Add the sliced carrots, broccoli florets, and bamboo shoots. Cook for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the sugar snap peas and the fried tofu. Cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Serve the green curry hot with the cooked jasmine rice, garnished with a squeeze of fresh lime juice.

title: Prawn Pad Thai-Style Noodles
steps: Soak rice noodles in hot water for 5-7 minutes until pliable, then drain and set aside.
Prepare the Pad Thai sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, tamarind paste, brown sugar, and fish sauce.
Mince the garlic and chop the spring onions, separating whites and greens. Roughly chop the roasted peanuts.
Heat half the oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add prawns and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until pink and cooked through. Remove from pan.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Crack in the egg and scramble until just set. Push to one side of the pan.
Add minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the soaked noodles and spring onion whites to the pan. Pour in the Pad Thai sauce and toss everything together for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the beansprouts and cooked prawns. Continue to toss for 1 minute until heated through.
Serve immediately, garnished with spring onion greens, chopped peanuts, and a wedge of fresh lime.

title: Spicy Chicken Larb Salad
steps: Heat a dry frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Drain any excess fat from the cooked chicken mince and transfer it to a mixing bowl.
Finely slice the red onion, roughly chop the fresh mint and coriander leaves.
Add the sliced red onion, mint, coriander, lime juice, fish sauce, and chilli flakes to the cooked chicken mince.
Stir in the ground toasted rice, ensuring all ingredients are well combined.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, adding more lime juice, fish sauce, or chilli flakes to your preference.
Serve the chicken larb warm or at room temperature, using crisp lettuce leaves as edible cups.

title: Peanut Noodles with Crispy Tofu & Veg
steps: Press tofu to remove excess water, then cut into small cubes. Grate ginger and mince garlic.
Cook spaghetti or linguine according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and rinse with cold water.
While pasta cooks, heat vegetable oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Fry tofu cubes until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Remove from pan and set aside.
Prepare the vegetables: Julienne the cucumber, carrots, and red bell pepper.
Make the peanut sauce: In a bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, minced garlic, sriracha, sesame oil, and 2-3 tablespoons of water until smooth and creamy.
In a large bowl, combine the drained noodles, crispy tofu, and all the julienned vegetables.
Pour the peanut sauce over the noodles and vegetables. Toss everything together until thoroughly coated.
Serve immediately, or chill for a refreshing cold noodle salad.

title: Budget Beef Massaman Curry
steps: Cut the beef stewing steak into bite-sized chunks. Peel and dice the potatoes into 2cm cubes, and roughly chop the onion.
Heat vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the beef chunks in batches, then remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium, add the chopped onion to the pot and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the Massaman curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Return the browned beef to the pot. Pour in the coconut milk and beef stock, stirring well to combine.
Add the diced potatoes, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Bring the curry to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and let it simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the beef is very tender, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, cook the jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Serve the hot Massaman curry with the cooked jasmine rice.

title: Coconut Lemongrass & Mushroom Soup
