steps: Bruise the lemongrass stalk with the back of a knife and slice into 2-inch pieces. Peel and slice the ginger.
Slice the mushrooms. Chop spring onions and coriander for garnish.
In a medium pot, combine vegetable stock, coconut milk, lemongrass, sliced ginger, and lime leaves. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms and chilli flakes to the pot. Continue to simmer for 5-7 minutes, until mushrooms are cooked through.
Remove the lemongrass and lime leaves from the soup.
Stir in the soy sauce and the juice of half a lime. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve the soup hot, garnished with fresh spring onions and coriander, and a wedge of fresh lime.

title: Spicy Thai Basil Chicken Stir-fry (Pad Krapow Gai)
steps: Cook jasmine rice according to package instructions and set aside.
Finely mince the garlic and chillies. Make sure basil leaves are clean and ready.
Prepare the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together fish sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and brown sugar.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Add minced garlic and chillies, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant (be careful not to burn).
Add the chicken mince to the pan. Break it up with a spoon and stir-fry until fully cooked and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour the prepared sauce over the chicken mince and stir well to coat. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Stir in the fresh basil leaves. Cook for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, until the basil wilts.
Serve the stir-fry immediately over the hot jasmine rice. If desired, top each serving with a freshly fried egg.

title: Vegan Red Curry with Sweet Potato & Chickpeas
steps: Peel and dice the sweet potato into 2cm cubes. Drain and rinse the canned chickpeas. Dice the onion.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or wok over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the red Thai curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced sweet potato to the pot and stir to coat with the curry paste.
Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable stock, stirring well to combine. Bring the curry to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the sweet potato is tender.
Stir in the drained chickpeas and fresh spinach. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, until the spinach has wilted.
Meanwhile, cook the jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Serve the vegan red curry hot with the cooked jasmine rice, garnished with a squeeze of fresh lime juice.

title: Spicy Thai Noodle Salad with Peanuts

ingredients.name: Fish Sauce (or soy sauce for vegan)

steps: Cook rice vermicelli noodles according to package instructions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and set aside.
Prepare the vegetables: Finely shred the red cabbage, julienne the carrots, red bell pepper, and cucumber. Slice the spring onions and roughly chop the coriander.
Make the dressing: In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, fish sauce (or soy sauce), brown sugar, minced garlic, finely chopped red chilli, and vegetable oil.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled noodles with all the prepared vegetables.
Pour the dressing over the noodle and vegetable mixture. Toss gently to ensure everything is evenly coated.
Roughly chop the roasted peanuts. Add most of the peanuts to the salad and toss again.
Serve immediately, garnished with the remaining chopped peanuts and fresh coriander.

title: Budget Chicken Teriyaki Donburi
steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob. Aim for fluffy, sticky rice.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces or strips.
Grate the fresh ginger and mince the garlic cloves finely.
In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, mirin, sugar, grated ginger, and minced garlic to make the teriyaki sauce.

Preheat your air fryer to 190°C. Toss the chicken pieces with a splash of vegetable oil and air fry for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through and slightly browned. Alternatively, pan-fry on the hob or bake in an oven.
Once the chicken is cooked, pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken in the air fryer basket (or pan) and cook for another 2-3 minutes, tossing to coat, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Slice the spring onions thinly for garnish.
Divide the cooked rice into two serving bowls. Top each with the saucy teriyaki chicken.
Garnish generously with sliced spring onions and a sprinkle of sesame seeds before serving hot.

title: Simple Beef Gyudon (Japanese Beef Bowl)

ingredients.name: Thinly sliced beef (frying steak or stir-fry beef)

steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob until tender.
Thinly slice the onion. If your beef isn't already thinly sliced, cut it into very thin strips against the grain.
In a medium pan or skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the thinly sliced beef to the pan and cook until it's no longer pink, breaking it up with a spoon.
Pour in the dashi stock, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar. Add the grated ginger. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld and the beef to become tender. The sauce should thicken slightly.
Slice the spring onions thinly for garnish.
Divide the hot cooked rice into two serving bowls. Spoon the beef and onion mixture generously over the rice.
Garnish with fresh spring onions before serving.

title: Quick Chicken Yaki Udon Stir-fry
steps: Prepare the udon noodles according to package instructions. If pre-cooked, rinse under hot water to separate. Drain well.
Cut the chicken breast into thin strips. Thinly slice the cabbage, carrot, onion, and bell pepper. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, mirin, and oyster sauce (or vegetarian alternative) to create the stir-fry sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Add the chicken strips and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the onion, carrot, and bell pepper to the same pan. Stir-fry for 3 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp.
Add the minced garlic, grated ginger, and sliced cabbage to the pan. Stir-fry for another 2 minutes until the cabbage wilts.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Add the prepared udon noodles and pour in the stir-fry sauce.
Toss everything together vigorously for 2-3 minutes, ensuring the noodles and vegetables are thoroughly coated in the sauce and heated through.
Stir in the sesame oil just before serving. Garnish with thinly sliced spring onion.

title: Simple Grilled Salmon Shioyaki with Rice
steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob until tender and fluffy.
Pat the salmon fillets dry with kitchen paper. This helps achieve a crispier skin.
Generously season both sides of the salmon fillets with salt. You can let them sit for 10-15 minutes if you have time, allowing the salt to draw out some moisture.
Preheat your oven's grill function to high, or preheat your air fryer to 200°C.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking tray lined with foil or in the air fryer basket.
Grill or air fry the salmon for 10-15 minutes, depending on thickness, until it's cooked through and the skin is crispy. If using an oven without a grill function, bake at 200°C for 12-18 minutes.
Once cooked, carefully transfer the salmon fillets to serving plates alongside the hot cooked rice.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the salmon, if desired.

title: Crispy Tofu Donburi with Ginger Scallion Sauce (Vegetarian)
steps: Press the tofu to remove excess water. You can wrap it in kitchen paper and place a heavy object on top for 20-30 minutes. Then cut the tofu into bite-sized cubes.
Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob.
Toss the tofu cubes with cornflour until evenly coated.

Heat vegetable oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Fry the tofu cubes in a single layer, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy on all sides (about 8-10 minutes). Remove and drain on kitchen paper.
While the tofu fries, prepare the ginger scallion sauce. Finely chop or thinly slice the spring onions and grate the fresh ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and water. Stir in the chopped spring onions and grated ginger.
Divide the hot cooked rice into two serving bowls. Top generously with the crispy tofu cubes.
Spoon the fragrant ginger scallion sauce over the tofu and rice. Serve immediately.

title: Homemade Chicken Katsu Curry
steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob.
Prepare the chicken katsu: Slice the chicken breast horizontally to create two thinner cutlets. Place between two sheets of cling film and lightly pound to an even thickness (about 1.5 cm).
Set up a breading station: one plate with plain flour, one shallow bowl with a beaten egg, and one plate with panko breadcrumbs.
Dredge each chicken cutlet in flour, then dip in beaten egg, ensuring it's fully coated, then press into the panko breadcrumbs until thoroughly covered.

Air Fryer Method: Lightly spray the breaded chicken with oil. Air fry at 190°C for 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown and cooked through. Deep Frying Method: Heat 2-3 cm of vegetable oil in a pan to 170°C. Fry chicken for 4-6 minutes per side until golden and cooked.
While chicken cooks, make the curry sauce: Finely chop the onion and carrot. Mince garlic and grate ginger.
In a saucepan, heat a splash of oil. Sauté onion and carrot for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add garlic, ginger, and curry powder; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the chicken stock to prevent lumps, then add soy sauce and honey/sugar. Bring to a simmer, stirring, until the sauce thickens (5-7 minutes).
Slice the cooked chicken katsu into strips.
Serve the hot rice on plates, topped with the sliced chicken katsu, and generously spoon the warm curry sauce over the chicken and rice.

title: Hearty Chicken Miso Ramen
steps: Hard boil the eggs: Place eggs in a saucepan, cover with cold water, bring to a boil, then cover and remove from heat. Let sit for 7-8 minutes. Rinse with cold water, peel, and halve.
Slice the chicken breast thinly or into bite-sized pieces. Sauté in a splash of oil in a saucepan until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same saucepan, add a little more oil if needed. Sauté the minced garlic and grated ginger for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock. Bring to a simmer.
In a small bowl, whisk the miso paste with a ladleful of hot stock until smooth. Stir this mixture back into the main stockpot. Add the soy sauce. Do not boil the broth vigorously after adding miso.
Cook the ramen noodles according to package instructions in a separate pot of boiling water. Drain well.
Thinly slice the spring onions.
Divide the cooked noodles between two large serving bowls. Ladle the hot miso broth over the noodles.
Top each bowl with the cooked chicken, halved boiled eggs, fresh spinach, and sliced spring onions.
Drizzle with sesame oil and sprinkle with chilli flakes, if using, before serving immediately.

title: Teriyaki Chicken and Veg Skewers
steps: If using bamboo skewers, soak them in water for at least 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Cut the chicken breast into 2-3 cm cubes. Cut the bell peppers and onion into similar-sized pieces.

In a bowl, combine soy sauce, mirin, sugar, grated ginger, and minced garlic to make the teriyaki marinade. Add the chicken cubes and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 15 minutes, or up to 30 minutes in the fridge.
Preheat your oven's grill function to medium-high, or preheat your air fryer to 190°C.
Thread the marinated chicken pieces and vegetable pieces alternately onto the soaked skewers.
Place the skewers on a baking tray lined with foil (for grilling) or in the air fryer basket.

Grill or air fry the skewers for 12-18 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender-crisp. You can brush with any remaining marinade halfway through cooking.
If serving with rice, cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob while the skewers cook.
Serve the hot teriyaki chicken and vegetable skewers immediately, with rice on the side if desired.

title: Speedy Ginger Garlic Prawn Stir-fry with Rice

ingredients.name: Raw king prawns (peeled, defrosted if frozen)
Optional: a pinch of chilli flakes

steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob.
Grate the fresh ginger and mince the garlic cloves. Slice the red bell pepper into thin strips. Thinly slice the spring onions, separating the white and green parts.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the white parts of the spring onions, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the bell pepper strips to the pan and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender-crisp.
Add the prawns to the pan. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, or until the prawns turn pink and are cooked through. Be careful not to overcook them, as they can become rubbery.
Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil. Add chilli flakes if desired. Toss everything together to coat the prawns and vegetables in the sauce. Cook for just 30 seconds more.
Remove from heat. Divide the hot cooked rice into two serving bowls. Top with the prawn stir-fry mixture.
Garnish with the green parts of the sliced spring onions before serving immediately.

title: Comforting Oyakodon (Chicken and Egg Rice Bowl)
steps: Cook the white rice according to package instructions on the hob.
Cut the chicken thigh into bite-sized pieces. Thinly slice the onion.
In a small pan (ideally one that can hold about 2 servings of rice underneath), combine the dashi stock, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the simmering sauce and cook for 3-5 minutes until it softens.
Add the chicken pieces to the pan. Continue to simmer for 5-7 minutes, ensuring the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken cooks, lightly beat two of the eggs in a small bowl, leaving some streaks of yolk and white for texture. The third egg can be beaten separately or mixed in.

Pour about two-thirds of the beaten egg mixture evenly over the simmering chicken and onion. Cover the pan immediately and cook for 1-2 minutes until the egg is mostly set but still slightly runny in places.
Pour the remaining beaten egg over the dish, re-cover, and cook for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, until the egg is cooked to your preferred consistency.
Divide the hot cooked rice into two serving bowls. Carefully slide the chicken and egg mixture from the pan over each bowl of rice.
Garnish generously with thinly sliced spring onions before serving immediately.

title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Çorbası)
steps: Rinse red lentils thoroughly under cold water until water runs clear. Drain well and set aside.
Finely chop the onion, carrot, and potato into small, even pieces.
Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the chopped carrot and potato, cooking for another 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the tomato paste, cumin, and paprika to the saucepan. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Pour in the rinsed lentils and vegetable stock. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Simmer the soup for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Carefully blend the soup using an immersion blender until smooth. Alternatively, let it cool slightly and blend in batches in a regular blender.
Season the soup with salt, black pepper, and dried mint. Heat through for another minute and serve hot.

title: Persian Adas Polo (Lentil Rice with Dates)
steps: Rinse basmati rice several times until the water runs clear. Soak in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Rinse green lentils and boil them in a saucepan with plenty of water for 15-20 minutes, or until tender but still firm. Drain well.
Finely chop the onion. Pit and chop the dates into small pieces.
Heat 15ml of olive oil in a large non-stick pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 8-10 minutes until deeply caramelised and golden brown.
Remove half of the crispy onions from the pot and set aside for garnish.
Add the chopped dates, cinnamon, cumin, turmeric, salt, and pepper to the remaining onions in the pot. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring continuously.
Add the drained basmati rice and cooked lentils to the pot. Gently mix everything together without mashing the rice or lentils.
Pour in enough boiling water to just cover the rice and lentils (approx 300ml). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to very low.
Drizzle with the remaining 10ml olive oil, cover the pot tightly with a lid, and cook for 25-30 minutes, or until rice is fluffy and water is absorbed. Serve topped with the reserved crispy onions.

title: Turkish Lahmacun (Minced Lamb Flatbreads)
steps: Prepare the dough: In a bowl, combine flour, yeast, 1 tsp salt, 150ml warm water, and 5ml olive oil. Knead for 5 minutes until smooth. Cover and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F) with a baking tray or pizza stone inside to get very hot.
Prepare the topping: Finely chop onion, red bell pepper, tomato, garlic, and fresh parsley. A food processor works well for a fine texture.

In a large bowl, combine lamb mince with the chopped vegetables, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, chilli flakes, salt, black pepper, and remaining 5ml olive oil. Mix thoroughly by hand until well combined.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each piece into a very thin, oval or round shape (approx. 20-25 cm diameter).
Spread an even, thin layer of the lamb mince topping over each rolled-out dough, leaving a small border around the edges.
Carefully transfer one lahmacun onto the hot baking tray/stone in the oven.
Bake for 7-10 minutes, or until the edges of the flatbread are golden and crispy, and the meat topping is cooked through and browned.
Remove from oven and repeat with remaining lahmacuns. Serve immediately, traditionally with lemon wedges and extra fresh parsley.

title: Lebanese Mujadara (Lentils and Rice with Crispy Onions)
steps: Rinse lentils thoroughly and place in a saucepan with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until tender but not mushy. Drain and set aside.
Rinse basmati rice until water runs clear. Soak in cold water for 15 minutes, then drain.
Thinly slice all the onions into half-moons. This is crucial for crispy onions.
Heat 40ml of olive oil in a large, deep pot or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the sliced onions and spread them in an even layer.
Fry the onions, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes until they are deeply golden brown and crispy. This takes patience.
Once crispy, remove about two-thirds of the onions with a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with kitchen paper to drain excess oil. These are for garnish.
To the remaining onions and oil in the pot, add the drained rice, cooked lentils, cumin powder, salt, and black pepper. Stir gently to combine.

Pour in 400ml of boiling water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to very low, cover tightly with a lid, and simmer for 18-20 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Remove from heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork. Serve warm, garnished generously with the crispy fried onions.

title: Turkish Köfte with Bulgur Pilaf
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Grate half an onion and mince the garlic for the köfte.
In a bowl, combine beef mince, grated onion, minced garlic, breadcrumbs, egg, finely chopped parsley, 1.5g cumin, 1.5g paprika, 2g salt, and 1g black pepper. Mix thoroughly until well combined.
Form the mixture into small, oval-shaped patties or logs (about 8-10 köfte). Place them on a baking tray lined with baking paper.
Bake the köfte in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway, until cooked through and nicely browned.
While köfte bake, prepare the bulgur pilaf. Finely chop the remaining half onion.
Heat 10ml olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato paste, remaining cumin and paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the bulgur wheat to the saucepan and stir for 2 minutes, coating it in the tomato mixture.

Pour in the vegetable stock and bring to a boil. Season with remaining salt and pepper. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until the bulgur has absorbed all the liquid and is tender.
Remove bulgur from heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Serve the baked köfte alongside the bulgur pilaf.

title: Persian Kuku Sabzi (Herb Frittata)
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F).
Thoroughly wash and finely chop all the fresh herbs (parsley, coriander, dill, and spring onions). Squeeze out any excess moisture from the chopped herbs using kitchen paper.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs until well combined. Add the chopped herbs, chopped walnuts, dried cranberries, baking powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix everything gently to combine.
Heat 15ml of olive oil in a 20-24cm oven-safe, non-stick frying pan over medium heat.
Pour the egg and herb mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly. Cook on the hob for 5-7 minutes until the edges are set and lightly golden.
Transfer the frying pan to the preheated oven. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the kuku sabzi is puffed up, set in the middle, and golden brown.
Remove from the oven, let it cool in the pan for a few minutes before slicing into wedges. Drizzle with a little extra olive oil if desired before serving.

title: Lebanese Batata Harra (Spicy Potatoes)
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut them into 2cm cubes. Rinse the potato cubes under cold water to remove excess starch, then pat them completely dry with a clean tea towel.
Heat 40ml of olive oil in a large frying pan or deep pot over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the dried potato cubes in a single layer if possible, or in batches if your pan is too small.

Fry the potatoes for 15-20 minutes, turning them occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides and cooked through. Remove potatoes from the pan with a slotted spoon and set aside on kitchen paper to drain excess oil.
While potatoes cook, finely mince the garlic cloves and roughly chop the fresh coriander.
Pour out most of the oil from the pan, leaving about 20ml. Reduce the heat to medium-low.
Add the minced garlic and red chilli flakes to the pan. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Return the crispy potatoes to the pan. Add the chopped fresh coriander, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until the potatoes are thoroughly coated with the aromatic mixture. Serve immediately as a hearty main.

title: Turkish Imam Bayildi (Stuffed Eggplant)
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Wash eggplants and slice them lengthwise into halves, leaving the stem intact. Scoop out some of the flesh, leaving a 1cm border, and dice the scooped-out flesh.
Generously salt the eggplant halves and diced flesh, then let them sit for 15 minutes to draw out bitter liquids. Rinse and pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.
Finely chop the onion, mince the garlic, dice the bell pepper, and chop the tomatoes. Roughly chop fresh parsley.
Heat 30ml of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the eggplant halves, cut-side down, and fry for 5-7 minutes until softened and lightly browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the diced eggplant flesh to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until soft. Remove and set aside.
Add the remaining 20ml of olive oil to the pan. Sauté the chopped onion for 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and bell pepper, cooking for another 3 minutes.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, cooked diced eggplant, sugar, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Place the fried eggplant halves in a baking dish. Fill each cavity generously with the vegetable mixture. Pour 100ml water around the eggplants in the dish.
Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the eggplants are very tender and the filling is bubbling. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving hot or at room temperature.

title: Persian Ghormeh Sabzi (Herb Stew - Simplified)
steps: Dice stewing steak into 2-3cm cubes. Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Roughly chop all the fresh herbs (parsley, coriander, spring onions) and spinach. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the beef or lamb cubes in batches on all sides. Remove meat and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the browned meat to the pot. Add the chopped herbs and spinach. Stir frequently for 5-7 minutes until the herbs have wilted and their colour has deepened.
Pour in the water or beef stock. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the meat is very tender.
Drain and rinse the tinned kidney beans. Add them to the stew along with the lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Continue to simmer, uncovered, for another 15-20 minutes to allow the flavours to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot, traditionally with basmati rice.

title: Ghanaian Chicken Jollof Rice
steps: Pat the chicken thighs dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Chop the red onion, red bell pepper, Scotch bonnet (deseeded for less heat), garlic, and ginger roughly. Place them into a blender.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes and tomato paste to the blender. Blend until a smooth sauce is formed.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken thighs on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove chicken and set aside.
Pour the blended sauce into the same pot, scraping up any browned bits. Add the curry powder, dried thyme, and bay leaves.
Crumble in the chicken stock cube and stir well. Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly and the oil separates.
Rinse the long grain rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well.
Add the rinsed rice to the simmering sauce, stirring to combine. Place the browned chicken thighs on top of the rice.
Pour in enough hot water or chicken stock to just cover the rice and chicken (approximately 500ml). Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly with a lid (or foil then lid), and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the rice is cooked through and has absorbed all the liquid.
Do not stir the rice during cooking. Once cooked, remove from heat and let it rest, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff the Jollof rice with a fork, gently mixing in the chicken. Serve hot, optionally with a side salad.

title: Nigerian Efo Riro (Spinach Stew) with Smoked Mackerel
steps: Wash the spinach thoroughly and chop roughly. If using frozen spinach, thaw and squeeze out excess water.
Chop the red bell peppers, Scotch bonnet (deseeded for less heat), and half of the red onion roughly. Place into a blender with the tinned chopped tomatoes.
Blend until a relatively smooth but still slightly chunky pepper mix is formed. Set aside.
Heat the palm oil (or vegetable oil) in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Finely chop the remaining half of the red onion and add it to the hot oil. Sauté until translucent, about 3-5 minutes.
Pour the blended pepper mix into the pot. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and add a pinch of salt.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil floats to the top.
Break the smoked mackerel fillets into large flakes, removing any bones and skin. Gently fold the mackerel into the simmering sauce.
Add the chopped spinach to the pot, in batches if necessary, and stir until it wilts and is combined with the sauce.
Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the Efo Riro hot, traditionally with fufu, pounded yam, or boiled rice.

title: Kenyan Sukuma Wiki with Ugali
steps: Wash the kale thoroughly. Remove the tough stems and finely chop the leaves. Set aside.
Finely chop the red onion and mince the garlic. Dice the tomatoes into small cubes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pan and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
Stir in the diced tomatoes. Cook for 5-7 minutes, pressing down on the tomatoes to help them break down and release their juices.
Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and add a pinch of salt. Stir well to combine.
Add the chopped kale to the pan. Cook, stirring frequently, until the kale wilts and becomes tender, about 7-10 minutes.
While the Sukuma Wiki cooks, prepare the Ugali. Bring 750ml of water to a boil in a separate medium-sized pot.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low. Gradually add the maize meal to the water, stirring continuously with a wooden spoon to prevent lumps from forming.
Continue stirring and pressing the mixture against the sides of the pot to incorporate all the maize meal. The mixture will thicken considerably.
Cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the Ugali is a firm, smooth dough-like consistency and pulls away from the sides of the pot.
Taste the Sukuma Wiki and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve the Sukuma Wiki hot alongside portions of Ugali.

title: Budget Piri Piri Chicken with Spicy Rice
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan / Gas Mark 5).
To make the piri-piri marinade: Roughly chop the red bell pepper, chillies (deseeded for less heat), garlic, and half of the red onion.
Place the chopped vegetables into a blender. Add the juice of half a lemon, smoked paprika, dried oregano, and 2 tablespoons (30g) of vegetable oil.
Blend until a relatively smooth paste is formed. Season with salt and black pepper.
Score the chicken drumsticks/thighs a few times with a sharp knife to help the marinade penetrate. Place chicken in a bowl.
Pour the piri-piri marinade over the chicken, ensuring all pieces are well coated. Marinate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally longer in the fridge.
Arrange the marinated chicken on a baking tray lined with baking paper and bake in the preheated oven for 35-45 minutes, or until cooked through and nicely browned, turning halfway.
While the chicken bakes, prepare the spicy rice. Finely chop the remaining half of the red onion.
Heat 1 tablespoon (15g) of vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the rinsed long grain rice to the pan and stir for 1-2 minutes until coated in oil.
Stir in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Crumble in the chicken stock cube and pour in 500ml of hot water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover tightly with a lid, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice has absorbed all the liquid.
Remove the rice from heat and let it stand, covered, for 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork.
Serve the hot piri-piri chicken alongside the spicy rice, optionally with a wedge of fresh lemon.

title: West African Groundnut (Peanut) Stew with Chicken
steps: Cut the chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. Season with salt and pepper.
Finely chop the red onion, mince the garlic, and grate the fresh ginger. Deseed and finely chop the Scotch bonnet if using.
Peel the sweet potato and carrots, then cut them into roughly 2cm cubes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and brown them on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion to the pot and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and chopped Scotch bonnet (if using). Cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, sweet potato, and carrots to the pot. Stir well. Crumble in the chicken stock cube.
Add 500ml of hot water and bring the mixture to a simmer. Cover and cook for 10 minutes, or until the vegetables start to soften.
In a separate small bowl, whisk the peanut butter with a ladleful of the hot stew liquid until smooth. Stir this peanut butter mixture back into the stew.
Return the browned chicken to the pot. Stir well to combine. Simmer, uncovered, for another 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fresh spinach and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve the groundnut stew hot, traditionally with rice or couscous.

title: Ghanaian Red Red (Beans and Plantain)
steps: Drain and rinse the tinned black-eyed beans thoroughly. Set aside.
Peel the ripe plantains and slice them into 1-inch thick rounds. If very large, halve the rounds.

Heat half of the palm oil (or vegetable oil, 40g) in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry the plantain slices in batches until golden brown and caramelised on both sides. Remove and set aside on paper towels to drain excess oil.
Finely chop the red onion, mince the garlic, and grate the fresh ginger. Deseed and finely chop the Scotch bonnet if using.
In a separate large pot or Dutch oven, heat the remaining palm oil (or vegetable oil) over medium heat.
Add the chopped red onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and chopped Scotch bonnet (if using). Cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes to the pot. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and add a pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer.
Cook the tomato mixture for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly and the oil begins to separate.
Stir in the drained black-eyed beans. Add about 100ml of hot water to thin the stew slightly, if needed.
Bring the stew back to a gentle simmer and cook for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve the Red Red hot, topped with the fried plantains.

title: Ethiopian Misir Wot (Red Lentil Stew)
steps: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well and set aside.
Finely chop the red onion. Mince the garlic and grate the fresh ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a medium-sized pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped red onion and sauté until very soft and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. This step is crucial for flavour.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.

Add the Berbere spice mix (or a blend of 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1/2 tsp ground ginger, 1/2 tsp ground turmeric, a pinch of cardamom) to the pot. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes to toast the spices.
Stir in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Cook for 5 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and blend with the spices.
Add the rinsed lentils to the pot. Pour in 750ml of hot water and crumble in the vegetable stock cube. Add a pinch of salt.

Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot and simmer for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are very tender and have broken down to create a thick stew.
If the stew becomes too thick, add a little more hot water to reach your desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve the Misir Wot hot, traditionally with injera, or with rice or flatbreads for a home-cookable meal.

title: Nigerian Egusi Soup with Smoked Mackerel
steps: Wash the spinach thoroughly and chop roughly. If using frozen, thaw and squeeze out excess water.
Break the smoked mackerel fillets into large flakes, removing any bones and skin. Set aside.
Chop the red bell peppers, Scotch bonnet (deseeded for less heat), and half of the red onion roughly. Place into a blender with the tinned chopped tomatoes.
Blend until a relatively smooth but still slightly chunky pepper mix is formed. Set aside.
In a small bowl, mix the ground egusi seeds with about 100ml of water to form a thick, smooth paste. Set aside.
Heat the palm oil (or vegetable oil) in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Finely chop the remaining half of the red onion and add it to the hot oil. Sauté until translucent, about 3-5 minutes.
Pour the blended pepper mix into the pot. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and add a pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer.
Cook the pepper mixture for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and the oil floats to the top.
Add small spoonfuls of the egusi paste directly into the simmering stew. Do not stir immediately; let the egusi cook undisturbed for 5 minutes.

Gently stir the egusi into the stew, breaking it up. It will thicken the soup. Cook for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to ensure the egusi is well cooked and the soup has a granular texture.
Fold in the flaked smoked mackerel and the chopped spinach. Cook for another 5-7 minutes, until the spinach is wilted and the flavours are well combined.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve the Egusi Soup hot, traditionally with fufu, pounded yam, or rice.

title: East African Kachumbari with Homemade Chapatis
steps: For the Kachumbari: Dice the tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber into small, even pieces.
Finely chop the fresh coriander. Combine all the diced vegetables and coriander in a mixing bowl.
Pour in the lime juice and add a pinch of salt. Toss gently to combine all ingredients. Set aside for the flavours to meld.
For the Chapatis: In a large bowl, combine the plain flour and 1 teaspoon of salt. Add 1 tablespoon (15g) of vegetable oil.
Gradually add the warm water, mixing with your hands until a soft, pliable dough forms. You may not need all the water, or you might need a tiny bit more.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until it is smooth and elastic. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball.
Lightly flour your work surface and a rolling pin. Roll each ball into a thin circle, about 6-8 inches in diameter. Try to make them as round and thin as possible.
Heat a flat non-stick frying pan or griddle over medium-high heat. Do not add oil to the pan for the first side.
Place one chapati in the hot pan. Cook for about 30-45 seconds until small bubbles appear on the surface and the underside has light brown spots.
Flip the chapati. Drizzle a tiny amount of vegetable oil (about 1/2 tsp) onto the cooked side and spread it with the back of a spoon.
Cook for another 30-45 seconds, then flip again. Drizzle oil on the other side. Press gently with a spatula to help it puff up (if it does). Cook until golden brown spots appear on both sides.
Remove the cooked chapati and keep it warm in a clean kitchen towel while you cook the remaining chapatis.
Serve the fresh Kachumbari immediately with the warm homemade Chapatis.

title: West African Fish and Vegetable Stew
steps: Cut the white fish fillets into large chunks (about 2-3 inches). Season lightly with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Finely chop the red onion, mince the garlic, and grate the fresh ginger. Deseed and finely chop the Scotch bonnet if using.
Dice the red bell pepper. Peel and chop the carrots and potatoes into roughly 1-inch cubes. Trim the green beans.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, chopped red bell pepper, and Scotch bonnet (if using). Cook for 3-5 minutes until the peppers soften slightly.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, diced carrots, and diced potatoes to the pot. Stir well. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube.
Pour in 500ml of hot water. Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the carrots and potatoes are nearly tender.
Add the green beans to the pot. Gently place the fish chunks into the simmering stew.
Cover and continue to simmer for another 7-10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily, and the green beans are tender-crisp.
Be careful not to overcook the fish, as it can become dry. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve the West African Fish and Vegetable Stew hot, ideally with rice, couscous, or plain boiled yams.

title: East African Irio (Mashed Peas, Potatoes & Maize)
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut them into evenly sized chunks. Place them in a large pot, cover with cold water, and add a pinch of salt.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are very tender when pierced with a fork.
About 5 minutes before the potatoes are done, add the frozen peas to the pot with the potatoes.
While the potatoes and peas cook, finely slice the spring onions. If using tinned sweetcorn, drain it. If using frozen, no need to thaw.
Once the potatoes and peas are tender, drain them thoroughly. Return them to the hot pot or a large mixing bowl.
Add the sweetcorn and sliced spring onions to the potato and pea mixture.
Add the vegetable oil, crumble in the vegetable stock cube, and season with salt and black pepper.
Mash the mixture thoroughly with a potato masher until well combined and mostly smooth, but with some texture remaining from the peas and sweetcorn.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. If desired, you can add a splash of hot water or vegetable stock to reach your preferred consistency.
Serve the Irio hot as a hearty side dish or light main meal, traditionally with stews or grilled meat.

title: West African Peanut Curry with Chickpeas
steps: Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas thoroughly. Set aside.
Peel the sweet potato and cut into 1-inch cubes. Chop the red onion, mince the garlic, grate the fresh ginger, and dice the red bell pepper.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped red onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the diced red bell pepper and sweet potato cubes to the pot. Stir well and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the curry powder and cook for 1 minute more, stirring constantly to toast the spices.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes to the pot. Crumble in the vegetable stock cube and pour in 400ml of hot water.
Bring the mixture to a simmer. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the sweet potato is tender.
In a small bowl, whisk the peanut butter with a ladleful of the hot curry liquid until smooth. Stir this peanut butter mixture into the pot.
Add the drained chickpeas to the curry. Stir well to combine. Simmer, uncovered, for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired.
Serve the West African Peanut Curry hot with rice, couscous, or flatbread.

title: Easy Lemon Herb Chicken Traybake
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C Fan/Gas Mark 6). Prepare a large baking tray.
Wash and chop the potatoes into roughly 2cm cubes. Peel and chop carrots into similar-sized pieces. Peel and cut the red onion into wedges.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped vegetables. Add half of the olive oil, dried mixed herbs, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Toss well to coat.
Spread the seasoned vegetables evenly on the baking tray.
Pat the chicken thighs dry. In the same bowl, drizzle the remaining olive oil over the chicken. Season with a pinch of salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Arrange the chicken thighs amongst the vegetables on the baking tray, ensuring everything is in a single layer for even cooking.
Bake for 30 minutes. Remove from the oven, flip the chicken and stir the vegetables gently.
Return to the oven for another 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through with no pink showing and the vegetables are tender and slightly caramelised.
Squeeze a little more fresh lemon juice over the traybake before serving.

title: Creamy Chicken & Spinach Curry
steps: Dice the chicken breast into 2cm pieces. Finely chop the onion. Mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and turmeric. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.
Add the diced chicken to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in the canned chopped tomatoes and half of the light coconut milk. Season with salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
While the curry simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Stir in the fresh spinach a handful at a time, allowing it to wilt before adding more. Cook until all the spinach has wilted into the sauce.
Check the seasoning and adjust if necessary. Serve the creamy chicken and spinach curry hot with the cooked basmati rice.

title: Speedy Chicken Chow Mein
steps: Cook the egg noodles according to package instructions. Drain, rinse with cold water to prevent sticking, and set aside.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips. Shred the carrots and cabbage. Finely chop the spring onions, separating whites from greens. Mince the garlic and ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil to create the chow mein sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat until shimmering. Add the chicken strips and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic, ginger, and white parts of the spring onions to the pan. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the shredded carrots and cabbage to the wok. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are slightly softened but still have a bite.
Add the cooked noodles to the wok, along with the prepared chow mein sauce. Toss everything together vigorously for 2-3 minutes, ensuring the noodles and ingredients are well coated.
Stir in the green parts of the spring onions just before serving. Serve immediately.

title: Sheet Pan Chicken Fajita Bowls
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C Fan/Gas Mark 6). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper for easy cleanup.
Slice the chicken breast into thin strips. Slice the bell peppers and red onion into similar-sized strips.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken, bell peppers, and red onion. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle generously with the fajita seasoning mix.
Toss all the ingredients together until everything is well coated with the seasoning.
Spread the chicken and vegetable mixture in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Ensure not to overcrowd the pan, use two if necessary.
Roast for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway through, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender-crisp and slightly charred.
While the fajita mixture cooks, prepare your preferred serving accompaniments, such as cooking rice.
Remove from the oven, squeeze fresh lime juice over the top, and sprinkle with chopped fresh coriander if desired.
Serve the chicken fajita mixture immediately in bowls, perhaps alongside rice, salsa, or guacamole.

title: One-Pan Creamy Chicken Pesto Pasta
steps: Dice the chicken breast into 2cm cubes. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat olive oil in a large, deep frying pan or pot over medium-high heat. Add the diced chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned on all sides.
Add the minced garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock and add the dry pasta. Stir well to ensure the pasta is submerged.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is al dente and most of the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the cream cheese and pesto until well combined and melted into a creamy sauce.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until they begin to soften.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of hot water or milk.
Serve hot, garnished with a little extra fresh black pepper if desired.

title: Hearty Chicken & Vegetable Stew
steps: Cut the chicken thighs into large chunks. Dice the potatoes, carrots, onion, and celery into roughly 2cm pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Dredge the chicken pieces lightly in flour and brown them in the pan for 3-4 minutes. You may need to do this in batches.
Transfer the browned chicken to the slow cooker pot.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, celery, and potatoes to the slow cooker pot.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and chicken stock. Add the dried thyme and bay leaf. Season with salt and black pepper.
Stir all the ingredients gently to combine.
Cover the slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours, or on high for 3-4 hours, until the chicken is very tender and the vegetables are soft.
Remove the bay leaf before serving. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
Serve the hearty chicken and vegetable stew hot, perhaps with crusty bread.

title: Creamy Tuscan Chicken with Spinach
steps: Slice the chicken breasts horizontally to create thinner cutlets. Pat them dry with paper towels. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Mince the garlic cloves. Chop the sun-dried tomatoes into small pieces.

Heat olive oil in a large frying pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the minced garlic and chopped sun-dried tomatoes to the pan. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken stock and double cream. Bring to a gentle simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Stir in the grated Parmesan cheese until it melts into the sauce and the sauce thickens slightly. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Add the baby spinach to the pan a handful at a time, allowing it to wilt into the sauce before adding more.
Return the cooked chicken cutlets to the pan, nestling them into the creamy spinach sauce. Warm through for 1-2 minutes.
Serve the creamy Tuscan chicken immediately, perhaps with rice, pasta, or crusty bread.

title: Quick Chicken & Veggie Noodles
steps: Slice chicken thighs into thin strips. Chop broccoli into small florets. Julienne the carrot. Slice spring onions thinly.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, and sesame oil to make the sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken strips and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Add minced garlic to the pan and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add broccoli florets and julienned carrots to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Meanwhile, cook instant noodles according to package instructions, discarding the flavour sachets. Drain well.
Add the cooked noodles to the pan with the chicken and vegetables. Pour in the prepared sauce.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until the noodles and ingredients are well coated and heated through.
Stir in the sliced spring onions just before serving. Serve immediately for a quick and satisfying meal.

title: Speedy Chicken Enchiladas (No Rolling)
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C Fan/Gas Mark 5).
Dice the chicken breast into small pieces. Finely chop the onion. Quarter the tortilla wraps.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe frying pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the diced chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tinned chopped tomatoes, drained kidney beans, enchilada seasoning, and water. Bring to a simmer.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Stir well to combine the filling. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Nestle the quartered tortilla wraps into the chicken mixture, pushing them down gently so they are partly submerged in the sauce.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese evenly over the top of the pan.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the sauce is hot.
Serve the speedy chicken enchiladas directly from the pan, perhaps with a dollop of sour cream or salsa.

title: Spicy Peanut Chicken Stir-fry
steps: Slice the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Chop the red bell pepper and onion. Chop broccoli into small florets. Mince the garlic and ginger.
In a bowl, whisk together the peanut butter, soy sauce, honey, lime juice, minced garlic, minced ginger, and chilli flakes (if using). Add 50ml of water to thin the sauce to a pourable consistency.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, red bell pepper, and broccoli to the same pan. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan with the vegetables. Pour the peanut sauce over everything.
Toss continuously for 2-3 minutes, ensuring all ingredients are thoroughly coated in the sauce and heated through. If the sauce becomes too thick, add a splash more water.
Serve the spicy peanut chicken stir-fry hot with freshly cooked rice.

title: Tangy Honey Garlic Chicken Drumsticks (Air Fryer)
steps: Pat the chicken drumsticks dry with kitchen paper. Mince the garlic cloves.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the honey, soy sauce, minced garlic, rice vinegar, and cornflour to create the marinade.
Add the chicken drumsticks to the bowl with the marinade. Toss to ensure all drumsticks are well coated.
Place the marinated chicken drumsticks into the air fryer basket in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd. You may need to cook in batches.
Air fry at 180°C for 20 minutes, then flip the drumsticks and cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 74°C and the skin is crispy and golden brown.
While the chicken cooks, steam or boil the green beans and cook the rice according to package instructions.
Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the air fryer. If there's any remaining sauce in the bowl, you can simmer it on the hob for a few minutes to thicken and serve alongside.
Garnish the chicken with sesame seeds if desired. Serve immediately with the green beans and rice.

title: Classic Beef Cottage Pie
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Peel and chop the potatoes into chunks. Boil in salted water for 15-20 minutes until tender. Drain well.
While potatoes boil, heat oil in a large ovenproof pan or casserole dish over medium-high heat.
Add beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned all over. Drain off any excess fat.
Finely chop the onion and carrots. Add to the pan with the mince and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in the plain flour and tomato puree. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Crumble in the beef stock cube and add 400ml of hot water. Bring to a simmer, stirring until the sauce thickens slightly.
Add the frozen peas, season with salt and pepper, and simmer gently for 5 minutes.
Mash the cooked potatoes with milk and butter until smooth. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the mashed potato evenly over the mince mixture, using a fork to create peaks for a crispy topping.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.

title: Lamb Kofta with Mint Yogurt & Couscous
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Line a baking tray with baking paper.
Finely chop the onion and crush the garlic. In a large bowl, combine lamb mince, chopped onion, garlic, cumin, ground coriander, half of the fresh mint, breadcrumbs, egg, salt, and pepper.
Mix thoroughly with your hands until well combined.
Divide the mixture into 16 equal portions. Shape each portion into a small sausage or oval kofta shape.
Place the kofta on the prepared baking tray. Bake for 15-20 minutes, turning halfway, until cooked through and nicely browned.
While kofta cooks, prepare the couscous: Place couscous in a heatproof bowl, pour over 300ml boiling water, cover, and let stand for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
For the mint yogurt: Dice half of the cucumber. In a small bowl, mix natural yogurt, lemon juice, remaining fresh mint, diced cucumber, and a pinch of salt.
Serve the hot lamb kofta immediately with fluffy couscous and a dollop of the refreshing mint yogurt.

title: Hearty Beef Stew with Herby Dumplings
steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3).
Season the diced beef with salt and pepper. Toss with 1 tablespoon of plain flour until lightly coated.
Heat oil in a large ovenproof casserole dish or pot over high heat. Brown the beef in batches until well sealed on all sides, then set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Chop onions, carrots, and celery. Add to the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the remaining plain flour and tomato puree. Cook for 1 minute.
Return the beef to the pot. Crumble in the beef stock cube and add 700ml of hot water. Add the bay leaf and dried thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven. Cook for 1.5 hours.

While the stew cooks, prepare the dumplings: In a bowl, mix self-raising flour, suet, and chopped parsley (if using). Add just enough cold water (approx. 4-5 tbsp) to form a soft, but not sticky, dough. Roll into 8 small balls.
After 1.5 hours, remove the stew from the oven. Peel and chop the potatoes into chunks and add to the stew. Stir well.
Place the dumpling balls on top of the stew. Cover again and return to the oven for another 30-40 minutes, or until the beef is very tender and dumplings are cooked through and fluffy.

title: Lamb Chops with Rosemary & Garlic Roast Potatoes and Green Beans
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Peel the potatoes and cut them into large chunks. Crush 2 garlic cloves. Pick the leaves from one rosemary sprig.
Toss the potato chunks with half the olive oil, crushed garlic, rosemary leaves, salt, and pepper on a baking tray.
Roast the potatoes in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and tender.
While potatoes roast, prepare the lamb chops. Mince the remaining garlic clove. Rub the lamb chops with the minced garlic, remaining rosemary sprig, salt, pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Heat a griddle pan or frying pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the lamb chops.
Cook the lamb chops for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, or longer to your preference. Let them rest for a few minutes after cooking.
Steam or boil the green beans for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Serve the rested lamb chops alongside the crispy rosemary and garlic roast potatoes and steamed green beans.

title: Quick Beef & Black Bean Stir-fry
steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions. Keep warm.
Thinly slice the beef strips if they are too thick. In a small bowl, mix the black bean sauce, soy sauce, and cornflour to create the stir-fry sauce.
Chop the broccoli into small florets, slice the red bell pepper into strips, slice the onion, mince the garlic, and grate the ginger.
Heat a large wok or frying pan over high heat until smoking. Add half of the vegetable oil.
Add the beef strips to the hot pan and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned. Remove beef from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the pan. Add the broccoli, bell pepper, and onion. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the vegetables. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the cooked beef to the pan. Pour in the prepared black bean sauce mixture.
Stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats all the ingredients.
Serve the hot beef and black bean stir-fry immediately with the cooked basmati rice.

title: Slow Cooker Lamb & Lentil Curry
steps: Dice the onion, mince the garlic, and grate the ginger.
Heat a tablespoon of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Brown the diced lamb in batches for 5 minutes until sealed on all sides. Transfer the lamb to the slow cooker pot.
Add the chopped onion to the frying pan (add a little more oil if needed) and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Transfer the onion mixture to the slow cooker pot with the lamb.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, red lentils, vegetable stock, and a pinch of salt to the slow cooker. Stir everything together well.
Cover and cook on LOW for 6-8 hours or on HIGH for 3-4 hours, until the lamb is very tender and the lentils are cooked.
About 15 minutes before serving, stir in the garam masala and the baby spinach. Cook until the spinach has wilted.
While the curry finishes, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the hot lamb and lentil curry with fluffy basmati rice.

title: Smoky Beef Chilli Con Carne

ingredients.name: Tinned Kidney Beans, drained and rinsed

steps: Heat vegetable oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat.
Add beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until thoroughly browned. Drain off any excess fat.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Add to the pan with the cooked mince and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Stir in the chilli powder, ground cumin, and smoked paprika. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly until aromatic.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and crumble in the beef stock cube. Add 150ml of hot water and bring to a simmer.
Add the drained and rinsed kidney beans. Season with salt and pepper.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for at least 30 minutes, or longer if you have time, stirring occasionally. The longer it simmers, the richer the flavour.
While the chilli simmers, cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions.
Taste the chilli and adjust seasoning if necessary. If it's too thick, add a splash more water; if too thin, remove the lid and simmer uncovered for a bit.
Serve the hot smoky beef chilli con carne with the fluffy basmati rice.

title: Lancashire Lamb Hotpot

ingredients.name: Worcestershire Sauce (check for dietary needs if applicable)

steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3).
Season the diced lamb with salt and pepper. Toss with plain flour until lightly coated.
Heat vegetable oil in a large ovenproof casserole dish or pot over medium-high heat. Brown the lamb in batches until sealed on all sides. Remove lamb from the pot and set aside.
Slice the onions and carrots. Add to the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened, stirring occasionally.
Return the lamb to the pot. Add the Worcestershire sauce.
Crumble in the lamb stock cube and pour in 500ml of hot water. Bring to a gentle simmer, scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pan.
Peel the potatoes and slice them thinly (about 3-4mm thick).
Layer the potato slices neatly over the lamb and vegetable mixture, overlapping them slightly to completely cover the top. Season the top layer of potatoes with salt and pepper.
Cover the hotpot with a lid (or foil if your dish doesn't have a lid) and bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour 30 minutes.
Remove the lid/foil and bake for a further 30-45 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and crisp, and the lamb is tender.
Let the hotpot stand for 5-10 minutes before serving. Enjoy hot.

title: Budget Beef Burgers with Sweet Potato Fries
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Wash sweet potatoes and cut them into fry shapes. Toss with half the vegetable oil, salt, and pepper on a baking tray.
Roast the sweet potato fries in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until crispy and golden.
While fries cook, prepare the burger patties: In a bowl, season the beef mince generously with salt and pepper. Mix well with your hands.
Divide the mince into two equal portions and shape each into a firm, flat patty slightly wider than your burger buns (they will shrink during cooking).
Heat a grill pan or frying pan over medium-high heat with the remaining vegetable oil. Cook the burger patties for 4-5 minutes per side for medium, or longer to your preference.
While burgers cook, slice the burger buns in half. You can lightly toast them in a toaster or pan for a minute if desired.
Assemble the burgers: Spread ketchup and mustard on the bun halves. Layer with lettuce, tomato, red onion, and the cooked beef patty.
Serve the homemade beef burgers immediately with the hot sweet potato fries on the side.

title: Spiced Lamb Keema with Peas and Rice
steps: Cook the basmati rice according to packet instructions. Keep warm.
Finely chop the onion, mince the garlic, and grate the ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the lamb mince to the pan. Cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it's browned all over and no pink remains.
Stir in the garam masala, turmeric, cumin, and ground coriander. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the spices are aromatic.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Add 50ml of water, season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat, cover the pan, and let it gently simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has reduced and thickened.
Stir in the frozen peas and cook for an additional 3-5 minutes, until the peas are tender and heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve the hot spiced lamb keema with peas alongside the fluffy basmati rice.

title: Caribbean Curry Goat with Rice
steps: In a large bowl, toss the diced goat shoulder with half of the curry powder, salt, and pepper. Marinate for at least 30 minutes, or overnight in the fridge.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or hob-safe slow cooker pot over medium-high heat. Brown the marinated goat meat in batches until sealed on all sides, then set aside.
Add chopped onion to the pan and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, and the remaining curry powder. Cook for 2 more minutes until fragrant.
If using a scotch bonnet, carefully add it whole to the pan (do not break it) for flavour, or finely chop it for more heat.
Return the browned goat meat to the pan. Add the chopped potatoes and carrots, coconut milk, and chicken or vegetable stock.
Bring the mixture to a simmer on the hob, then transfer everything to the slow cooker if not already using a hob-safe slow cooker pot.
Cook on low for 6-8 hours or on high for 3-4 hours, or simmer gently on the hob for 2.5-3 hours, until the goat meat is very tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the scotch bonnet chilli carefully before serving if used whole. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the rich and tender Caribbean curry goat hot with fluffy basmati rice.

title: Moroccan Goat and Chickpea Tagine
steps: Preheat your oven to 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3). Heat half of the olive oil in a large oven-safe casserole dish or tagine over medium-high heat.
Brown the diced goat meat in batches until well sealed on all sides, then remove and set aside.
Add the remaining olive oil to the dish. Sauté the chopped onion for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated fresh ginger. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the ras el hanout, ground cumin, ground coriander, and turmeric powder. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Return the browned goat meat to the dish. Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and vegetable stock.
Add the drained chickpeas and chopped dried apricots. Stir everything together well, scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer on the hob. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Cover the casserole dish with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Cook for 2.5 to 3 hours, or until the goat meat is exceptionally tender.
Prepare the couscous according to package instructions while the tagine finishes cooking. Fluff with a fork.
Once cooked, carefully remove the tagine from the oven. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Serve the rich Moroccan goat and chickpea tagine hot, garnished with fresh coriander, alongside fluffy couscous.

title: Mexican Birria-style Goat Stew
steps: Remove stems and seeds from dried chillies. Place them in a bowl, cover with boiling water, and let rehydrate for 20 minutes.
While chillies soak, dice the goat meat into large chunks. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the goat meat in batches until well-sealed on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Drain the rehydrated chillies, reserving some of the soaking liquid. In a blender, combine the chillies, half of the onion, garlic, tinned tomatoes, and a splash of stock until smooth.
Strain the chilli mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the blender to remove any skin or seeds, pressing down with a spoon. Discard solids. This is your birria sauce base.

Return the browned goat meat to the pot. Pour the strained birria sauce over the meat. Add the remaining beef or chicken stock, apple cider vinegar, ground cumin, dried oregano, bay leaves, and cinnamon stick.
Bring the stew to a gentle simmer. Cover the pot with a lid and reduce the heat to low. Cook for 3 to 4 hours, or until the goat meat is incredibly tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Once cooked, carefully remove the bay leaves and cinnamon stick. Take out the goat meat and shred it using two forks.
Return the shredded goat meat to the pot with the sauce. Stir well to combine. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Serve the birria-style goat stew hot. You can serve it as a hearty stew or use the shredded meat and consommé for making delicious tacos. Warm corn tortillas and offer lime wedges for serving.

title: Indian Saag Gosht (Goat with Spinach Curry)
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the diced goat meat in batches until sealed on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the finely chopped onions to the pot and sauté for 10-12 minutes until deeply golden brown, stirring frequently.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated fresh ginger, and finely chopped green chillies. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder, and red chilli powder. Stir constantly for 1 minute to toast the spices, adding a splash of water if needed to prevent burning.
Return the browned goat meat to the pot. Add the tinned chopped tomatoes and stir well to combine. Cook for 5 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down.
Add the fresh spinach in batches, stirring until each batch wilts down before adding more. Once all spinach is added and wilted, pour in the hot water or stock.
Bring the curry to a gentle simmer. Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 2 to 2.5 hours, or until the goat meat is very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Once the goat is tender, stir in the garam masala and lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
While the curry finishes, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the rich and vibrant Indian saag gosht hot with fluffy basmati rice or warm naan bread.

title: Slow Cooker Pulled Goat Baps
steps: In a small bowl, combine the brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, mustard powder, salt, and black pepper to create a dry rub.
Pat the goat shoulder dry with kitchen paper. Generously rub the spice mixture all over the goat meat, ensuring it's fully coated.
If desired, quickly sear the goat shoulder in a hot pan with a little oil for 2-3 minutes per side to develop some colour, then transfer to the slow cooker insert.
Scatter the chopped onion and minced garlic around the goat meat in the slow cooker.
Pour in the apple cider vinegar and chicken or vegetable stock. Do not submerge the meat fully; the liquid should come up about halfway.
Cover the slow cooker with its lid. Cook on low for 6-8 hours or on high for 3-4 hours, until the goat meat is incredibly tender and easily pulls apart with a fork.
Once cooked, carefully remove the goat meat from the slow cooker and place it in a large bowl. Using two forks, shred the meat until it's all in small strands.

Skim any excess fat from the cooking liquid in the slow cooker. Stir in the BBQ sauce into the liquid, then add the shredded goat meat back into the slow cooker, tossing to coat evenly with the sauce.
Keep the pulled goat warm in the slow cooker on a 'warm' setting or serve immediately.
Serve the delicious slow cooker pulled goat hot in soft baps or burger buns, topped with a generous spoonful of coleslaw.

title: Rustic Goat and Root Vegetable Pie

ingredients.name: Puff pastry block or ready-rolled sheet

steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Heat the vegetable oil in a large oven-safe casserole dish or heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Brown the diced goat meat in batches until sealed on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, parsnip, and potatoes to the pot. Sauté for 8-10 minutes until slightly softened.
Sprinkle the plain flour over the vegetables and stir well, cooking for 1 minute to cook off the raw flour taste.
Gradually pour in the beef or vegetable stock, stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Return the browned goat meat to the pot. Add the dried thyme and bay leaf. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Cook for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the goat meat is very tender. Alternatively, simmer gently on the hob until tender.
While the filling is cooking, prepare the pastry. If using a block, roll it out on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 3-4mm, slightly larger than your pie dish.
Once the goat filling is tender, remove the bay leaf. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Pour the filling into an ovenproof pie dish (approx. 20cm diameter and 5cm deep).
Top the pie dish with the puff pastry, trimming any excess and crimping the edges to seal. Make a few small slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
For a golden crust, brush the pastry with an egg wash (beaten egg).
Bake in the preheated oven for 30-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed up.
Let the pie rest for 10 minutes before serving. Serve hot with a side of green vegetables.

title: Middle Eastern Goat Kofta with Herbed Rice

ingredients.name: Extra fresh parsley and mint (for rice)

steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Line a baking tray with baking paper.
Finely chop the onion, mince the garlic, and finely chop the fresh parsley and mint for the kofta mixture.
In a large bowl, combine the goat mince, chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, chopped mint, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, egg, salt, and black pepper.
Mix thoroughly with your hands until all ingredients are well combined. Do not overmix.
Shape the mixture into elongated sausage-like shapes (kofta) about 7-8 cm long. You should get approximately 12-16 kofta from this mixture.
Arrange the kofta on the prepared baking tray, leaving a little space between each one. Drizzle lightly with olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway through, until the kofta are cooked through and nicely browned on all sides.
While the kofta are baking, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions. Once cooked, finely chop additional fresh parsley and mint.
Stir the fresh herbs through the cooked rice to make a fragrant herbed rice.
Serve the hot goat kofta with the herbed rice, and optionally with a simple yoghurt dip or a fresh salad.

title: Roast Leg of Goat with Rosemary & Garlic
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6).
Pat the leg of goat dry with kitchen paper. Using a sharp knife, score the skin in a criss-cross pattern, being careful not to cut too deep into the meat.

Make small incisions all over the leg with the tip of a knife. Roughly chop half of the fresh rosemary and thinly slice the garlic cloves. Stuff the rosemary pieces and garlic slices into the incisions.
Rub the entire leg of goat generously with olive oil, then season all over with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Place the seasoned goat leg in a large roasting tin. Arrange the peeled and chopped potatoes, carrots, and quartered onions around the goat in the tin. Toss the vegetables with a little olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast the goat in the preheated oven for 20 minutes at the higher temperature to get a good crust.

Reduce the oven temperature to 160°C (140°C fan/Gas Mark 3). Continue roasting for approximately 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 60-65°C for medium-rare (it will rise during resting), or 70°C for medium.
During the last 30 minutes of cooking, you can add the remaining fresh rosemary sprigs to the roasting tin for extra aroma.

Once cooked to your liking, remove the goat leg from the oven and transfer it to a carving board. Cover loosely with foil and let it rest for at least 15-20 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute.
While the goat rests, return the vegetables to the oven for 10-15 minutes if they need to crisp up further.
Carve the rested goat leg into slices and serve immediately with the roasted root vegetables and any pan juices.

title: Thai-Inspired Goat Massaman Curry

ingredients.name: Massaman curry paste (check for no dairy/nuts)

steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the diced goat meat in batches until sealed on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the Massaman curry paste to the pot and fry for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant and a film of oil separates.
Pour in half of the coconut milk. Stir well, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Bring to a gentle simmer, allowing the coconut milk to thicken slightly.
