Return the browned goat meat to the pot. Add the remaining coconut milk, vegetable stock, chopped potatoes, and sliced onion.

Bring the curry to a gentle simmer. Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 2 to 2.5 hours, or until the goat meat is very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.
Once the goat meat is tender, stir in the fish sauce, brown sugar (or palm sugar), and lime juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more salt, sugar, or lime if required.
While the curry finishes, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the rich and aromatic Thai-inspired Massaman goat curry hot with fluffy basmati rice, garnished with fresh coriander.

title: East African Goat Pilau
steps: Wash the basmati rice thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Soak the rice in fresh water for 20-30 minutes, then drain well.

Heat the vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced onions and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently, until they are deeply golden brown and caramelised. This is crucial for pilau flavour.
Add the minced garlic and grated fresh ginger to the pot. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the pilau masala spice blend. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.
Add the diced goat meat to the pot. Brown the goat meat on all sides for 5-7 minutes, ensuring it gets a good sear and absorbs the spice flavours.
Add the chopped fresh tomatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they break down and release their juices.

Pour in the water or goat stock and bring the mixture to a boil. Season generously with salt. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until the goat meat is about three-quarters cooked.
Increase the heat to medium-high. Add the drained soaked rice to the pot and stir gently to combine everything without breaking the rice grains. Ensure the liquid covers the rice completely.

Bring the mixture back to a rapid boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to the lowest setting, cover the pot tightly with a lid, and cook undisturbed for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it rest, covered, for another 10 minutes. Do not lift the lid during this time.
Fluff the pilau gently with a fork. Garnish with fresh coriander before serving. Serve hot with kachumbari (fresh tomato and onion salad) or plain yoghurt.

title: Spicy Goan-style Goat Vindaloo

ingredients.name: Dried red chillies (whole, mild-hot to taste)

steps: For the vindaloo paste: In a small frying pan, lightly toast the dried red chillies, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, black peppercorns, cloves, and cardamom pods until fragrant (about 1-2 minutes). Let cool.

In a blender or food processor, combine the toasted spices, minced garlic, grated fresh ginger, turmeric powder, and white vinegar. Blend until you have a smooth, thick paste, adding a tiny splash of water if needed.
Place the diced goat meat in a bowl, add the prepared vindaloo paste, and mix thoroughly to coat all the meat. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or ideally overnight.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the finely chopped onions and cook for 8-10 minutes until deeply golden brown and softened.
Add the marinated goat meat to the pot, including any remaining paste. Sear the goat meat on all sides for 5-7 minutes until it's browned and the spices are fragrant.
Pour in the hot water or stock. Bring the curry to a gentle simmer. Season with salt.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and cook for 2.5 to 3 hours, or until the goat meat is incredibly tender and easily falls apart. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
If the sauce becomes too thick during cooking, add a little more hot water or stock. If it's too thin at the end, remove the lid and simmer uncovered for a further 10-15 minutes to reduce.
While the curry is simmering, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Taste the vindaloo and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve the spicy Goan-style goat vindaloo hot with fluffy basmati rice and a cooling raita or plain yoghurt (if not dairy-free).

title: Creamy Smoked Mackerel & Potato Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
Peel the potatoes and cut them into 1.5 cm cubes. Boil them in salted water for 10-12 minutes until tender but still firm. Drain well.
Flake the smoked mackerel, checking carefully for any small bones, and set it aside.
Chop the onion and finely mince the garlic cloves. Sauté the onion in vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Stir the plain flour into the onion mixture and cook for 1 minute, stirring continuously to form a roux.
Gradually whisk in the oat milk until the sauce thickens and is smooth. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Gently fold the flaked mackerel, boiled potatoes, and frozen peas into the creamy sauce.
Transfer the mixture to an ovenproof dish. Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until bubbling and lightly golden on top.
Allow the bake to rest for a few minutes before serving. Enjoy this comforting and flavourful meal.
Serve hot, perhaps with a simple side salad for extra freshness and crunch.

title: Quick Sardine & Tomato Linguine
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil for the linguine.
While the water heats, finely mince the garlic cloves.
Heat a tablespoon of olive oil (you can use some from the sardine tin) in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and chilli flakes to the pan, cooking for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and cook for 8-10 minutes, allowing it to reduce slightly and deepen in flavour.
Add the linguine to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
Drain the tinned sardines, reserving a tiny bit of the oil if you wish. Gently flake the sardines into the simmering tomato sauce.
Season the sauce with salt and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
Once the pasta is cooked, drain it, reserving about half a cup of the pasta water.
Add the drained linguine directly into the sardine and tomato sauce. Toss well to combine.
If the sauce seems too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Garnish with freshly chopped parsley, if using, and serve immediately for a delicious and speedy meal.

title: Budget White Fish & Spinach Curry

ingredients.name: Frozen White Fish Fillets (e.g., Pollock, Haddock)

steps: Start by cooking the basmati rice according to package instructions. You can use a hob or microwave.
While the rice cooks, finely chop the onion. Mince the garlic and grate or finely chop the ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until it softens and turns translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and ginger, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the curry powder to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to toast the spices.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and bring the mixture to a simmer. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in the light coconut milk and season with salt. Bring the curry sauce back to a gentle simmer.
Add the frozen white fish fillets directly into the simmering sauce. Cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily.
Stir in the frozen spinach and cook for 2-3 minutes until it has wilted completely into the curry.
Taste the curry and adjust the seasoning if necessary. If desired, garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve the hot fish and spinach curry immediately with the cooked basmati rice.

title: Lemon & Herb Crusted White Fish

ingredients.name: Frozen White Fish Fillets (e.g., Cod, Haddock)

steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Line a baking tray with parchment paper.
If using frozen fish, ensure it is fully defrosted and patted dry with kitchen paper.
In a small bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, dried mixed herbs, zest of half a lemon, a pinch of salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Place the fish fillets on the prepared baking tray. Drizzle a little olive oil over each fillet.
Press the breadcrumb mixture firmly onto the top of each fish fillet, creating an even crust.
Meanwhile, boil the baby potatoes for 10-15 minutes until tender. Steam or boil the green beans until crisp-tender.
Bake the crusted fish in the preheated oven for 15-18 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the crust is golden brown and crispy.
While the fish bakes, slice the remaining lemon into wedges for serving.
Once cooked, carefully remove the fish from the oven.
Serve the lemon and herb crusted white fish immediately with the boiled baby potatoes and steamed green beans, and a fresh lemon wedge.

title: Spicy Mackerel & Veg Rice Bowl
steps: If using pre-cooked rice, heat it gently in a microwave or a pan. If cooking fresh rice, do so now.
Open the tin of mackerel in chilli oil. You can drain a little oil if you prefer less heat, or include it all for a spicier kick.
Heat a large non-stick pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add the frozen mixed vegetables directly to the hot pan.
Stir-fry the vegetables for 5-7 minutes until they are tender-crisp and heated through.
Add the hot cooked rice to the pan with the vegetables. Break up any clumps of rice and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Gently flake the tinned mackerel (with its chilli oil) into the rice and vegetables. Stir to combine, being careful not to break up the fish too much.
Pour in the soy sauce and mix everything well. Continue to stir-fry for another 2 minutes until everything is piping hot.
Slice the spring onion finely. If using, cut the lime into wedges.
Divide the spicy mackerel rice between two bowls.
Garnish each bowl generously with the sliced spring onion. Serve immediately with a lime wedge on the side for squeezing, if desired.

title: Air Fryer Fish Goujons & Wedges
steps: Defrost the white fish fillets and pat them thoroughly dry with kitchen paper. Cut each fillet into 2-3 cm wide strips (goujons).
Wash the potatoes well and cut them into evenly sized wedges. Par-boil the wedges for 5 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
In a bowl, mix the breadcrumbs with smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Set up a dredging station: one bowl with plain flour, one bowl with a beaten egg, and one bowl with the seasoned breadcrumbs.
Lightly coat each fish goujon in flour, then dip in beaten egg, and finally roll in the seasoned breadcrumbs, pressing firmly to adhere.
Spray the air fryer basket with a little oil. Arrange the potato wedges in a single layer in the air fryer, spray with oil, and cook at 200°C for 15 minutes, flipping halfway.
After 15 minutes, add the breaded fish goujons to the air fryer basket alongside the wedges (you might need to do this in batches if your air fryer is small). Spray the goujons with oil.
Continue to cook at 200°C for another 8-10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through, the crust is golden, and the wedges are crispy, shaking the basket occasionally.
Ensure the fish is flaky and cooked through before serving. The internal temperature should reach 63°C.
Serve the hot fish goujons and potato wedges with your favourite dipping sauce, such as ketchup or tartar sauce.

title: Sardine & Chickpea Power Salad with Pitta

ingredients.name: Tinned Sardines in Brine or Water

steps: Drain the tinned sardines thoroughly and gently flake them into a medium-sized mixing bowl.
Drain and rinse the tinned chickpeas well under cold water. Add them to the bowl with the sardines.
Dice the cucumber and bell pepper into small, bite-sized pieces. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Finely slice the red onion.
Add all the chopped vegetables to the bowl with the sardines and chickpeas.
In a small separate bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to create a simple dressing.
Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients in the bowl. Gently toss everything together until evenly coated.
Warm the pitta breads according to package instructions (toaster, grill, or microwave) or serve them fresh.
Taste the salad and adjust the seasoning if necessary, adding more salt or pepper to your preference.
Serve the sardine and chickpea power salad immediately with the warm pitta breads on the side for scooping.
This refreshing and protein-packed meal is perfect for a light lunch or dinner, offering a burst of Mediterranean flavours.

title: Speedy Smoked Mackerel Paté with Toast

ingredients.name: Fresh Chives (or spring onion green part)

steps: Ensure the smoked mackerel fillets have their skin removed. Check carefully for any small bones and discard them.
Place the mackerel fillets into a food processor or blender. If you don't have one, you can mash it well with a fork.
Add the soft cheese to the food processor with the mackerel.
Squeeze the juice of half a lemon into the mixture. You can also add a little lemon zest for extra flavour.
Finely chop the fresh chives (or the green part of a spring onion) and add them to the processor.
Season with a generous grinding of black pepper. Salt might not be needed as mackerel is already salty, but taste first.
Blend all the ingredients until smooth and creamy. If you're mashing by hand, keep mashing until well combined.
Taste the paté and adjust the seasoning as needed, adding more lemon juice, pepper, or a pinch of salt if required.
Transfer the mackerel paté to a serving dish. Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 15-20 minutes to allow the flavours to meld.
While the paté chills, toast your bread until golden and crispy. Cut the toast into triangles or fingers.
Serve the chilled smoked mackerel paté generously on the warm toast. It's also delicious with cucumber sticks or crackers.

title: One-Pan White Fish & Veg Tray Bake

ingredients.name: Frozen White Fish Fillets (e.g., Pangasius, Coley)

steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Line a large baking tray with parchment paper for easy clean-up.
Wash the potatoes and cut them into 2cm chunks (no need to peel small or new potatoes). Peel the carrots and cut them into similar-sized chunks.
Chop the red onion into wedges. If using fresh broccoli, cut it into small florets. If using frozen broccoli, add it later.
On the prepared baking tray, toss the potatoes, carrots, and red onion with half the olive oil, garlic powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Roast these vegetables in the preheated oven for 15 minutes to give them a head start.
While the vegetables are roasting, ensure your white fish fillets are fully defrosted and patted dry.
Remove the tray from the oven. Arrange the broccoli florets (fresh or frozen) around the partially cooked vegetables.
Place the fish fillets on top of the vegetables. Drizzle the remaining olive oil over the fish and season with a little extra salt and pepper.
Slice the lemon into thin wedges and place a few slices on top of each fish fillet.
Return the tray to the oven and cook for another 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily and the vegetables are tender.
Serve the hot one-pan white fish and vegetable tray bake directly from the tray. Enjoy this simple and healthy meal.

title: Spicy Thai-Inspired Fish Soup

ingredients.name: Frozen Mixed Vegetables (e.g., peas, sweetcorn, carrots)
Soy Sauce (or fish sauce for authentic flavour)

steps: Ensure the white fish fillets are fully defrosted and pat them dry. Cut the fillets into 2-3 cm chunks.
Finely mince the garlic cloves and grate or finely chop the fresh ginger.
Heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the red curry paste and cook for another minute, stirring continuously, to release its aromatic flavours.
Pour in the vegetable stock and light coconut milk. Bring the soup to a gentle simmer, stirring well to combine the curry paste.
Add the frozen mixed vegetables to the simmering soup. Cook for 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are almost tender.
Carefully add the chunks of white fish to the soup. Simmer gently for 5-7 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily.
Squeeze in the juice of half a lime and stir in the soy sauce (or fish sauce). Taste the soup and adjust seasoning as needed.
If using, finely chop the fresh coriander for garnish.
Ladle the hot spicy Thai-inspired fish soup into bowls. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve immediately with the remaining lime wedges.

title: Quick Shakshuka with Feta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a medium frying pan or skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and chopped red pepper, cook for another 5 minutes.
Add the tinned chopped tomatoes, tomato puree, cumin, paprika, chilli flakes, salt, and pepper. Stir well.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Make two small wells in the sauce. Carefully crack an egg into each well.
Cover the pan and cook for 7-10 minutes, or until the egg whites are set and the yolks are still runny (or to your preference).
Crumble the feta cheese over the shakshuka just before serving. Garnish with fresh parsley if using.
Serve hot, optionally with crusty bread for dipping.

title: Budget Egg Fried Rice with Veg
steps: If not using leftover cold rice, cook 100g of dry rice according to packet instructions, then spread it out to cool completely.
Whisk the two eggs with a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat.
Pour in the whisked eggs and quickly scramble them, breaking them into small pieces. Remove the eggs from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining vegetable oil to the pan. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the mixed frozen vegetables and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add the cold cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, ensuring the rice heats through and gets slightly crispy.
Return the scrambled eggs to the pan. Pour in the soy sauce and a dash of sesame oil (if using).
Stir-fry everything together for another 1-2 minutes until well combined and heated through.
Garnish with chopped spring onion before serving immediately.

title: Oven-Baked Green Veggie Frittata
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Chop the broccoli into small florets. Steam or boil the broccoli for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp. Drain well.
Chop the onion and mince the garlic. Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe, non-stick frying pan (approx. 20-22cm) over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and spinach, cook for another 2-3 minutes until the spinach wilts.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs with milk, salt, and pepper until well combined. Stir in the steamed broccoli and half of the grated cheddar cheese.
Add the cooked onion, garlic, and spinach mixture to the egg mixture, stirring gently.
Pour the entire mixture back into the oven-safe frying pan. Ensure the vegetables are evenly distributed.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheddar cheese over the top of the frittata.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the frittata is set and golden brown on top.
Let it cool slightly before slicing into wedges and serving.

title: Speedy Spicy Scrambled Egg Wraps
steps: Finely chop the red onion and red chilli (remove seeds for less heat if desired).
Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped red onion and chilli, cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the chopped tinned tomatoes to the pan and cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring, until slightly reduced.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs with salt and pepper.
Pour the whisked eggs into the pan with the vegetables. Scramble the eggs gently until just set but still moist.
Warm the tortilla wraps according to packet instructions (microwave for 10-20 seconds or dry fry in a pan for 30 seconds per side).
Spoon the spicy scrambled egg mixture onto the centre of each warm tortilla.
Sprinkle with grated cheddar cheese and optional fresh coriander.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla and then roll up tightly to create a wrap. Serve immediately.

title: Curried Egg and Chickpea Scramble (Bhurji)
steps: Chop the onion finely. Mince the garlic and ginger. Slice the green chilli.
Heat the vegetable oil in a medium frying pan over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the curry powder and turmeric. Cook for another minute, stirring continuously to prevent burning.
Add the drained chickpeas to the pan, stir well to coat them in the spices, and cook for 3-4 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs with salt. Pour the whisked eggs over the chickpea mixture in the pan.
Stir gently as the eggs begin to set, scrambling them until cooked through but still moist.
Stir in half of the fresh coriander.
Serve hot, garnished with the remaining fresh coriander. Great with rice or flatbread.

title: Cheesy Baked Egg & Veggie Muffin Cups

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil spray or butter for greasing

steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4). Lightly grease a 12-hole muffin tin with oil spray or butter.
Finely chop the onion. If using large frozen veg, chop them smaller.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs with milk, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Stir in the mixed frozen vegetables, chopped onion, and half of the grated cheddar cheese into the egg mixture.
Pour the egg mixture evenly into the prepared muffin holes, filling each about two-thirds full.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheddar cheese over the top of each egg cup.
Bake for 18-22 minutes, or until the egg cups are set in the centre and lightly golden on top.
Remove from the oven and let them cool in the muffin tin for a few minutes before carefully removing them.
Serve warm or let them cool completely and store for meal prep.

title: Classic Mushroom and Cheese Omelette
steps: Slice the mushrooms thinly.
Heat half of the butter in a small non-stick frying pan (approx. 20cm) over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 5-7 minutes until they release their liquid and start to brown. Remove mushrooms from the pan and set aside.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs with milk (if using), salt, and pepper until light and frothy.
Add the remaining butter to the same frying pan over medium-high heat. Once the butter is melted and sizzling, pour in the egg mixture.
Let the eggs cook for about 30 seconds without stirring, allowing the edges to set.

Using a spatula, gently push the cooked egg from the edges towards the centre, tilting the pan to allow uncooked egg to flow underneath. Repeat until most of the egg is set but still slightly runny on top.
Spoon the cooked mushrooms and grated cheddar cheese over one half of the omelette.
Fold the other half of the omelette over the filling. Cook for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until the cheese is melted.
Slide the omelette onto a plate, garnish with fresh chives if desired, and serve immediately.

title: Quick and Easy Egg Drop Soup
steps: Bring the vegetable stock to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
In a small bowl, whisk the cornflour with 30ml of cold water to create a smooth slurry.
Slowly pour the cornflour slurry into the simmering stock while stirring continuously, until the soup slightly thickens.
In another small bowl, lightly whisk the eggs with a pinch of salt and a dash of white pepper.
Once the stock is simmering again, slowly drizzle the whisked eggs into the soup in a thin stream, stirring gently in one direction with a fork or chopsticks. This will create delicate egg 'ribbons'.
Stir in the soy sauce and sesame oil. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Garnish with finely chopped spring onion just before serving.
Serve hot as a light meal or a starter.

title: Speedy Kedgeree-style Eggs & Rice (Meat-Free)
steps: Boil the eggs for 7-8 minutes for a firm yolk, then plunge into cold water, peel, and quarter them.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder and turmeric, cook for 1 minute, stirring continuously.
Add the cooked cold rice and frozen peas to the pan. Stir well to combine and heat through, breaking up any clumps of rice. Cook for 5-7 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Gently fold in the quartered hard-boiled eggs just before serving, or arrange them on top.
Garnish with fresh parsley if using. Serve hot.

title: Hearty Lentil Shepherd's Pie
steps: Peel and chop potatoes into even pieces. Boil in salted water until very tender (about 15-20 minutes). Drain well.
While potatoes cook, finely chop onions, carrots, and celery. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat vegetable oil in a large ovenproof pan or casserole dish over medium heat. Sauté chopped onions, carrots, and celery for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Add minced garlic and dried thyme to the pan, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in red lentils, chopped tomatoes, tomato puree, vegetable stock, and vegetarian Worcestershire sauce. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are tender and the sauce has thickened. Season with salt and pepper.
Mash the drained potatoes with milk and butter until smooth and creamy. Season the mashed potato to taste.
Spoon the mashed potato evenly over the lentil mixture in the ovenproof dish. If using, sprinkle with grated cheddar cheese.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C (Fan 160°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and bubbling.
Remove from oven and let stand for a few minutes before serving hot.

title: Quick Chickpea and Spinach Curry
steps: Cook basmati rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
Finely chop the onion. Mince the garlic cloves and grate the fresh ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for about 5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add curry powder, cumin powder, and turmeric powder to the pan. Cook for another minute, stirring constantly to toast the spices.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and coconut milk. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Drain and rinse the canned chickpeas, then add them to the curry. Cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Stir in the fresh spinach a handful at a time until it wilts completely into the sauce.
Season the curry with salt and pepper to taste. Chop fresh coriander for garnish.
Serve the hot chickpea and spinach curry with the cooked basmati rice, garnished with fresh coriander.

title: Hearty Vegetable and Bean Chilli
steps: Peel and dice the sweet potato into 1-2cm cubes. Dice the red pepper and onion. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or deep pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add diced sweet potato and red pepper to the pot. Cook for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in minced garlic, chilli powder, cumin powder, and smoked paprika. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes, tomato puree, and vegetable stock. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Drain and rinse the kidney beans and black beans. Add them to the pot with the vegetables.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for at least 25-30 minutes, or until the sweet potato is tender and the sauce has thickened. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Season the chilli with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice.
While the chilli simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the hot vegetable and bean chilli with rice and garnish generously with chopped fresh coriander.

title: Creamy Mushroom and Leek Risotto
steps: Heat the vegetable stock in a separate saucepan and keep it at a gentle simmer.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Slice the leeks thinly and rinse them well. Slice the mushrooms.
In a large, heavy-bottomed pan, melt half the butter with the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add minced garlic and sliced leeks to the pan, cook for another 3-4 minutes until they have softened.
Stir in the Arborio rice, ensuring all grains are coated in the oil and butter mixture. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the edges of the rice become translucent.
Add a ladleful of hot vegetable stock to the rice, stirring constantly until almost all the liquid has been absorbed.
Continue adding the stock, one ladleful at a time, stirring continuously and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding the next. This process will take about 20-25 minutes.
When about halfway through the stock additions, add the sliced mushrooms to the risotto.
The risotto is ready when the rice is al dente (cooked through but still with a slight bite) and the consistency is creamy. You may not need all the stock, or you might need a little more water.
Remove the pan from the heat. Stir in the remaining butter and grated Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.

title: Roasted Vegetable and Halloumi Couscous
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (Fan 180°C).
Chop courgette, red pepper, and red onion into bite-sized pieces. Halve the cherry tomatoes.
In a large bowl, toss the chopped vegetables with 20ml of olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Spread them evenly on a baking tray.
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly charred.
While vegetables roast, cook the giant couscous: bring vegetable stock to a boil in a saucepan. Add couscous, reduce heat, and simmer for 8-10 minutes until tender. Drain any excess liquid.
Slice the halloumi into 1cm thick pieces. In a non-stick frying pan, heat 10ml olive oil over medium-high heat. Cook halloumi slices for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked giant couscous and the roasted vegetables.
Squeeze the juice from half a lemon over the couscous mixture. Add the remaining 10ml olive oil and chopped fresh parsley. Toss gently to combine.
Serve the couscous mixture topped with the grilled halloumi slices.

title: Sweet Potato and Black Bean Burgers
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (Fan 180°C). Pierce the sweet potato several times with a fork. Bake for 30-40 minutes until very tender. Alternatively, microwave for 8-10 minutes until soft.
Once cooked, scoop out the sweet potato flesh into a large bowl and mash well.
Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic cloves.
Heat a small amount of oil in a pan over medium heat. Sauté chopped onion and minced garlic for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Drain and rinse the black beans. Add them to the sweet potato along with the sautéed onion and garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, and chopped fresh coriander.
Mash the black beans slightly with a fork, leaving some texture. Mix all ingredients well until combined.
Add the breadcrumbs to the mixture and mix thoroughly until it can be formed into patties. If the mixture is too wet, add a little more breadcrumbs.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and shape them into burger patties.

Heat the remaining oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat. Cook the burgers for 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and heated through. Alternatively, bake in the oven for 15-20 minutes at 180°C (Fan 160°C).
Serve the burgers in buns with your favourite toppings such as salad leaves, tomato slices, and red onion.

title: Creamy Tomato Pasta Bake
steps: Preheat oven to 190°C (Fan 170°C).
Cook the penne pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain well and set aside.
While pasta cooks, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic cloves.
Heat vegetable oil in a large ovenproof pan or casserole dish over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add minced garlic and dried mixed herbs to the pan, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and tomato puree. Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing it to thicken slightly.
Stir in the double cream. Season the sauce generously with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the drained cooked pasta to the sauce and toss gently to coat evenly.
Tear the mozzarella ball into pieces and scatter them evenly over the top of the pasta mixture in the dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the mozzarella is melted, golden, and bubbling. Serve hot, optionally with a sprinkle of Parmesan.

title: Hearty Root Vegetable Stew with Herb Dumplings
steps: Peel and chop carrots, potatoes, and swede into 2-3cm chunks. Slice leeks and onion.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or casserole dish over medium heat. Add chopped onions and leeks, cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in chopped carrots, potatoes, and swede. Cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of plain flour over the vegetables and stir to coat. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in chopped tomatoes, tomato puree, vegetable stock, and dried mixed herbs. Bring the stew to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 25-30 minutes, or until the vegetables are almost tender.

While the stew simmers, prepare the dumplings: In a bowl, mix the remaining 50g plain flour, vegetable suet, chopped fresh parsley, a pinch of salt and pepper. Add 3-4 tablespoons of cold water gradually, mixing until a soft, pliable dough forms.
Divide the dough into 8-10 small balls.
Remove the lid from the stew. Carefully place the dumpling balls gently on top of the simmering stew.
Cover the pot tightly again and cook for a further 15-20 minutes, or until the dumplings have doubled in size and are fluffy. Serve the hearty stew and dumplings hot.

title: Tofu & Vegetable Stir-fry with Noodles
steps: Press the tofu to remove excess water, then cut into 2cm cubes. In a bowl, toss the tofu cubes with 1 tbsp of cornflour until lightly coated.
Prepare vegetables: Chop broccoli into florets. Thinly slice carrots and bell pepper into strips. Slice spring onions (separate white and green parts). Mince garlic and grate fresh ginger.
Cook rice noodles according to package instructions (usually soaking in hot water or boiling briefly). Drain and set aside.

Heat 15ml vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the coated tofu and fry until golden and crispy on all sides, about 8-10 minutes. Remove tofu from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining 15ml vegetable oil to the wok. Add broccoli, carrots, and bell pepper. Stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and the white parts of the spring onions. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, and rice vinegar. Pour the sauce over the vegetables in the wok. Bring to a simmer.
Return the cooked tofu to the wok. Add the cooked rice noodles and toss everything together to coat with the sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Garnish with the green parts of the spring onions before serving the hot stir-fry.

title: Lentil Curry
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion, garlic, and ginger, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add curry powder and cook for 1 minute
Add lentils, coconut milk, salt, and pepper, and stir well
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes

title: Chickpea and Spinach Stew
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion and garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add chickpeas, spinach, and diced tomatoes, and stir well
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes
Season with salt and pepper to taste

title: Tofu Stir Fry
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat
Add tofu and cook until golden brown
Add onion and garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add broccoli and cook until tender
Stir in soy sauce, salt, and pepper

title: Black Bean and Sweet Potato Enchiladas
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C
Cook sweet potatoes in the oven for 45 minutes
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion and garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add black beans and cook until heated through
Assemble enchiladas with tortillas, sweet potatoes, and black beans
Cover with enchilada sauce and bake for 20 minutes

title: Lentil Bolognese
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion, garlic, and carrots, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add lentils, diced tomatoes, tomato paste, salt, and pepper, and stir well
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes

title: Chickpea and Quinoa Bowl
steps: Cook quinoa according to package instructions
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion and garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add chickpeas and cook until heated through
Assemble bowls with quinoa, chickpeas, and roasted vegetables
Drizzle with tahini and lemon juice

title: Vegan Sushi Rolls
steps: Prepare sushi rice according to package instructions
Cut cucumber, avocado, and carrots into thin strips
Assemble sushi rolls with norii seaweed sheets, sushi rice, and fillings
Serve with soy sauce and wasabi

title: Tofu and Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat grill to medium-high heat
Thread tofu, onion, garlic, bell peppers, and zucchini onto skewers
Brush with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Grill for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally

title: Chickpea and Spinach Curry
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add onion, garlic, and ginger, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add chickpeas, spinach, and curry powder, and cook until the spinach is wilted
Stir in coconut milk and bring to a simmer
Reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes

title: Roasted Vegetable Medley
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Peel and chop the carrots into bite-sized sticks.
Trim and halve the Brussels sprouts.
In a bowl, toss the carrots and Brussels sprouts with olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the vegetables out in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and lightly browned.

title: Spicy Pumpkin Soup
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the pumpkin, curry powder, and a pinch of salt, and stir to combine.
Pour in enough water to cover the pumpkin, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the pumpkin is tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Stir in the coconut milk, and adjust the seasoning as needed.

title: Grilled Portobello Mushroom Burgers
steps: Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the mixture onto both sides of the mushrooms.
Grill the mushrooms for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they are tender and lightly charred.
Assemble the burgers with lettuce, tomato, and your favorite condiments.

title: Stir-Fried Kale and Garlic
steps: Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add the garlic and ginger, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the kale, and stir-fry until it is tender and slightly wilted.
Season with soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to taste.

title: Roasted Beetroot Salad
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Wrap the beetroot in foil, and roast for 45-50 minutes, or until tender.
Let the beetroot cool, then peel and slice it into wedges.
In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the mixed greens, feta cheese, and walnuts to the bowl, and toss to combine.
Top the salad with the roasted beetroot.

title: Spicy Sweet Potato and Black Bean Tacos
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the sweet potatoes, black beans, cumin, and chili powder, and stir to combine.
Cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos with the sweet potato and black bean mixture, and your favorite toppings.

title: Japanese-Style Stir-Fried Bok Choy
steps: Heat sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add the garlic and ginger, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the bok choy, and stir-fry until it is tender and slightly wilted.
Season with soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to taste.

title: Indian-Style Saag Aloo
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the potatoes, curry powder, and garam masala, and stir to combine.
Cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Add the spinach, and stir-fry until it is wilted.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

title: Thai-Style Green Curry with Vegetables
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the green curry paste, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk, and stir to combine.
Add the bell peppers, and cook until they are tender.
Stir in the Thai basil, salt, and black pepper.
Serve hot over rice or noodles.

title: Middle Eastern-Style Stuffed Bell Peppers
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Cut the tops off the bell peppers, and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a bowl, mix together the cooked rice, black beans, feta cheese, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Stuff each bell pepper with the rice mixture, and place them in a baking dish.
Cover the dish with foil, and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the bell peppers are tender.

title: Black Bean Chilli
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the red bell pepper and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Add the cumin, chilli powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the black beans, tomato paste, and diced tomatoes, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the flavours have melded together and the chilli has thickened slightly.
Serve hot.

title: Red Lentil Dal
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for a further minute.
Add the coriander powder, turmeric powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the red lentils and 400ml of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Serve hot.

title: Butter Bean and Leek Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large pan, sauté the leeks, carrots, and onion in butter until softened.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the milk, stirring to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it thickens.
Stir in the butter beans and cheddar cheese.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top with additional cheese if desired.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Serve hot.

title: Spicy Black Bean Soup
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the red bell pepper and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, chilli powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the black beans, vegetable broth, and 200ml of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the beans are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.

title: Kidney Bean and Sweet Potato Curry
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for a further minute.
Add the coriander powder, turmeric powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the kidney beans, sweet potatoes, and 200ml of water, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Serve hot.

title: Cannellini Bean and Kale Stew
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for a further minute.
Add the cannellini beans, diced tomatoes, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 200ml of water and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the beans are tender.
Stir in the kale and cook until wilted.
Serve hot.

title: Mung Bean and Vegetable Stir-Fry
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for a further minute.
Add the mung beans, broccoli, carrots, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Serve hot.

title: Fava Bean and Spinach Stew
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for a further minute.
Add the fava beans, spinach, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 200ml of water and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the beans are tender.
Serve hot.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned
Add the mixed vegetables, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger
Stir-fry for 10-15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through

title: Roasted Vegetable Tray Bake
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C
Toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder
Spread the vegetables out in a single layer on a baking tray
Roast in the oven for 30-40 minutes or until the vegetables are tender

title: Chicken and Rice One-Pot
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned
Add the uncooked rice, chicken stock, cumin, and paprika
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is cooked

title: Lentil and Vegetable Curry
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the onion and cook until softened
Add the mixed vegetables, lentils, curry powder, and tomato puree
Simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are cooked

title: Baked Chicken Fajitas
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C
Toss the chicken breast, mixed bell peppers, and onion with fajita seasoning
Spread the mixture out in a single layer on a baking tray
Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through

title: Vegetable and Bean Chili
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the onion and cook until softened
Add the mixed vegetables, kidney beans, chili powder, and tomato puree
Simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the vegetables are tender

title: Thai-Style Chicken and Rice
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned
Add the uncooked rice, coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, and Thai red curry paste
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is cooked

title: Japanese-Style Teriyaki Chicken
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned
Add the teriyaki sauce, soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and ginger
Simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through

title: Mediterranean Chicken and Vegetable Tray Bake
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C
Toss the chicken breast, mixed vegetables, olive oil, feta cheese, lemon juice, and oregano
Spread the mixture out in a single layer on a baking tray
Bake in the oven for 25-30 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through

title: Indian-Style Vegetable and Lentil Curry
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the onion and cook until softened
Add the mixed vegetables, lentils, curry powder, and tomato puree
Simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are cooked

title: Spicy Shrimp and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the shrimp from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the chilli peppers to the wok and cook for 1 minute.
Return the shrimp to the wok and stir in the soy sauce and fish sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the shrimp is coated in the sauce.

title: Vegetable and Tofu Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu and cook until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the tofu from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the broccoli to the wok and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Return the tofu to the wok and stir in the soy sauce and grated ginger.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the tofu is coated in the sauce.

title: Chicken Fajita Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the fajita seasoning to the wok and stir to combine.
Return the chicken to the wok and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Serve the chicken and vegetables with tortillas.

title: Beef and Mushroom Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the beef strips and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the beef from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mushrooms to the wok and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Return the beef to the wok and stir in the soy sauce and oyster sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the beef is coated in the sauce.

title: Spinach and Chickpea Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chickpeas to the wok and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the spinach to the wok and cook until wilted, about 1-2 minutes.
Stir in the garam masala and cumin.
Cook for an additional 1 minute, until the flavors are combined.

title: Turkey and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the turkey breast and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the turkey from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the broccoli to the wok and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Return the turkey to the wok and stir in the soy sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the turkey is coated in the sauce.

title: Lamb and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb strips and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the cumin and coriander.
Return the lamb to the wok and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the lamb is coated in the spices.

title: Vegetable and Bean Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the bell pepper to the wok and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the black beans to the wok and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the fajita seasoning.
Cook for an additional 1 minute, until the flavors are combined.
Serve the vegetables and beans with tortillas.

title: Shrimp and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the shrimp from the wok and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Return the shrimp to the wok and stir in the soy sauce and grated ginger.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the shrimp is coated in the sauce.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Stew
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F)
Heat oil in a large oven-safe pot over medium-high heat
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes
Add the carrots, potatoes, onions, and celery, and cook for 5 minutes
Add the diced tomatoes, chicken broth, thyme, rosemary, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven
Bake for 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs

title: Roasted Butternut Squash Soup
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F)
Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds
Place the squash on a baking sheet, cut side up, and drizzle with olive oil
Roast in the preheated oven for 45-50 minutes, or until the squash is tender
Scoop the flesh out of the squash and transfer to a blender
Add the onions, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to the blender
Blend until smooth, then add the vegetable broth and blend until combined
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs

title: Thai Coconut Chicken Soup
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes
Add the lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves, and cook for 1-2 minutes
Add the coconut milk, chicken broth, fish sauce, and lime juice, and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through
Season with salt and black pepper to taste
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro

title: Minestrone Soup
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add the onions, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the diced tomatoes, kidney beans, vegetable broth, tomato paste, basil, oregano, salt, and black pepper
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley

title: Lentil and Vegetable Stew
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add the onions and garlic, and cook until the onions are translucent
Add the carrots and potatoes, and cook for 5 minutes
Add the lentils, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, cumin, coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro

title: Chicken and Rice Soup
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes
Add the onions, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the rice, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the rice is tender
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley

title: Spicy Prawn Pasta
steps: Bring a large saucepan of salted water to the boil and cook the spaghetti according to the packet instructions until al dente.
While the spaghetti is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and chilli flakes to the frying pan and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the prawns to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until pink and cooked through.
Drain the spaghetti and add it to the frying pan with the prawns and garlic.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the top and toss everything together to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Roasted Vegetable Pasta
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
Cut the red onion, red bell pepper, and zucchini into bite-sized pieces.
Place the vegetables on a baking tray, drizzle with olive oil, and season with salt and black pepper.
Roast the vegetables in the oven for 20-25 minutes until tender.
While the vegetables are roasting, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
In a large frying pan, heat a little olive oil over medium heat and cook the garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until they start to release their juices.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the cherry tomatoes and garlic.
Add the roasted vegetables to the pasta and toss everything together to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

title: Indian-Style Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the curry powder, cumin seeds, and chilli flakes to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk to the frying pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Mexican Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the chilli flakes and cumin to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned tomatoes to the frying pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Add the black beans to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce and black beans.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Thai-Style Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the curry powder to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk to the frying pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Add the tamarind paste to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until combined.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Japanese-Style Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the soy sauce to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until combined.
Add the sesame oil to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Middle Eastern Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the cumin and paprika to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned tomatoes to the frying pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Crumble the feta cheese into the frying pan and cook for 1-2 minutes until combined.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce and feta cheese.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: African-Style Pasta
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the frying pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the cumin and coriander to the frying pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tinned tomatoes to the frying pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is cooking, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions until al dente.
Drain the pasta and add it to the frying pan with the sauce.
Toss everything together to combine and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Biryani
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds
Add onion, carrot, and peas, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add chicken breast and cook until it is browned and cooked through
Add basmati rice, turmeric, and salt, and stir well
Add 800ml of water to the pan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the water is absorbed

title: Roasted Vegetable Pilaf

ingredients.name: mixed vegetables (such as bell peppers, zucchini, cherry tomatoes)

steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C
Toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil, salt, and black pepper in a bowl
Spread the vegetables on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until tender
Cook the white rice according to package instructions
Combine the cooked rice and roasted vegetables in a bowl
If using feta cheese, crumble it on top of the pilaf

title: Jambalaya with Sausages and Chicken
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add the sausages and cook until browned, about 5 minutes
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5 minutes
Add the onion and bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the tomato paste, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir well
Add the long-grain rice and stir well
Add 800ml of water to the pan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the water is absorbed

title: Mushroom and Spinach Risotto
steps: Heat butter in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add the onion and garlic, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown
Add the arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly
Add the white wine (if using) and cook until the liquid is almost completely absorbed
Add 200ml of vegetable broth to the pan and cook, stirring constantly, until the liquid is mostly absorbed
Repeat the process, adding the broth in 200ml increments, and waiting for it to be absorbed before adding more
When the rice is cooked and creamy, stir in the spinach and parmesan cheese
Season with salt and black pepper to taste

title: Chicken and Vegetable Fried Rice

ingredients.name: mixed vegetables (such as peas, carrots, corn)

steps: Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat on the hob
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned and cooked through
Add the mixed vegetables, onion, and garlic, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula
Stir-fry the rice with the vegetables and chicken, adding soy sauce to taste
Season with salt and black pepper to taste

title: Lamb and Spinach Biryani
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and cook until the onion is translucent
Add the lamb mince and cook until it is browned, breaking it up with a spatula as it cooks
Add the coriander powder, turmeric, salt, and black pepper, and stir well
Add the basmati rice and stir well
Add 800ml of water to the pan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the water is absorbed
Stir in the spinach and cook until wilted

title: Shrimp and Vegetable Jambalaya
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add the onion and bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the shrimp and cook until they are pink and cooked through
Add the tomato paste, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir well
Add the long-grain rice and stir well
Add 800ml of water to the pan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the water is absorbed

title: Vegetable and Bean Chili with Rice

ingredients.name: mixed beans (such as kidney beans, black beans)

steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat on the hob
Add the onion and bell pepper, and cook until the vegetables are tender
Add the mixed beans, tomato paste, cumin, chili powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir well
Add 400ml of water to the pan and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the chili has thickened
Cook the white rice according to package instructions
Serve the chili over the cooked rice

title: Vegetable Pad Thai
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the bean sprouts, carrots, and peanuts, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the rice noodles, tamarind paste, and soy sauce, and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Chicken Ramen
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the ramen noodles, soy sauce, and chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Vegan Soba Noodle Stir-Fry
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and bell peppers and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the soba noodles, soy sauce, and sesame oil, and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Lo Mein
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the lo mein noodles, bean sprouts, carrots, and soy sauce, and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Thai-Style Glass Noodle Soup
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the glass noodles, chicken broth, and fish sauce, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Japanese-Style Udon Noodle Soup
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the udon noodles, soy sauce, and dashi broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Spicy Rice Noodle Stir-Fry
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and bell peppers and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the rice noodles, chilli flakes, and soy sauce, and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.

title: Chicken Fajita Wrap
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and season with fajita seasoning.
Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the chicken, cooking until browned and cooked through.
Add sliced onion and bell pepper to the pan and cook until tender.
Warm the tortilla wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by filling with the chicken and vegetable mixture.

title: Roasted Veggie Flatbread
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Slice the aubergine, zucchini, and red bell pepper into thin rounds.
Place the sliced vegetables on a baking tray and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender.
Spread the flatbread with a layer of hummus or tzatziki sauce.
Top the flatbread with the roasted vegetables and crumbled feta cheese.

title: Lamb Kofta Stuffed Pita
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Mix the lamb mince with minced onion and garlic.
Form the lamb mixture into skewers and grill for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the stuffed pita by filling with the lamb kofta, yogurt, and chopped cucumber and tomato.

title: Indian-Style Chickpea Wrap
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the onion and garlic, cooking until the onion is translucent.
Add the chickpeas and curry powder to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Warm the tortilla wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by filling with the chickpea mixture and topping with chopped cucumber and yogurt.
Serve immediately and enjoy.

title: Grilled Chicken Shawarma
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and your favorite shawarma spices.
Grill the chicken for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the shawarma by filling the pita with the grilled chicken, yogurt, cucumber, and tomato.

title: Veggie and Hummus Wrap
steps: Spread a layer of hummus on each tortilla wrap.
Top the hummus with sliced cucumber, bell pepper, and carrot.
Add a sprinkle of salt and pepper to taste.
Roll up the wrap tightly and slice in half.
Serve immediately and enjoy.

title: Beef and Onion Quesadilla
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the beef mince, cooking until browned.
Add the sliced onion to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Place a tortilla wrap in the pan and sprinkle with cheese and the beef mixture.
Fold the tortilla in half and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tortilla is crispy.
Serve with salsa and enjoy.

title: Falafel Stuffed Pita
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the falafel mix, cooking according to package instructions.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the stuffed pita by filling with the falafel, yogurt, cucumber, and tomato.
Serve immediately and enjoy.
Add a sprinkle of sumac or paprika for extra flavor.

title: Chicken Caesar Wrap
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the chicken breast, cooking until browned and cooked through.
Warm the tortilla wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by filling with the chicken, caesar dressing, parmesan cheese, and romaine lettuce.
Serve immediately and enjoy.
Add a sprinkle of croutons for extra crunch.

title: Cheesy Chicken & Vegetable Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5). Lightly grease an ovenproof dish.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Dice the potatoes and carrots. Chop the onion finely.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and potatoes to the same pan. Sauté for 8-10 minutes until slightly softened.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute to cook off the raw flour taste.
Gradually pour in the milk and chicken stock, stirring continuously to prevent lumps until the sauce thickens.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Add the broccoli florets and season with salt and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into the prepared ovenproof dish. Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese evenly over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until bubbling and golden brown on top.

title: One-Pot Sausage & Bean Casserole

ingredients.name: Tinned cannellini beans, drained and rinsed

steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Slice the sausages into 2-3cm pieces. Chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large, oven-safe casserole dish or pan over medium-high heat. Add the sausage pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove sausages and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Stir in the minced garlic and dried mixed herbs, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and vegetable stock. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the browned sausages to the pan. Add the drained and rinsed cannellini beans. Stir everything together.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Cover the casserole dish with a lid or foil.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the sausages are cooked through.

title: Easy Tuna Pasta Bake

ingredients.name: Tinned tuna in brine or spring water, drained

steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Lightly grease an ovenproof dish.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain well and set aside.
While pasta cooks, chop the onion finely. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to make a roux.
Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, ensuring each addition is fully incorporated before adding more, until you have a smooth, thick sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
Drain the tuna and flake it into the cheese sauce. Stir in the drained sweetcorn and cooked pasta. Mix gently to combine all ingredients.
Pour the mixture into the prepared ovenproof dish. Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese evenly over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden and bubbling.

title: Moroccan-Inspired Sweet Potato & Chickpea Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
Peel and chop the sweet potatoes into 2cm cubes. Chop the onion and mince the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large, oven-safe pan or casserole dish over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and turmeric. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped sweet potatoes, tinned chopped tomatoes, and vegetable stock. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the drained and rinsed chickpeas. Season with salt and pepper. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Cover the casserole dish with a lid or foil.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Remove the lid or foil for the last 10 minutes of baking to allow it to brown slightly. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired before serving.

title: Creamy Chicken Korma Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Cook the basmati rice according to package instructions. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces. Chop the onion finely. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large, oven-safe pan or casserole dish over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the mild curry powder, ground cumin, and ground coriander. Cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken pieces to the pan and cook until lightly browned on all sides.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and coconut milk. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Season with salt and pepper.
Stir the cooked rice into the chicken korma sauce, ensuring everything is well combined.
Cover the casserole dish with a lid or foil and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is bubbling.
