title: Root Veggie Cottage Pie
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/Gas Mark 5).
Rinse the lentils. Dice the carrots, celery, and onion finely. Mince the garlic.
Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery, cooking for 8-10 minutes until softened.
Add the minced garlic and dried mixed herbs, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste, rinsed lentils, and vegetable stock. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat, cover, and let simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until lentils are tender and most liquid is absorbed. Season with salt and pepper.
While the lentils are simmering, peel and chop the potatoes, swede, and parsnips into similar-sized pieces. Boil them in salted water for 20-25 minutes until very tender.
Drain the root vegetables thoroughly, then mash them with milk and butter until smooth. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the lentil mixture into an ovenproof dish. Top evenly with the root vegetable mash, creating peaks with a fork.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.

title: Hearty Beef Mince Hotpot
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan/Gas Mark 4).
Peel and thinly slice the potatoes (about 3mm thick). Dice the carrots and chop the onion finely.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large, oven-safe pan or casserole dish over medium-high heat. Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat.
Add the chopped onion and carrots to the pan with the mince. Cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually pour in the beef stock and Worcestershire sauce, stirring until the sauce thickens slightly. Add the dried thyme and season with salt and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then remove from the heat.
Arrange the sliced potatoes neatly over the top of the mince mixture, overlapping them slightly to create a crust.
Cover the casserole dish with a lid or foil and bake for 45 minutes. Remove the lid/foil and bake for another 20-25 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and golden brown.

title: Creamy Mushroom & Spinach Pasta Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Lightly grease an ovenproof dish.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain well and set aside.
Chop the onion and mince the garlic. Melt 20g of butter in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 5-7 minutes until they have released their liquid and browned slightly. Stir in the garlic and spinach, cooking until the spinach has wilted.
In a separate saucepan, melt the remaining 20g butter over medium heat. Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute to make a roux.

Gradually whisk in the milk and vegetable stock, a little at a time, ensuring each addition is fully incorporated before adding more, until you have a smooth, thick sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Combine the cooked pasta, mushroom and spinach mixture, and the cheese sauce in a large bowl. Mix gently to combine.
Pour the mixture into the prepared ovenproof dish. Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese evenly over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden and bubbling.

title: Classic Broccoli & Cauliflower Cheese Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan/Gas Mark 6). Lightly grease an ovenproof dish.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the broccoli and cauliflower florets and cook for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp. Drain well and arrange in the prepared ovenproof dish.
While the vegetables are cooking, prepare the cheese sauce. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to make a roux.
Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, ensuring each addition is fully incorporated before adding more, until you have a smooth, thick sauce.
Remove the sauce from the heat. Stir in 100g of the grated cheddar cheese, Dijon mustard (or powder), nutmeg, salt, and pepper until the cheese has melted and the sauce is smooth.
Pour the cheese sauce evenly over the broccoli and cauliflower in the dish.
Sprinkle the remaining 50g of grated cheddar cheese over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until bubbling and golden brown on top.

title: Chicken Tikka Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together the yogurt, lemon juice, garam masala, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated chicken, onion, and bell pepper onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Grilled Halloumi and Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium heat.
Thread the halloumi cheese, zucchini, bell pepper, onion, and cherry tomatoes onto skewers.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the cheese is golden brown and the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Beef and Mushroom Kebabs
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, toss the beef strips with a little bit of olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Thread the beef strips, mushrooms, onion, and bell pepper onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the beef is cooked to your liking.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Lamb Koftas with Tzatziki Sauce
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb mince, onion, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the lamb is cooked to your liking.
Meanwhile, combine the yogurt, cucumber, and olive oil in a bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the lamb koftas with the tzatziki sauce and pita bread.

title: Thai-Style Chicken Satay
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together the coconut milk, peanut butter, lime juice, garlic, ginger, salt, and pepper.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and mix well to coat.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Grilled Vegetable Skewers with Chimichurri Sauce
steps: Preheat the grill to medium heat.
Thread the zucchini, bell pepper, onion, and cherry tomatoes onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the vegetables are tender.
Meanwhile, combine the parsley, oregano, garlic, red pepper flakes, red wine vinegar, and olive oil in a blender.
Blend until smooth.
Serve the grilled vegetables with the chimichurri sauce.

title: Indian-Style Vegetable Kebabs
steps: Preheat the grill to medium heat.
Thread the cauliflower, carrots, potatoes, onion, and bell pepper onto skewers.
In a small bowl, mix together the garam masala, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Sprinkle the spice mixture over the vegetables.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Middle Eastern-Style Lamb Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb strips, onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the lamb is cooked to your liking.
Serve hot with your favorite sides.

title: Speedy Tuna Pasta with Tomato Sauce
steps: Bring a kettle of water to a boil, then pour into a saucepan with a pinch of salt. Add the pasta and cook according to pack instructions (typically 8-10 minutes).
While pasta cooks, finely chop the onion and garlic. Heat the olive oil in a separate frying pan over a medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened. Stir in the garlic and dried oregano, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and let it cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Drain the tuna well and flake it into the tomato sauce. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Continue to simmer for 1-2 minutes.
Once the pasta is cooked, drain it thoroughly. Add the drained pasta directly to the tuna tomato sauce and toss everything together until well combined. Serve immediately.

title: Ultimate Speedy Jacket Potato with Beans & Cheese
steps: Thoroughly wash the potato. Prick it all over with a fork about 8-10 times to prevent bursting.
Place the potato on a microwave-safe plate and microwave on high for 5 minutes. Flip the potato over and microwave for another 4-5 minutes, or until soft when squeezed.
While the potato is microwaving, empty the tinned baked beans into a microwave-safe bowl. Heat on high for 2-3 minutes, stirring halfway through, until piping hot.
Grate the cheddar cheese using a box grater.
Once the potato is cooked, carefully remove it from the microwave. Slice it lengthwise down the middle, being careful not to cut all the way through.
Fluff up the inside flesh with a fork, then add the butter, salt, and pepper. Stir it into the potato flesh.
Spoon the hot baked beans generously into the potato, then sprinkle the grated cheese on top. Serve immediately.

title: 15-Minute Chicken & Veg Noodle Stir-fry
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips. Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat.
Add the chicken strips to the hot pan and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add a splash more oil to the pan if needed. Add the grated ginger and minced garlic, stir-frying for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the stir-fry vegetable mix to the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently, until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
While the vegetables cook, prepare the instant noodles according to package instructions (often just soaking in hot water or adding directly to the pan).
Return the cooked chicken to the pan with the vegetables. Add the soy sauce and sesame oil. Stir well to combine.
Add the drained noodles to the pan and toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until heated through and coated in the sauce. Serve immediately.

title: Lightning-Fast Egg Fried Rice (uses leftover rice)
steps: Heat half of the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat. Crack the eggs into the pan and quickly scramble them until just set. Remove the scrambled eggs from the pan and set aside.
Add the remaining vegetable oil to the pan. Add the frozen peas and the white parts of the sliced spring onions (reserve green parts for garnish). Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the cold cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula. Stir-fry vigorously for 3-4 minutes until the rice is heated through and slightly toasted.
Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss the rice mixture to ensure everything is evenly coated.
Return the scrambled eggs to the pan. Stir everything together for another minute until well combined and hot.
Garnish with the green parts of the spring onions before serving immediately.

title: Halloumi & Mediterranean Veg Wraps

ingredients.name: Mediterranean vegetable mix (e.g., peppers, courgette, onion)
Dried herbs (e.g., oregano or mixed Italian)

steps: Slice the halloumi into 1cm thick pieces. Heat half the olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the halloumi slices to the pan and fry for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and slightly crisp. Remove from pan and set aside.
Add the remaining olive oil to the same pan. Add the Mediterranean vegetable mix and a sprinkle of dried herbs. Stir-fry for 5-6 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
While vegetables cook, lightly warm the wholemeal wraps in a dry pan or microwave for 15-20 seconds each.
To assemble, spread a spoonful of hummus onto each warm wrap. Layer with fresh salad leaves, then the cooked Mediterranean vegetables.
Top with the golden halloumi slices. Fold or roll up the wraps tightly and serve immediately.

title: Speedy Tinned Chickpea Curry with Rice
steps: Finely chop the onion. Heat vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the curry paste to the pan and cook for another minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and the coconut milk. Stir well to combine, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the drained and rinsed chickpeas to the curry. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld.
While the curry simmers, prepare the microwaveable rice pouch according to package instructions (typically 1-2 minutes on high).
Stir the fresh spinach into the curry until it wilts, which should only take about 1 minute. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the speedy chickpea curry immediately alongside the hot basmati rice.

title: Quick Sausage, Instant Mash & Gravy
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add the sausages and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown all over and cooked through.
While sausages cook, bring a kettle of water to a boil. Place the instant mash potato flakes into a heatproof bowl. Add milk, butter, and a pinch of salt if desired.
Pour the hot water from the kettle over the mash flakes (refer to pack for exact ratio, usually about 300ml per 100g flakes) and stir well until smooth and fluffy. Cover to keep warm.
In a small saucepan, add the frozen peas with a splash of water. Bring to a boil and simmer for 3-4 minutes until tender. Alternatively, microwave them according to pack instructions.
Prepare the gravy: place gravy granules in a heatproof jug. Pour about 200ml of boiling water from the kettle over the granules, stirring vigorously with a fork until smooth and thick.
Once sausages are cooked, drain any excess fat. Serve the hot sausages alongside the fluffy instant mash, a portion of peas, and a generous pour of hot gravy.

title: Pesto Pasta with Cherry Tomatoes & Rocket
steps: Bring a kettle of water to a boil. Pour into a saucepan, add a generous pinch of salt, and bring to a rolling boil on the hob. Add the pasta and cook according to pack instructions (typically 8-10 minutes).
While the pasta cooks, wash and halve the cherry tomatoes. Wash the rocket leaves.
Once the pasta is cooked al dente, reserve about 50ml of the starchy pasta water, then drain the remaining water thoroughly.
Return the drained pasta to the empty saucepan. Add the green pesto and the halved cherry tomatoes.
Pour in a splash of the reserved pasta water (1-2 tablespoons) and toss everything together vigorously until the pasta is coated in the pesto and the tomatoes are slightly warmed.
Stir in the rocket leaves until just wilted. Serve immediately, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese if desired.

title: Quick Smoked Mackerel Salad with Crusty Bread

ingredients.name: Smoked mackerel fillets (skin on or off)

steps: Carefully flake the smoked mackerel fillets into a bowl, discarding any bones or skin if present.
Wash and slice the cucumber into half-moons. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Prepare the mixed salad leaves.
In a serving bowl, combine the mixed salad leaves, cucumber, and cherry tomatoes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil and the juice from half a lemon to make a simple dressing. Season with salt and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat the vegetables.
Top the dressed salad with the flaked smoked mackerel. Serve alongside a generous slice of crusty bread. For extra zest, squeeze the remaining lemon half over the mackerel.

title: Speedy Bean & Cheese Quesadillas
steps: Finely chop the small onion. Heat vegetable oil in a medium frying pan over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the drained and rinsed mixed beans to the pan along with the chilli powder (or other spices). Cook for 2-3 minutes, mashing some of the beans with the back of a fork to create a chunky mixture.
Season the bean mixture with salt and black pepper to taste. Remove from heat and set aside.
Wipe the frying pan clean. Place one flour tortilla in the dry pan over medium heat. Sprinkle half of the grated cheddar cheese over the entire tortilla.
Spoon half of the bean mixture over one half of the cheesy tortilla. Once the cheese starts to melt slightly, fold the other half of the tortilla over the bean mixture to create a half-moon shape.
Cook for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy, and the cheese is fully melted. Repeat with the remaining tortilla, cheese, and bean mixture.
Once cooked, remove the quesadillas from the pan, slice into wedges, and serve immediately, ideally with a side of salsa.

title: Beef and Mushroom Stew
steps: Peel and chop the carrots and potatoes into bite-sized pieces.
Chop the onions into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the onions until softened.
Add the beef to the pan and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped mushrooms, carrots and potatoes, stock cubes and salt and pepper to the slow cooker.
Add the cooked beef and onion mixture to the slow cooker.
Stir well and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.

title: Butternut Squash and Black Bean Chili
steps: Peel and chop the butternut squash into bite-sized pieces.
Chop the onions into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the onions until softened.
Add the garlic to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chopped butternut squash, black beans, diced tomatoes, chili powder, cumin and salt and pepper to the slow cooker.
Add the cooked onion mixture to the slow cooker.
Stir well and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.

title: Chicken Fajitas
steps: Slice the chicken into thin strips.
Chop the onions and bell peppers into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the onions and bell peppers until softened.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the fajita seasoning to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked chicken and onion mixture to the slow cooker.
Stir well and cook on low for 2-3 hours.
Serve with tortillas, sour cream and salsa.

title: Lamb and Apricot Tagine
steps: Chop the onions into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the onions until softened.
Add the garlic to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the lamb to the pan and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped apricots, stock cubes, cumin, coriander, cinnamon and salt and pepper to the slow cooker.
Add the cooked lamb and onion mixture to the slow cooker.
Stir well and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.

title: Air Fryer Chicken Fajitas
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and season with fajita seasoning.
Heat the hob with a little oil and cook the chicken until browned.
Add the sliced onions and bell peppers to the hob and cook until they start to soften.
Transfer the chicken and vegetables to the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 10 minutes.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp cloth and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the fajitas by placing the cooked chicken and vegetables onto the tortillas and topping with sour cream and cheddar cheese.

title: Roasted Vegetable Quinoa Bowl
steps: Rinse the quinoa and cook according to package instructions.
Toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Cook the vegetables in the air fryer at 200°C for 15-20 minutes, shaking halfway through.
Crumble the feta cheese and squeeze the lemon juice over the quinoa.
Assemble the bowls by placing the cooked quinoa on the bottom, topping with roasted vegetables, and finishing with feta cheese and a squeeze of lemon juice.

title: Air Fryer Chicken Tikka
steps: In a bowl, mix together the tikka masala paste, yogurt, lemon juice, and garam masala.
Add the chicken breast to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Place the marinated chicken in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the chicken with basmati rice and garnish with fresh cilantro.

title: Air Fryer Falafel
steps: Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then add to a blender with onions, garlic, parsley, and spices.
Blend the mixture until it forms a thick paste.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small patties.
Place the falafel in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 10-12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Serve the falafel in a pita with your favorite toppings.

title: Air Fryer Sweet Potato Fries
steps: Peel the sweet potatoes and cut into long, thin strips.
Toss the sweet potato strips with olive oil, salt, pepper, and paprika.
Place the sweet potato fries in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, or until crispy.
Shake the basket halfway through cooking to ensure even cooking.

title: Air Fryer Chicken Satay
steps: In a bowl, mix together the peanut butter, coconut milk, lime juice, and garam masala.
Add the chicken breast to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, if using.
Place the chicken in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the chicken with basmati rice and garnish with fresh cilantro.

title: Air Fryer Vegetable Spring Rolls
steps: Fill a bowl with warm water.
Place a spring roll wrapper in the water for 10-15 seconds, until it becomes soft and pliable.
Fill the center of the wrapper with mixed vegetables and a drizzle of soy sauce.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Place the spring rolls in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 5-7 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.

title: Air Fryer Chicken Quesadilla
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips and season with fajita seasoning.
Heat the hob with a little oil and cook the chicken until browned.
Place a tortilla in the air fryer and top with cooked chicken, cheddar cheese, and a dollop of sour cream.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook the quesadilla in the air fryer at 200°C for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tortilla is crispy.

title: Air Fryer Queso Fries
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut into long, thin strips.
Toss the potato strips with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Place the potato fries in the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, or until crispy.
Melt the cheddar cheese in a bowl and mix with sour cream.
Serve the queso fries by dipping the cooked potato fries into the cheese sauce.

title: Air Fryer Shrimp and Vegetable Stir-Fry
steps: Peel and de-vein the shrimp.
Heat the hob with a little oil and cook the garlic and ginger until fragrant.
Add the mixed vegetables to the hob and cook until they start to soften.
Add the shrimp to the hob and cook until pink and cooked through.
Transfer the shrimp and vegetables to the air fryer and cook at 200°C for 2-3 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.

title: Chicken and Quinoa Bowl
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the chicken breast, cook until browned on both sides and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side.
While the chicken is cooking, heat another pan over medium heat and add the quinoa, cook according to package instructions.
In a separate pan, heat some oil over medium heat and sauté the black beans, red bell pepper, and onion until the vegetables are tender.
To assemble the bowls, slice the cooked chicken and place it on top of the quinoa, then add the black bean and vegetable mixture.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.

title: Lentil and Spinach Curry
steps: In a large pan, heat some oil over medium heat and sauté the onion and garlic until the onion is translucent.
Add the red lentils, spinach, and coconut milk to the pan, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Season the curry with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot over rice or with naan bread.
Garnish with fresh cilantro, if desired.

title: Grilled Chicken and Avocado Salad
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, and season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Grill the chicken for 5-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, slice the avocado and cherry tomatoes.
In a large bowl, combine the mixed greens, sliced avocado, cherry tomatoes, and crumbled feta cheese.
Once the chicken is cooked, slice it and add it to the salad, and serve immediately.

title: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
steps: Heat a large pan or wok over high heat, and add the beef strips, cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the broccoli, soy sauce, and vegetable oil to the pan, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, or until the broccoli is tender.
Add the garlic to the pan and stir-fry for an additional minute.
Season the stir-fry with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot over rice.
Garnish with sliced green onions, if desired.

title: Turkey and Avocado Wrap
steps: Slice the turkey breast and avocado.
In a large bowl, combine the mixed greens, sliced turkey, and avocado.
Add the crumbled feta cheese to the bowl and toss to combine.
Warm the tortilla in the microwave for 10-15 seconds, or until pliable.
Assemble the wrap by spooning the turkey and avocado mixture onto the tortilla, and serve immediately.

title: Salmon and Quinoa Bowl
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C, and season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the oven for 12-15 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the salmon is cooking, heat a large pan over medium heat and cook the quinoa according to package instructions.
In a separate pan, heat some oil over medium heat and sauté the mixed vegetables until tender.
To assemble the bowls, slice the cooked salmon and place it on top of the quinoa, then add the sautéed vegetables and crumbled feta cheese.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Stir-Fry
steps: Heat a large pan or wok over high heat, and add the chicken breast, cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the broccoli, carrots, and soy sauce to the pan, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Add the vegetable oil to the pan and stir-fry for an additional minute.
Season the stir-fry with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot over rice.
Garnish with sliced green onions, if desired.

title: Turkey and Mushroom Meatball Subs
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C, and combine the turkey breast, mushrooms, and onion in a bowl.
Mix the ingredients together with your hands until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Form the mixture into meatballs, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Bake the meatballs in the oven for 12-15 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the meatballs are cooking, toast the sub rolls in the oven for 2-3 minutes, or until lightly browned.

title: Shrimp and Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, and thread the shrimp and mixed vegetables onto skewers.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink and cooked through.
While the skewers are cooking, combine the lime juice and feta cheese in a bowl.
To serve, place the skewers on a plate and spoon the lime juice and feta cheese mixture over the top.

title: Spicy Chicken and Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the mixed vegetables and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the soy sauce and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.

title: Roasted Vegetable and Lentil Bowl
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Spread the vegetables on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, until tender.
Cook the lentils according to package instructions.
Combine the roasted vegetables and cooked lentils in a bowl and top with feta cheese.

title: Spinach and Feta Stuffed Chicken Breast
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Butterfly the chicken breast and fill with spinach and feta cheese.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.

title: Grilled Salmon with Roasted Asparagus
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Grill the salmon for 4-5 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Toss the asparagus with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Roast in the oven at 200°C for 10-12 minutes, until tender.

title: Chicken and Mushroom Fried Rice
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the cooked rice and stir to combine with the chicken and mushrooms.
Add soy sauce and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the rice is heated through.

title: Roasted Vegetable and Quinoa Bowl
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Spread the vegetables on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, until tender.
Cook the quinoa according to package instructions.
Combine the roasted vegetables and cooked quinoa in a bowl and top with feta cheese.

title: Baked Cod with Lemon and Herbs
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Season the cod fillet with salt and pepper.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice.
Sprinkle with fresh herbs.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, until the cod is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.

title: Beef and Vegetable Stew
steps: Brown the beef mince in a large pan over medium heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pan, along with the beef stock cube and water.
Bring the mixture to the boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.

title: Roasted Vegetable Soup
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Toss the carrots, zucchini and onion with a little oil and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
Sauté the garlic in a pan over medium heat until softened.
Add the roasted vegetables, vegetable stock cube and water to the pan.
Bring the mixture to the boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.

title: Chilli Con Carne
steps: Brown the beef mince in a large pan over medium heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and garlic to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the chilli powder to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the canned tomatoes and kidney beans to the pan, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to the boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.

title: Stir-Fried Noodles
steps: Cook the noodles according to the package instructions.
Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the cooked noodles to the pan, along with the soy sauce.
Stir-fry the mixture for 2-3 minutes, until the noodles are well coated with the sauce.

title: Grilled Chicken and Vegetables
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Brush the chicken breast with oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the chicken for 5-6 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Meanwhile, brush the onion, bell pepper and zucchini with oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the vegetables for 3-4 minutes per side, until tender.

title: Baked Potatoes with Tuna and Cheese
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Scrub the potatoes clean and poke some holes in them with a fork.
Bake the potatoes in the oven for 45-50 minutes, until tender.
Meanwhile, mix the canned tuna with cheese and butter.
Split the baked potatoes open and top with the tuna mixture.

title: Spaghetti with Tomato Sauce
steps: Cook the spaghetti according to the package instructions.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and garlic to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the canned tomatoes to the pan, and stir to combine.
Simmer the sauce for 10-15 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.

title: Fried Rice with Vegetables
steps: Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and carrots to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, along with the peas.
Stir-fry the mixture for 2-3 minutes, until the rice is well coated with the vegetables.
Season with soy sauce to taste.

title: Chana Masala
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and garlic to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the ginger to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chickpeas, tomato puree and water to the pan, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to the boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.

title: Chicken Fajita
steps: Heat a large pan over medium-high heat on the hob.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the sliced onion and bell pepper to the pan and cook until they start to soften.
Add the fajita seasoning to the pan and stir to combine.
Continue to cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Warm the tortilla wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the fajitas by slicing the cooked chicken and vegetables and serving them in the tortilla wraps.

title: Vegetable Curry
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat on the hob.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it starts to soften.
Add the sliced carrot and diced potato to the pan and continue to cook until they start to tenderize.
Add the curry powder to the pan and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry cook for an additional 10-15 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt to taste and serve over rice or with naan bread.

title: Chicken and Rice Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
In a large bowl, combine the uncooked rice, frozen mixed vegetables, and chicken stock.
Add the diced chicken breast to the bowl and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue to bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Serve hot and enjoy.

title: Lentil Soup
steps: Heat a large pot over medium heat on the hob.
Add the sliced onion, carrot, and celery and cook until they start to soften.
Add the red lentils, vegetable broth, and diced tomatoes to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 20-25 minutes, until the lentils are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Optional: use an immersion blender to puree the soup for a smoother texture.

title: Chicken and Broccoli Stir-Fry
steps: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat on the hob.
Add the vegetable oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the chicken breast to the pan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the broccoli to the pan and cook until it starts to soften.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and 1 tablespoon of water.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the chicken and broccoli, then stir to combine.
Continue to cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the broccoli is tender.

title: Beef and Vegetable Kabobs
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Add the beef strips, bell pepper, onion, and mushroom to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated ingredients onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kabobs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the beef is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot and enjoy.

title: Thai-Style Green Curry
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat on the hob.
Add the vegetable oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the green curry paste to the pan and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the sliced bell pepper and bamboo shoots to the pan and cook until they start to soften.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for an additional 5-7 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve over rice or noodles.

title: Chicken and Rice Bowl
steps: Heat a large pan over medium-high heat on the hob.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned and cooked through.
Add the uncooked white rice and stir to combine with the chicken and any accumulated juices.
Add water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in soy sauce and serve.

title: Spinach and Feta Omelette
steps: Whisk the eggs in a bowl and season with salt.
Heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat on the hob.
Add the eggs to the pan and cook until the edges start to set.
Add the chopped spinach and crumbled feta cheese to one half of the omelette.
Fold the other half of the omelette over the filling.
Cook for a further 1-2 minutes until the cheese is melted and the eggs are cooked through.

title: Baked Potatoes with Cheese and Beans
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Wash and poke the potatoes with a fork a few times.
Rub the potatoes with a little bit of oil and place on a baking tray.
Bake the potatoes in the oven for 45-60 minutes until tender.
Top the potatoes with cheese and baked beans.
Return to the oven for a further 5-10 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.

title: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
steps: Butter one side of two slices of bread.
Place one slice of bread, butter-side down, in a pan or on a grill.
Place cheese on top of the bread in the pan.
Place the second slice of bread, butter-side up, on top of the cheese.
Cook over medium heat on the grill or in a pan until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Flip the sandwich over and cook for a further 2-3 minutes until the other side is also golden brown.

title: Veggie and Bean Chili
steps: Heat a large pan over medium heat on the hob.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the sliced carrot and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Add the chili powder and stir to combine.
Add the kidney beans, tomato paste, and water, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-25 minutes until the carrots are tender.

title: Full English Breakfast
steps: Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk them together with a fork.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Pour in the eggs and scramble them until they are cooked through.
Grill the sausages and bacon until they are browned and cooked through.
Heat the baked beans in a saucepan over low heat.
Toast the bread and serve everything together.

title: Veggie Omelette
steps: Chop the onions, bell peppers, and mushrooms into small pieces.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the chopped vegetables to the pan and cook until they are softened.
Crack in the eggs and cook until the edges start to set.
Sprinkle the cheese over the top of the eggs and fold the omelette in half.
Cook for another minute and serve hot.

title: Indian-Style Scrambled Eggs
steps: Chop the onions and ginger into small pieces.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the chopped onions and ginger to the pan and cook until they are softened.
Crack in the eggs and cook until they are scrambled.
Add the curry powder, salt, and pepper to the eggs and stir well.
Serve hot with naan bread or rice.

title: American-Style Pancakes
steps: Whisk together the flour, sugar, and eggs in a bowl.
Add the milk to the bowl and whisk until the batter is smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of butter.
Pour in the batter and cook until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip the pancakes and cook for another minute.
Serve hot with maple syrup and fresh fruit.

title: Mediterranean Omelette
steps: Chop the olives, sun-dried tomatoes, and spinach into small pieces.
Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk them together with a fork.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Pour in the eggs and cook until the edges start to set.
Add the chopped vegetables, feta cheese, and spinach to the eggs and fold the omelette in half.
Cook for another minute and serve hot.

title: Chinese-Style Fried Rice
steps: Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the cooked rice to the pan and stir-fry until it is heated through.
Push the rice to one side of the pan and add a little more oil to the other side.
Add the scrambled eggs to the pan and stir-fry until they are cooked through.
Add the soy sauce and peas to the pan and stir-fry for another minute.
Serve hot.

title: Thai-Style Breakfast Bowl
steps: Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the cooked rice to the pan and stir-fry until it is heated through.
Push the rice to one side of the pan and add a little more oil to the other side.
Add the scrambled eggs to the pan and stir-fry until they are cooked through.
Add the soy sauce, peas, and chilli flakes to the pan and stir-fry for another minute.
Serve hot in a bowl.

title: Middle Eastern Shakshuka
steps: Chop the onions and bell peppers into small pieces.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the chopped onions and bell peppers to the pan and cook until they are softened.
Add the tomatoes, cumin, and paprika to the pan and cook for another 5 minutes.
Crack the eggs into the pan and cook until the whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your liking.
Serve hot with pita bread or rice.

title: Mexican Breakfast Burrito
steps: Scramble the eggs in a bowl and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the black beans to the pan and cook until they are heated through.
Add the scrambled eggs to the pan and stir-fry until they are combined with the black beans.
Warm the tortilla in the pan for a few seconds.
Assemble the burrito by filling the tortilla with the egg and black bean mixture, cheese, and salsa.

title: Japanese-Style Breakfast
steps: Cook the steamed rice according to the package instructions.
Prepare the miso soup according to the package instructions.
Grill the salmon in a non-stick pan until it is cooked through.
Slice the pickled ginger into thin pieces.
Assemble the breakfast by serving the steamed rice, miso soup, grilled salmon, and pickled ginger together.
Serve hot.

title: Chicken and Mushroom Risotto
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the chicken.
Cook the chicken for 5-6 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
Add the sliced mushroom to the pan and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Add the arborio rice to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, until the rice is coated in oil and slightly toasted.
Add the chicken stock to the pan, one ladle at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each ladle to be absorbed before adding the next.
Stir in the parmesan cheese and serve the risotto hot.

title: Turkey and Vegetable Meatballs
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Combine the turkey mince, sliced onion, and carrot in a bowl.
Add the breadcrumb and egg to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into small meatballs.
Place the meatballs on a baking tray lined with parchment paper.
Bake the meatballs in the oven for 15-20 minutes, until cooked through.
Serve the meatballs hot.

title: Thai-Style Chicken and Rice Bowl
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat a large pan over medium-high heat and add the chicken.
Cook the chicken for 5-6 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
Add the thai red curry paste to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the coconut milk to the pan and stir to combine.
Bring the curry to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Serve the chicken and curry sauce over jasmine rice, garnished with peanuts.

title: Tomato Pasta
steps: Boil the pasta in a large pan of salted water until it is al dente.
Heat the oil in a separate pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is soft.
Add the canned tomatoes to the pan and stir well.
Drain the pasta and add it to the tomato sauce.
Season with salt to taste.

title: Chickpea Curry
steps: Chop the onion into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is soft.
Add the curry powder and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chickpeas, stock cube and water to the pan.
Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Season with salt to taste.

title: Fried Rice
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is soft.
Add the cooked rice to the pan and stir well.
Add the soy sauce and stir well.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt to taste.

title: Black Bean Tacos
steps: Chop the onion into small pieces.
Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is soft.
Add the black beans, taco seasoning and water to the pan.
Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp cloth and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos with the black bean mixture and any desired toppings.

title: Roasted Chickpeas
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas.
Mix the chickpeas with the olive oil, salt and paprika in a bowl.
Spread the chickpeas out in a single layer on a baking tray.
Roast in the oven for 30-40 minutes, or until crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool.

title: Baked Beans on Toast
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Butter the bread on both sides.
Place the bread on a baking tray and toast in the oven for 5-10 minutes.
Heat the baked beans in a saucepan over low heat.
Serve the baked beans on top of the toasted bread.

title: Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the frozen vegetables to the pan and cook until they are tender.
Add the soy sauce to the pan and stir well.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Season with salt to taste.

title: Chicken and Mushroom Pie
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large pan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat and add the diced chicken breast.
Cook the chicken for 5-6 minutes, or until browned and cooked through.
Add the sliced mushrooms and onion to the pan and cook for a further 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are starting to soften.
Stir in the chicken stock and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Roll out the puff pastry and use it to cover a pie dish.
Fill the pastry with the chicken and mushroom mixture and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.

title: Spinach and Feta Stuffed Chicken Breasts
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a bowl, combine the thawed spinach, crumbled feta cheese, and minced garlic.
Lay the chicken breasts flat and make a horizontal incision in each breast to create a pocket.
Stuff each chicken breast with the spinach and feta mixture.
Drizzle the tops of the chicken breasts with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the chicken for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through.

title: Beef and Vegetable Meatballs
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a bowl, combine the minced beef, grated onion, grated carrot, and breadcrumbs.
Add the egg to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into small meatballs.
Place the meatballs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the meatballs are baking, heat the tomato sauce in a saucepan over medium heat.
Once the meatballs are done, add them to the tomato sauce and simmer for a few minutes to coat.

title: Thai Green Curry
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the thai green curry paste to the pan and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Add the coconut milk to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and bamboo shoots to the pan and cook until they start to soften.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes.
Serve the curry over rice or noodles.

title: Indian Butter Chicken
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the butter to the pan and melt.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the butter chicken spice blend to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the tomato puree and heavy cream to the pan and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes.
Serve the chicken over basmati rice.

title: Japanese Teriyaki Chicken
steps: Slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the sesame oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the teriyaki sauce, ginger, and garlic to the pan and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes.
Serve the chicken over steamed vegetables or rice.

title: Mexican Chili Con Carne
steps: Heat a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the ground beef to the pan and cook, breaking up with a spoon, until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the chili powder to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the canned tomatoes to the pan and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes.
Serve the chili over rice or with tortilla chips.

title: British Beef and Vegetable Stew
steps: Heat a large pot over medium heat.
Add the beef to the pot and cook, browning on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the carrots, potatoes, and onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes.
Add the beef stock to the pot and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 25-30 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs.

title: American Grilled Chicken and Avocado Salad
steps: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken for 5-6 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Slice the avocado, tomato, and cucumber into thin slices.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced avocado, tomato, and cucumber.
Slice the grilled chicken into thin strips and add to the bowl.
Crumble the feta cheese over the top of the salad.
Serve the salad immediately, garnished with fresh herbs.

title: Mediterranean Lamb Koftas
steps: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb mince, onion, garlic, and paprika.
Mix the ingredients together with your hands until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and shape into koftas.
Grill the koftas for 5-6 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Serve the koftas with a side of tomato and cucumber slices.

title: Chinese Stir-Fried Chicken and Vegetables
steps: Heat a large frying pan or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the vegetable oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the broccoli and carrots to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften.
Add the soy sauce to the pan and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Serve the stir-fry over rice or noodles.

title: Grilled Chicken Fajitas
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat
Slice the chicken, bell peppers, and onion into thin strips
In a large bowl, mix together the chicken, bell peppers, and onion
Sprinkle the fajita seasoning over the top and toss to coat
Grill the mixture for 5-7 minutes until the chicken is cooked through
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds
Assemble the fajitas with the grilled mixture, tortillas, and any desired toppings

title: Roasted Eggplant with Lentils and Tomatoes
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C
Rinse the lentils and cook according to package instructions
Toss the eggplant with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Spread the eggplant out in a single layer on a baking sheet
Roast in the oven for 25-30 minutes until the eggplant is tender
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, roasted eggplant, onion, and canned tomatoes

title: Grilled Chicken and Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium heat
Thread the chicken, onion, bell peppers, and zucchini onto skewers
Brush the skewers with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh herbs

title: Grilled Salmon with Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Grill the salmon for 4-6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Toss the asparagus, bell pepper, and zucchini with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Spread the vegetables out in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast the vegetables in the oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes, or until tender.
Serve the grilled salmon with the roasted vegetables.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Skewers
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the chicken, bell pepper, onion, and mushroom onto skewers.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.

title: Beef and Carrot Stew
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides
Add the onions and cook until softened
Add the carrots and potatoes, flour and stock
Bring to the boil, then cover and transfer to the oven
Cook for 1.5 hours, or until the meat is tender

title: Roasted Vegetable Curry
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius
Toss the vegetables in oil and roast for 30 minutes
Heat the curry powder in a pan over medium heat
Add the coconut milk and bring to the boil
Add the roasted vegetables and simmer for 10 minutes
Season with salt and serve

title: Lentil and Root Vegetable Soup
steps: Saute the onions and celery in oil until softened
Add the lentils, carrots, potatoes and stock
Bring to the boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes
Season with salt and serve

title: Chicken and Parsnip Casserole
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides
Add the onions and cook until softened
Add the parsnips and carrots, flour and stock
Bring to the boil, then cover and transfer to the oven
Cook for 1.5 hours, or until the meat is tender

title: Beetroot and Sweet Potato Gnocchi
steps: Boil the beetroot and sweet potatoes until tender
Mash and mix with flour and eggs
Roll out the mixture to form a long rope
Cut into small pieces to form gnocchi
Cook in boiling water until they float to the surface

title: Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
steps: Saute the onions, garlic and ginger in oil until softened
Add the butternut squash and cook until tender
Add the stock and bring to the boil
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes
Blend until smooth and season with salt

title: Braised Lamb Shanks with Root Vegetables
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides
Add the onions and cook until softened
Add the carrots and potatoes, flour and stock
Bring to the boil, then cover and transfer to the oven
Cook for 2.5 hours, or until the meat is tender

title: Roasted Root Vegetable Tart
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius
Toss the vegetables in oil and roast for 30 minutes
Roll out the pastry and place the vegetables in the center
Fold the pastry over the vegetables and brush with oil
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown

title: Chicken and Leek Gratin
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides
Add the leeks and cook until softened
Add the potatoes, flour and stock
Bring to the boil, then cover and transfer to the oven
Cook for 1.5 hours, or until the meat is tender

title: Vegetable Tagine
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add the onions and cook until softened
Add the carrots, potatoes and sweet potatoes
Add the cumin and coriander, and cook for 1 minute
Add water to cover the vegetables and bring to the boil
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes

title: Classic Roast Chicken Dinner with All the Trimmings
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan / Gas Mark 5). Remove the chicken from its packaging, pat dry with kitchen paper, then season generously inside and out with salt and pepper.
Place the chicken in a large roasting tin. Roughly chop half the onion and a couple of garlic cloves, then stuff them into the chicken cavity.

Peel the potatoes and chop them into large, even-sized chunks. Par-boil the potatoes in a large pan of salted boiling water for 8-10 minutes, then drain thoroughly and shake in the colander to rough up the edges.
Drain the potatoes and toss them in the roasting tin around the chicken with half of the vegetable oil. Ensure they are coated evenly.
Roast the chicken and potatoes for 1 hour. Meanwhile, peel and chop the carrots into large pieces, and cut the broccoli into florets.

After 1 hour, add the carrots to the roasting tin with the potatoes and chicken. Continue to roast for another 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is golden brown and the juices run clear when pierced with a skewer.
Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the tin and let it rest on a carving board, covered loosely with foil, for at least 15 minutes. This helps keep it moist.

While the chicken rests, increase oven temperature to 200°C (180°C fan / Gas Mark 6) if potatoes are not crispy enough. Put broccoli in a pot of boiling water and cook for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
To make the gravy, carefully pour any excess fat from the roasting tin into a heatproof bowl, leaving the delicious pan juices. Place the tin on the hob over a medium heat.

Dissolve the chicken stock cube in 400ml boiling water. Pour this into the roasting tin, scraping up any delicious browned bits from the bottom. Stir in the gravy granules and simmer for a few minutes until thickened. Serve the carved chicken with roasted vegetables, broccoli, and gravy.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Nut Roast with Onion Gravy
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan / Gas Mark 4). Lightly grease and line a 900g (2lb) loaf tin with baking parchment, leaving an overhang.
Finely chop one onion, the carrot, and the mushrooms. Heat 10ml of vegetable oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and mushrooms to the pan and cook for 8-10 minutes until softened. Stir in the dried mixed herbs and tomato puree.
Meanwhile, pulse the mixed nuts in a food processor until finely chopped, or chop them very finely by hand. In a large bowl, combine the chopped nuts with the fresh breadcrumbs.
Add the cooked vegetable mixture to the bowl with the nuts and breadcrumbs. Stir in the eggs and 50ml of vegetable stock. Mix thoroughly until well combined.
Season the mixture generously with salt and pepper. Press the mixture firmly into the prepared loaf tin, ensuring there are no air pockets.
Bake in the preheated oven for 45-55 minutes, or until golden brown and firm to the touch. Let it rest in the tin for 10 minutes before carefully turning it out onto a serving plate.
While the nut roast bakes, prepare the onion gravy. Finely slice the remaining onion. Heat 10ml of vegetable oil in a small saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the saucepan and cook slowly for 10-12 minutes until very soft and lightly caramelised. Stir in the plain flour and cook for 1 minute.

Gradually whisk in the remaining 150ml of vegetable stock, bringing the gravy to a simmer. Cook for 5 minutes until thickened, stirring occasionally. Season to taste and serve hot with slices of the nut roast.

title: Warming Slow Cooker Beef Stew with Fluffy Herb Dumplings
steps: Trim any excess fat from the beef braising steak and cut it into 3-4cm chunks. Season the beef generously with salt and pepper.
Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan over a medium-high heat. Brown the beef chunks in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until nicely sealed. This step is optional but adds flavour.
Transfer the browned beef to your slow cooker pot. If not browning, simply add the raw beef chunks directly.
Peel and chop the potatoes, carrots, and onion into large chunks. Slice the celery into thick pieces. Add all the chopped vegetables to the slow cooker with the beef.
In a jug, whisk together the beef stock, plain flour, tomato puree, and dried mixed herbs until smooth. Pour this mixture over the beef and vegetables in the slow cooker.
Stir everything gently to combine, ensuring the beef and vegetables are mostly submerged in the liquid. Cover with the lid.
Cook on a 'Low' setting for 6-8 hours or on a 'High' setting for 3-4 hours, until the beef is meltingly tender and the vegetables are soft.
Approximately 30 minutes before the end of cooking time, prepare the dumplings. In a bowl, combine the self-raising flour, vegetable suet, a pinch of salt, and the chopped fresh parsley.
Add 3-4 tablespoons of cold water, a little at a time, mixing with your hands until a soft, firm dough forms. Avoid overworking the dough.

Divide the dough into 8-10 equally sized balls. Carefully place the dumplings on top of the stew in the slow cooker. Replace the lid and continue to cook for the remaining 30 minutes, or until the dumplings are light and fluffy.

title: Speedy Air Fryer Salmon with Mediterranean Roasted Vegetables
steps: Preheat your air fryer to 190°C. Cut the bell peppers and courgette into bite-sized chunks. Halve the cherry tomatoes. Slice the red onion into thin wedges.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped bell peppers, courgette, red onion, and cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with 15ml of olive oil and sprinkle with dried oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Toss the vegetables thoroughly to ensure they are evenly coated with the oil and seasonings. Arrange the seasoned vegetables in a single layer in your air fryer basket.
Air fry the vegetables for 12-15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, until they are tender and slightly charred. The cooking time may vary slightly depending on your air fryer model.
While the vegetables are cooking, pat the salmon fillets dry with kitchen paper. Drizzle the salmon with the remaining 5ml of olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Once the vegetables are nearly done (around 5 minutes before they finish), create a space in the air fryer basket and place the salmon fillets skin-side up (if skin-on) amongst the vegetables.
Continue to air fry for another 5-8 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking time will depend on the thickness of your fillets and desired doneness.
Carefully remove the salmon and vegetables from the air fryer. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the cooked salmon and vegetables before serving.
Serve immediately as a complete and healthy meal. Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley if available.

title: Comforting Lamb Shepherd's Pie with Cheesy Mash Top
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan / Gas Mark 5). Peel the potatoes and cut them into even-sized chunks. Place them in a large saucepan of cold, salted water, bring to a boil, then simmer for 15-20 minutes until very tender.
While potatoes cook, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Peel and dice the carrots into small cubes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or oven-safe casserole dish over medium-high heat. Add the lamb mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned all over. Drain any excess fat.
Add the chopped onion and carrots to the pan with the lamb mince. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables have softened. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato puree, dried mixed herbs, and Worcestershire sauce (if using). Cook for 2 minutes, then pour in the lamb/beef stock.

Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavours to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir in the frozen peas for the last 5 minutes.

Once the potatoes are cooked, drain them thoroughly. Return them to the hot saucepan and add the milk and butter. Mash until smooth and creamy, then season with salt and pepper. Stir in half of the grated cheese.

Pour the lamb mince filling into an ovenproof dish (if not already cooking in one). Top evenly with the cheesy mashed potato, spreading it out to the edges. You can use a fork to create ridges on top for extra crispiness.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheese over the mashed potato. Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the filling is bubbling.
Remove from the oven and let it stand for 5 minutes before serving. This allows the pie to settle and makes it easier to serve.

title: Easy Herby Chicken and Vegetable Traybake
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan / Gas Mark 6). Prepare a large baking tray (or two if needed to avoid overcrowding) by lining it with baking parchment for easier cleanup.
Wash and chop all the vegetables: cut potatoes into 2cm cubes, carrots into similar-sized chunks, red onion into thick wedges, bell pepper into large pieces, and courgette into thick slices.

Place all the chopped vegetables into a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with 20ml of olive oil, sprinkle with half the dried mixed herbs, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Toss well to coat every piece evenly.
Spread the seasoned vegetables out in a single layer on the prepared baking tray. Ensure there is enough space between them for even roasting.
Pat the chicken thighs dry with kitchen paper. Drizzle the chicken with the remaining 10ml of olive oil and season generously with the remaining dried mixed herbs, salt, and pepper.
Arrange the seasoned chicken thighs amongst the vegetables on the baking tray. Try to get the chicken skin-side up for maximum crispiness.
Bake in the preheated oven for 40-50 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through (juices run clear when pierced), and the vegetables are tender and slightly caramelised.
Halfway through cooking (around 20-25 minutes), give the vegetables a gentle stir to ensure even browning. You can also turn the chicken pieces if desired, but skin-side up is best for crispy skin.
Once cooked, remove the traybake from the oven. Let it rest for a couple of minutes before serving directly from the tray for a casual, comforting meal.
Serve hot, perhaps with a side of crusty bread or a simple green salad if you desire a bit more freshness.

title: Impressive Vegan Lentil and Mushroom Wellington
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan / Gas Mark 5). Line a baking tray with baking parchment.
Finely chop the onion and garlic. Roughly chop the mushrooms. Heat 10ml of olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.

Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and chopped mushrooms, cooking for another 5-7 minutes until the mushrooms have released their water and started to brown.
Stir in the spinach and cook until wilted, which will only take 1-2 minutes. Remove the pan from the heat.
In a large bowl, combine the drained green lentils, the cooked mushroom and spinach mixture, tomato puree, vegetable stock, breadcrumbs, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Mix everything thoroughly with a spoon or your hands until well combined and the mixture holds together when pressed. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.

Unroll the sheet of vegan puff pastry onto the prepared baking tray. Spoon the lentil and mushroom mixture down the centre of the pastry, forming a compact log shape, leaving a border of pastry on both long sides.
Carefully bring one long side of the pastry over the filling, then bring the other long side over, overlapping slightly. Press the seams firmly to seal, and also seal the short ends.

Turn the wellington over so the seam is underneath. You can crimp the edges with a fork for a decorative finish. Brush the top of the pastry with a little remaining olive oil or plant-based milk for a golden glaze.

Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is puffed up, golden brown, and cooked through. Allow to rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving. Great with roasted vegetables or a side salad.

title: Aromatic Weekend Chicken Biryani (Simplified)
steps: Wash the basmati rice thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Soak the rice in fresh water for at least 20 minutes while you prepare the chicken, then drain well.

Cut the boneless chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl, combine the chicken with 50g of plain yoghurt, half of the biryani spice mix, and a pinch of salt. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 15 minutes (or longer if time allows).

Finely slice the onions. Heat 20ml of vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pan or oven-safe pot over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and fry for 8-10 minutes until golden brown and caramelised.

Add the ginger-garlic paste to the pan and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in the chopped tomatoes and the remaining biryani spice mix. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes break down and the oil separates.
Add the marinated chicken to the pan and stir to coat it evenly with the spice mixture. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is lightly browned on the outside.
Pour in 200ml of water and bring to a simmer. Cover the pan and let the chicken cook for 10-15 minutes on low heat, ensuring it's mostly cooked through but not completely tender yet.

While the chicken is simmering, bring a separate large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the drained basmati rice and cook for only 5-7 minutes until it is about 70% cooked (it should still be firm but bendable). Drain the rice immediately.
Preheat your oven to 180°C (160°C fan / Gas Mark 4) if you plan to finish in the oven. If not, reduce hob heat to lowest setting.
Layer the partially cooked rice evenly over the chicken curry in the pot. Sprinkle with finely chopped fresh coriander and the remaining 50g of plain yoghurt (optional, for extra moisture).

Cover the pot tightly with a lid (or foil if oven finishing) and place in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or continue cooking on the lowest hob heat for 25-30 minutes. This allows the rice to steam and absorb flavours.
Once cooked, let the biryani rest for 5 minutes. Gently fluff the rice with a fork, mixing the chicken and rice layers. Serve hot, garnished with extra fresh coriander.

title: Hearty Roast Root Vegetable and Halloumi Bake
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan / Gas Mark 6). Prepare a large baking tray by lining it with baking parchment for easier cleaning.
Peel the sweet potatoes, carrots, and parsnips, then cut them into roughly 2cm chunks. Halve the red onion and cut it into thick wedges. Cut the bell pepper into large pieces.
Place all the prepared root vegetables and bell pepper into a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with 30ml of olive oil, sprinkle with dried oregano, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Toss the vegetables thoroughly with your hands to ensure they are all evenly coated with the oil and seasonings. Spread them out in a single layer on the prepared baking tray.
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, tossing halfway through, until they are starting to soften and become tender-crisp.
While the vegetables are roasting, slice the halloumi cheese into 1cm thick pieces. You can then cut these slices into smaller pieces or leave them as is.
After the initial vegetable roasting time, remove the tray from the oven. Arrange the halloumi slices evenly amongst the partially cooked vegetables on the tray.
Drizzle the halloumi with the remaining 10ml of olive oil. Return the tray to the oven and continue to bake for another 10-15 minutes.
The halloumi should be golden brown and slightly crispy, and the vegetables fully tender and caramelised. Cooking time may vary depending on your oven.
Remove the bake from the oven. Squeeze fresh lemon juice generously over the entire dish before serving hot. This dish is great on its own or with a simple green salad.

title: Economical Beef Roast with Crispy Yorkshire Puddings
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (180°C fan / Gas Mark 6). Season the beef joint generously all over with salt and black pepper. You can rub it with a little oil first if desired.
Place the beef joint in a roasting tin. Roast for 20 minutes per 450g for medium-rare, 25 minutes for medium, and 30 minutes for well-done. For 800g, this is roughly 35-45 minutes for medium.

While the beef cooks, prepare the Yorkshire pudding batter. In a blender or large bowl, whisk together the plain flour, eggs, milk, and a pinch of salt until smooth. Let the batter rest for at least 15 minutes (longer is better, up to an hour).

Peel the potatoes and cut them into large, even-sized chunks. Par-boil the potatoes in a large pan of salted boiling water for 8-10 minutes, then drain thoroughly and shake in the colander to rough up the edges.
About 30 minutes before the beef is due to finish, remove the roasting tin from the oven. Add the par-boiled potatoes around the beef, ensuring they are coated in the beef fat. Return to the oven.

When the beef is cooked to your liking, remove it from the oven and transfer it to a carving board. Cover loosely with foil and let it rest for at least 15-20 minutes. Keep the oven on for the Yorkshires and potatoes.
Increase the oven temperature to 220°C (200°C fan / Gas Mark 7). Place 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable oil into each hole of a 4-hole Yorkshire pudding tin or a 12-hole muffin tin.
Place the tin with oil into the very hot oven for 5-10 minutes until the oil is smoking hot. Carefully pour the Yorkshire pudding batter into each hole, filling about a third.
Immediately return the tin to the oven and cook for 20-25 minutes without opening the door, until the Yorkshires are golden brown, puffed up, and crispy. Ensure potatoes are also golden and crispy.

While Yorkshires cook and beef rests, make gravy. Pour off most of the fat from the roasting tin, leaving the pan juices. Place the tin on the hob over medium heat, add beef stock and gravy granules. Whisk until smooth and thickened. Slice beef, serve with roasted potatoes, Yorkshire puddings, and gravy.

title: Hearty Vegetarian Shepherd's Pie with Root Vegetable Topping

ingredients.name: Mixed Root Vegetables (carrots, parsnips, sweet potato)

steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (170°C fan / Gas Mark 5). Peel and chop the mixed root vegetables (carrots, parsnips, sweet potato) into even-sized chunks.
Place the chopped root vegetables in a large saucepan, cover with cold water, add a pinch of salt, bring to a boil, then simmer for 15-20 minutes until very tender. Drain thoroughly.
While the vegetables cook, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Heat 10ml of olive oil in a large oven-safe casserole dish or a deep frying pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, drained lentils, tomato puree, dried mixed herbs, and vegetable stock. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken.
Stir in the frozen peas for the last 5 minutes of cooking the lentil base. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Once the root vegetables are cooked and drained, return them to the hot saucepan. Add the plant-based milk and vegan margarine. Mash until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper.

Pour the lentil mixture into your ovenproof dish (if not already cooking in one). Top evenly with the mashed root vegetable topping, spreading it out to the edges. You can use a fork to create ridges on top for a nice texture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling hot.
Remove from the oven and let it stand for 5 minutes before serving to allow the pie to set. Serve hot, perhaps with a side of steamed green beans or a fresh salad.

title: Classic British Shepherd's Pie
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (Fan 160°C). Peel and chop the potatoes into even pieces for boiling.
Place the chopped potatoes in a large saucepan, cover with cold water, add a pinch of salt, and bring to a boil. Cook for 15-20 minutes until very tender.
While potatoes cook, finely chop the onion and carrots. Heat vegetable oil in a large, oven-safe pan or casserole dish over medium-high heat.
Add the beef mince to the hot pan and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned all over. Drain off any excess fat.
Stir in the chopped onion and carrots, cooking for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add the plain flour and tomato puree, stirring for 1 minute.
Dissolve the beef stock cube in 400ml of boiling water to make stock. Pour this into the mince mixture, bring to a simmer, and cook for 10-15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the frozen peas and season generously with salt and black pepper. Remove the mince mixture from the heat.
Once potatoes are tender, drain them thoroughly. Return them to the pan, add milk and butter, then mash until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the mashed potato evenly over the mince mixture in the pan. You can rough up the top with a fork for a crispy texture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the filling is bubbling around the edges.

title: Creamy Chicken Tikka Masala with Rice
steps: Cut the chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces. In a bowl, mix the chicken with 50g of plain yogurt, 5g garam masala, turmeric, cumin, chilli powder, and a pinch of salt. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Finely chop the onion. Grate or finely chop the ginger and garlic.
