Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the marinated chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and mostly cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the same pan and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and lightly golden. Add the ginger and garlic, cooking for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the remaining 5g garam masala, another pinch of turmeric, cumin, and chilli powder. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.

Add the tinned chopped tomatoes and tomato puree to the pan. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil separates slightly.

Return the cooked chicken to the sauce. Stir in the remaining 50g plain yogurt and the double cream. Mix well and heat through gently for 5 minutes, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil vigorously after adding dairy.
While the curry simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions, typically by rinsing, then boiling in double the amount of water until absorbed.
Season the curry with salt and a pinch of sugar to balance the flavours.
Serve the creamy chicken tikka masala hot with the fluffy basmati rice.

title: Hearty Beef Bolognese with Spaghetti
steps: Finely chop the onion, carrot, and celery stick. Mince the garlic cloves.
Heat vegetable oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat. Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until thoroughly browned. Drain off any excess fat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery to the pan with the mince. Cook for 7-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables have softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and dried mixed herbs, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato puree, tinned chopped tomatoes, and the beef stock (dissolved in 150ml of boiling water) to the pan.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for at least 30-40 minutes (the longer the better), stirring occasionally, allowing the flavours to meld.
About 15 minutes before the bolognese is ready, bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
Drain the cooked spaghetti.
Season the bolognese sauce generously with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the hearty bolognese sauce spooned over the hot spaghetti.

title: Creamy Baked Macaroni Cheese
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (Fan 170°C). Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the macaroni pasta and cook according to package instructions until al dente. Drain well and set aside.
While the pasta cooks, melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Once melted, stir in the plain flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to create a roux.
Gradually whisk in the milk, a little at a time, ensuring each addition is fully incorporated before adding more, to create a smooth béchamel sauce. Continue whisking until the sauce thickens.

Remove the saucepan from the heat. Stir in three-quarters of the grated cheddar cheese, the English mustard, and season generously with salt and black pepper. Stir until the cheese has melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the drained macaroni pasta to the cheese sauce and stir to coat all the pasta evenly.
Pour the macaroni cheese mixture into an oven-proof baking dish.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheddar cheese over the top of the macaroni.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and bubbling around the edges.
Let it stand for a few minutes before serving hot.

title: Hearty Chilli Con Carne with Rice
steps: Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Deseed and chop the red bell pepper into small pieces.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or deep frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until thoroughly browned. Drain off any excess fat.
Add the chopped onion and red bell pepper to the pan with the mince. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables have softened.
Stir in the minced garlic, chilli powder, cumin powder, and smoked paprika. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and the beef stock (dissolved in 150ml of boiling water). Bring to a simmer.

Add the drained tinned kidney beans to the pot. Stir everything together, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the chilli simmer for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavours to deepen.
While the chilli simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions, typically by rinsing, then boiling in double the amount of water until absorbed.
Season the chilli generously with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the hot chilli con carne with a side of fluffy basmati rice.

title: Hearty Red Lentil Dahl with Spinach
steps: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well.
Finely chop the onion. Grate or finely chop the ginger and garlic.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot or saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the grated ginger and garlic, cooking for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the turmeric powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder to the pot. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.

Add the rinsed red lentils, tinned chopped tomatoes, and the vegetable stock (dissolved in 800ml of boiling water) to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and have broken down, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
While the dahl simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions, typically by rinsing, then boiling in double the amount of water until absorbed.
Once the lentils are cooked, stir in the fresh spinach. Cook for a few minutes until the spinach has wilted.
Season the dahl generously with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the hot red lentil dahl immediately with the fluffy basmati rice.

title: Coconut Chicken & Sweet Potato Curry
steps: Cut the chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces. Peel and chop the sweet potatoes into 2cm cubes. Chop the onion and red bell pepper. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or pot over medium heat. Add the chicken pieces and cook for 5-7 minutes until browned and mostly cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the chopped onion, red bell pepper, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the same pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Stir in the red curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add the chopped sweet potatoes to the pan, along with the tinned coconut milk and the vegetable stock (dissolved in 200ml of boiling water). Bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the cooked chicken to the pan. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the curry simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender, stirring occasionally.
While the curry simmers, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions, typically by rinsing, then boiling in double the amount of water until absorbed.
Season the curry with salt and a squeeze of lime juice for brightness, adjusting to your taste.
Serve the hot coconut chicken and sweet potato curry immediately with the fluffy basmati rice.

title: Crispy Air Fryer Chicken Katsu Curry
steps: Prepare the chicken: Halve each chicken breast horizontally to create thinner cutlets. Lightly season both sides with salt and pepper.
Set up a breading station: Place plain flour in one shallow dish, beaten egg in a second, and panko breadcrumbs in a third.
Dredge each chicken cutlet first in flour (shaking off excess), then dip in egg (letting excess drip off), and finally coat thoroughly in panko breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray or brush the breaded chicken cutlets with vegetable oil. Place in your air fryer basket in a single layer (cook in batches if necessary).
Air fry at 180°C for 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown and cooked through. Alternatively, shallow fry in oil on the hob.
While chicken cooks, prepare the curry sauce: Chop the onion and carrot into small dice.
Heat a little vegetable oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in the curry powder and tomato puree, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Gradually whisk in 400ml of vegetable stock (dissolved from cube) until smooth. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the honey (or sugar) and soy sauce. Season the curry sauce to taste with salt and pepper.
While the sauce is simmering, cook the basmati rice according to package instructions.
Serve the crispy chicken katsu cutlets sliced, alongside fluffy rice, and generously spooned with the warm curry sauce.

title: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Beef & Rice
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (Fan 160°C). Halve the bell peppers lengthwise and remove the seeds and white membrane.

In a large bowl, combine the beef mince, uncooked white rice, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, dried mint, dried oregano, and half of the tinned chopped tomatoes. Season well with salt and black pepper.
Mix the filling thoroughly with your hands until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
Generously stuff each bell pepper half with the beef and rice mixture, pressing it down firmly.
Arrange the stuffed bell peppers in an oven-proof baking dish. Pour the remaining half of the tinned chopped tomatoes around the peppers.
Dissolve the beef stock cube in 200ml of boiling water and pour this stock into the baking dish, around the peppers.
Cover the baking dish tightly with foil and bake for 45 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 20-30 minutes, or until the peppers are tender, the filling is cooked through, and the top is lightly browned.
Serve hot, with some of the tomato sauce from the baking dish spooned over.

title: Quick Creamy Tomato & Basil Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the pasta and cook according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve about 100ml of pasta cooking water before draining.
While the pasta cooks, finely chop the onion and mince the garlic. Roughly chop the fresh basil leaves.
Heat vegetable oil in a large saucepan or deep frying pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Pour in the tinned chopped tomatoes and the vegetable stock (dissolved in 100ml of boiling water). Add a pinch of sugar to balance the acidity.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly and flavours to meld.
Stir in the single cream and most of the chopped fresh basil into the tomato sauce. Heat through gently for 2-3 minutes; do not boil vigorously.
Add the drained pasta to the creamy tomato sauce. Toss well to coat the pasta. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta cooking water until desired consistency is reached.
Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with the remaining fresh basil and an optional sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese.
